questions.question: The tiny pores present in the leaves of the plants for exchange of gases are called
questions.explanation: Stomata

questions.question: The green colour of plants is due to the presence of or why are leaves green in colour?
questions.explanation: The green colour of plants is due to the presence of chlorophyll inside the chloroplast. Chlorophyll is essential for photosynthesis. Chlorophyll is a pigment found in the thylakoid membranes of the chloroplasts in the leaves.

questions.question: What is Photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a process used by plants and other organisms to convert light energy into chemical energy. Photosynthesis is the process by which plants make their own food.

questions.question: Which of the following component of food provide us energy:
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates provide us energy. It is the main source of energy. It is made up of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen. There are three types of carbohydrates i.e. Sugar, Starch & cellulose. Protein is essential for bodybuilding. Vitamins and minerals protect us from the disease.

questions.question: In non-green plants and animals mode of nutrition is:
questions.explanation: The process by which living organism obtains their food is called the mode of nutrition. In non-green plants and animals mode of nutrition is heterotrophs as they obtain food from plants or other animals.

questions.question: The process by which green plants produce their own food is called ________.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants produce their own food using carbon dioxide, water in the presence of sunlight.

questions.question: The small pores present on the leaves are called ________.
questions.explanation: The small pores present on the leaves of plant are called stomata. Stomata help in the exchange of gases and transpiration.

questions.question: Ultimate sources of energy for all living organism is ________.
questions.explanation: Sun is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms. The solar energy captured during photosynthesis is used by all organisms as a source of energy.

questions.question: Which gas is released during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis carbon dioxide gas is taken and oxygen gas is released along with glucose and water.

questions.question: Rhizobium is an example of
questions.explanation: Symbiosis

questions.question: Which of them is not an insectivorous plant?
questions.explanation: Some plants like Venus flytrap, Bladderwort, and Drosera trap insects as their food and are called insectivorous plants. Banana is not an insectivorous plant.

questions.question: Name the bacteria that convert atmospheric nitrogen into soluble form?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium is present in the root nodules of leguminous plants. This bacteria converts atmospheric nitrogen into water-soluble nitrates and nitrites.

questions.question: Amar bel is an example of ________.
questions.explanation: Amar bel is a plant parasite that is devoid of leaves, having yellow, slender stems. It is a total parasite.

questions.question: By which one of the following types of nutrition, plants obtain their food?
questions.explanation: Autotrophic nutrition

questions.question: Rate of photosynthesis is faster in:
questions.explanation: The light between wavelengths of 400 nm and 700 nm is most effective, for photosynthesis. The wavelength of red light is higher than other lights i.e. 700 nm.

questions.question: The molecular formula of carbohydrate is:
questions.explanation: The general formula for carbohydrates is Cn(H2O)n. So if n = 11 then, according to the formula it becomes C11H22O11. So, C11H12O11 is carbohydrate.

questions.question: Which of the following chemical is used for absorbing CO2?
questions.explanation:
Carbon dioxide is acidic in nature and potassium hydroxide is a strong base. Hence, carbon dioxide reacts with potassium hydroxide to form potassium carbonate and water.
2KOH + CO2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} K2CO3 + H2O

questions.question: In desert plants, stomata open during:
questions.explanation: In desert plants, stomata open during the night because if it opens during the day it will cause transpiration(loss of water).

questions.question: During the test for the starch part of leaf uncovered with black paper become:
questions.explanation: Because uncovered part of leaf able to remove chlorophyll, and then present starch exposed to pass the test which results in blue-black.

questions.question: Animals are not able to prepare food using carbon dioxide, water, and sunlight because:
questions.explanation: Because chlorophyll is able to trap sunlight as well as it causes the green colour of the leaf. Chlorophyll is absent in animals.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is known as ‘food factory’?
questions.explanation: Because food is prepared by the leaf with the help of chlorophyll, sunlight, carbon dioxide and water. Chlorophyll and stomata present in leaf. Chlorophyll trap sunlight and stomata causes exchange of gases.

questions.question: Leaves of some plants are red, violet, or brown; whether they carry on photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Yes, all these kinds of plants have chlorophyll. They are in different colours because they have different colour pigments.

questions.question: Nitrogen is necessary for the formation of protein. From where plants obtain nitrogen?

options.option: From air in form of gaseous nitrogen
From the soil in the form of nitrite and nitrate

questions.explanation: Roots of the leguminous plants absorb atmospheric nitrogen from the soil and use it to make protein:

questions.question: The molecular formula of glucose is:
questions.explanation: Glucose is a simple sugar. The molecular formula for glucose is C6H12O6 . which means that it is a molecule that is made of six carbon atoms, twelve hydrogen atoms, and six oxygen atoms. Glucose circulates in the blood of animals as blood sugar.

questions.question: Which one is not an insectivore’s plant?
questions.explanation: Because the banana plant is growing in nutrient-rich soil and does not have any modified structure to trap insects like Venus flytrap, Bladderwort and Drosera have.

questions.question: Which one is not a mode of nutrition in non-green plants?
questions.explanation: Non-green plants obtain their food from plants and animals whether they are dead or alive which may be the heterotrophic, saprophytic, and parasitic mode of nutrition.

questions.question: Which kinds of plants fix the maximum amount of solar energy into chemical energy?
questions.explanation: Aquatic plants fix the maximum amount of solar energy into chemical energy by the process of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Leaves of some plants are not green, either red or brown due to:
questions.explanation: Because of chromoplast pigments along with chloroplast that give another colour like red or brown.

questions.question: Chlorophyll can convert:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll trap sunlight and during photosynthesis, chlorophyll present in chloroplast pigment converts light energy into chemical energy.

questions.question: Manure and fertiliser are added to the field to increase fertility as they contain:
questions.explanation: Farmers add manure and fertilizer into their field to increase the fertility of soil as nitrogen and other nutrients are soluble in water and absorbed by the plant for their growth.

questions.question: Which of the following nutrients can not be synthesised by plants?
questions.explanation: When hydrogen burns in the presence of oxygen it produces water and it cannot be done by plants.

questions.question: Which one is a symbiotic association between two organisms?
questions.explanation: Lichen is a symbiotic association among two organisms like fungi and algae for mutual benefits. Fungi absorb nutrients and algae prepare food with the help of these nutrients.

questions.question: Pitcher plant: insectivores:: mushroom:?
questions.explanation: The pitcher plant is an insectivorous plant as it catches insects to obtain nutrients. Mushroom is saprophytes as it obtains food from dead and decaying matters.

questions.question: Plants obtain carbon dioxide from the atmosphere but not the nitrogen because:

options.option: Nitrogen is inert and insoluble in water

questions.explanation: Plant obtains carbon dioxide from the atmosphere for photosynthesis. Nitrogen is an inert gas that cannot be utilized by the plant directly and insoluble in water.

questions.question: Carbohydrates produced in the process of photosynthesis is converted into:
questions.explanation: Plants form starches, by stringing together sugars. When you eat starchy foods, the starches are broken down into sugars, including glucose, maltotriose, and maltose, by an enzyme called amylase found in your saliva and small intestine.

questions.question: More stomata are found on the lower surface of leaves because:
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores in the leaf that allow gas exchange where water vapor leaves the plant and carbon dioxide enters. When stomata are open, transpiration rates increase and when they are closed, transpiration rates decrease. So, more stomata decrease the rate of evaporation.

questions.question: Pitcher plants have green leaves that perform photosynthesis, then why pitcher plant intake insect?
questions.explanation: Because the soil where pitcher plant is growing have very less amount of nitrogen. Every organism has nitrogen so it takes insects to absorb nitrogen.

questions.question: Which bacteria convert atmospheric nitrogen into soluble form?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium bacteria are symbiotic bacteria that shares it's relation with leguminous plants. It converts atmospheric nitrogen into a soluble form.

questions.question: Leguminous plant: Gram:: ? : Saprophytes.
questions.explanation: Gram is a leguminous plant having root nodules. Fungi are saprophytic organisms as obtain food from dead and decaying materials.

questions.question: Rhizobium, present in the root nodules of leguminous plant converts atmospheric nitrogen into:
questions.explanation: Rhizobium is the nitrogen-fixing bacteria present mainly in the soil. The Rhizobia chemically convert the nitrogen from the air to make it available for the plant. These bacteria live in a symbiotic relationship with leguminous plants. The Rhizobia live in nodules in the roots of the plant. These bacteria convert atmospheric nitrogen to nitrates and nitrites which can be used by the plants directly.

questions.question: Which one is not an activity of fungi?
questions.explanation: Because fungi is non green plant and get their food from inorganic substances.

questions.question: Plant do not store carbohydrates as glucose but do as starch because glucose
questions.explanation: Because glucose can easily react with oxygen to produce bi-products. But starch do not show the same.

questions.question: Rate of photosynthesis is:
questions.explanation: The rate of photosynthesis is faster than that of respiration during daytime in the presence of sunlight.

questions.question: Chlorophyll helps the plant in trapping the sunlight from atmosphere. Which one of the following plants do not require chlorophyll to obtain food?
questions.explanation: Non green plants

questions.question: If your food is lack of roughages, which one of the following problems might you face?
questions.explanation: Constipation

questions.question: Which one of the following is broken down into glucose by Intestinal juice?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates

questions.question: Ultimate source of energy required by living organism is
questions.explanation: Solar energy is necessary for plants to make their food by photosynthesis. All living beings directly or indirectly depends on plants for their food requirements.

questions.question: Which one is saprophytic organism?
questions.explanation: Those organisms that obtain their food from dead and decaying matter are called as saprophytic organism. Mushroom is a saprophytic organism.

questions.question: Green plants are called ________.
questions.explanation: Auto means self and trophies mean nourishment. Plants are called autotrophs because they make their food themselves. The making of food for themselves is called Autotrophic nutrition. Autotrophic nutrition is found in green plants, and in some bacteria.

questions.question: The plant which traps and feeds on insects is:
questions.explanation: Nepenthes, also called as pitcher plant has its leaves modified into tubular pitcher like structure closed with leaves. These have a network of fibres which entangle the trapped organism/insects  and later digest it by secreting hydrolysing enzymes to digest them.

questions.question: Which part of the plant takes in carbon dioxide from the air for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Stomata are small pores present in the lower epidermis of the leaf to take-in carbon dioxide from the air. The opening and closing of stomata pores is controlled by Guard cells.

questions.question: Plants take carbon dioxide from the atmosphere mainly through their:
questions.explanation: Plants take carbon dioxide from the atmosphere mainly through their leaves. Green leaves make food from Carbon dioxide and water in the presence of sunlight and chlorophyll.

questions.question: Which part of the plant is called its food factory?
questions.explanation: Leaves are known as the kitchen or food factories of the plants because photosynthesis takes place in leaves. Leaves look green because of the presence of chlorophyll.

questions.question: What is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms?
questions.explanation: The solar energy is very important to carry out the process of photosynthesis, it is captured by the leaves and stored in the plant in the form of food. And this in turn used by other organisms to get food to obtain energy Thus, we say that the sun is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a parasite?
questions.explanation: A plant or organism that lives on or inside other organism and derives its food from that organism. Cuscuta is a parasite.

questions.question: Which of the following class of organisms belongs to saprotrophs?
questions.explanation: Those organisms which feed on dead and decomposed plant and animals as their food are known as Saprotrophs. E.g. Fungi, mushroom, yeast, etc.

questions.question: The organism that can prepare their own food is called ________.
questions.explanation: Organisms that make food themselves from simple substances are called Autotrophs. E.g. Mango tree, rose plant, etc. Autotrophic plants can synthesize their own food by the process of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which of the following is not required by plant for food synthesis?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the food manufacturing process of green plants containing chlorophyll, in the presence of sunlight, with the help of carbon dioxide and water to synthesise carbohydrates and oxygen. So, oxygen is not required by the plant for food synthesis.

questions.question: Which one is an insectivorous plant?
questions.explanation: Some plants eat insects. Such plants are called insectivorous plants. They trap and digest the insects. Pitcher plant is the example of an insectivorous plant.

questions.question: In the process of photosynthesis, the gas given out by green leaves is ________.
questions.explanation:
Photosynthesis is the food manufacturing process of green plants containing chlorophyll, in the presence of sunlight, with the help of carbon dioxide and water to synthesise carbohydrates and oxygen. The equation for the process is as follow:
Carbon dioxide + water {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} carbohydrate + Oxygen

questions.question: To test the presence of starch by iodine, the green leaf is first boiled in alcohol to:
questions.explanation:
To test the presence of starch by iodine, the green leaf is first boiled in alcohol to Dissolve chlorophyll.
The hot water kills the leaf and the alcohol breaks down the chlorophyll, taking the green color out of the leaf. When you put iodine on the leaves, one of them will turn blue-black and the other will be a reddish-brown. Iodine is an indicator that turns blue-black in the presence of starch. The leaf that was in the light turns blue-black, which demonstrates that the leaf has been performing photosynthesis and producing starch.

questions.question: Which of the following is an insectivorous plant?
questions.explanation: Some plants eat insects. Such plants are called insectivorous plants. They trap and digest the insects. Pitcher plant is the example of an insectivorous plant. Nepenthes, also called as pitcher plant.

questions.question: Which one is a saprophytic organism?
questions.explanation: Those organisms which feed on dead and decomposed plant and animals as their food are known as saprophytic organism. E.g. Fungi, mushroom, yeast, etc.

questions.question: Insectivorous plants are found mostly in areas which are:
questions.explanation: Insectivorous plants are found mostly in areas which are nitrogen deficient. The soil of marshy land is deficient in nitrogen. Plants living in marshy areas do not get nitrogen from the soil. Their nitrogen need is fulfilled by sucking the juice of insects.

subtopics.name: Other Modes of Nutrition in Plants

questions.question: Exchange of gases in leaves takes place through:
questions.explanation: The small pores present on the lower surface of leaf, are called stomata. Stomata absorb carbon dioxide from air for photosynthesis. Stomata facilitates exchange of gases and transpiration.

questions.question: The green colour in plants is due to a pigment called ________.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment present in green leaves. Leaves look green because of the presence of chlorophyll, which is a green pigment.

questions.question: Which of the following is an omnivorous organism?
questions.explanation: Those animals which can eat both plants, as well as animals, are called Omnivores organism.  The dog is Omnivores.

questions.question: Which of the following is a nutrient?
questions.explanation: Nutrition is the mode of intake of food by the organism and its utilization by the body. All organisms need food to perform their life processes. Food comprises different nutrients which include carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals.

questions.question: Organisms which prepare food for themselves using simple naturally available raw materials are referred to as
questions.explanation:
Autotrophs (auto = self; trophos = nourishment) are organisms who prepare food themselves, from simple substances.
For example, Plants.

questions.question: In the absence of which of the following will photosynthesis not occur in leaves?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll captures energy from sunlight to perform photosynthesis. This energy is used to prepare food from carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: Pitcher plant traps insects because it:

options.option: has a digestive system like human beings.
grows in soils which lack in nitrogen.

questions.explanation: Such plants do not get all nutrients from the soil, in which they grow. Therefore, to get enough nutrition they trap insects.

questions.question: The term that is used for the mode of nutrition in yeast, mushroom, and bread-mold is
questions.explanation:
Saprophytes are organisms which take nutrition from dead and decaying matter, in solution form:

  • Mushroom grows on rotting wood
  • Bread mould is fungi grown on rotten bread.

questions.question: Two organisms are good friends and live together. One provides shelter, water, and nutrients while the other prepares and provides food. Such an association of organisms is termed as
questions.explanation: Some organisms live together and share food and shelter. Such a relationship is called Symbiosis.

questions.question: Which of the following raw material is available in the air for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation:
Photosynthesis = carbon dioxide + water {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} carbohydrate + oxygen, in presence of sunlight and chlorophyll.
Carbon dioxide is taken in from the air, through pores called stomata.

questions.question: When we observe the lower surface of a leaf through a magnifying lens we see numerous small openings. Which of the following is the term given to such openings?
questions.explanation: Stomata are pores through which plants take in carbon dioxide from the air, for the process of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Cuscuta is an example of:
questions.explanation: parasite

questions.question: By which method plants make their food?
questions.explanation: Plant roots take up water and minerals from the ground and their leaves absorb a gas called carbon dioxide (CO2) from the air. They convert these ingredients into food by using energy from sunlight. This process is called photosynthesis, which means ‘making out of light’.

questions.question:
Which of the statements given below are correct?

  1. Plants convert solar energy into chemical energy
  2. Producers make their food themselves from inorganic compounds.
  3. Green plants get their food from organic compounds
  4. All green plants and blue green algae are producers
questions.explanation: Plants convert solar energy into chemical energy. All green plants and blue green algae are producers end producers make their food themselves from inorganic compounds.

questions.question: It is essential for both photosynthesis and respiration-
questions.explanation: Phytochrome is an important pigment found in plants. It is essential for both respiration and photosynthesis in green plants.

questions.question: Which of the following plants does not depend on autotrophic nutrition for food?
questions.explanation: Dodder plant

questions.question: ________ is a fungus which obtains nutrition through saprophytic nutrition.
questions.explanation: Mildew

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct?

  1. Parasitic nutrition is a heterotrophic nutrition.
  2. Saprophytic plants cannot grow without the host plant.
  3. Insectivorous plants do not perform photosynthesis at all.
  4. Insectivorous plants obtain nutrients from insects.
questions.explanation: (i) and (iv)

questions.question: Mutually beneficial relationship between Cynobacterium and a fungus is known as:
questions.explanation: Symbiosis

questions.question: Simple carbohydrate produced as a product of photosynthesis by green plants is:
questions.explanation: Glucose

questions.question: Which plant is shown in the given figure?
questions.explanation: Pitcher plant

questions.question: In biology, a relation between two organisms in which one organism is benefited and the other is usually harmed is called:
questions.explanation: Parasitism

a-cell wall, b-cell memberane, c- vacuole, d-nucleus, e-mitochondria
a-cell membrane, b-cell wall, c- nucleus, d-vacuole, e-mitochondria
a-cell wall, b-cellmembrane, c- nucleus, d-vacuole, e-mitochondria

questions.question: In human beings Cellulose can be taken as roughage in diet because:

options.option: It can be properly digested by our body.
It helps in proper functioning of digestive system.
Digestive juices will convert it into glucose.
Both It can be properly digested by our body and Digestive juices will convert it into glucose.

questions.explanation: It helps in proper functioning of digestive system.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is correct ?

  1. The large intestine is longer and wider than the small intestine in human beings.
  2. Every tooth is held in a separate socket in the jaw.
  3. Mucus protects the stomach from its own secretions of hydrochloric acid.
  4. Proteins get break down to form fatty acids.
questions.explanation: (ii) and (iii)

questions.question:
Which one is an insectivorous plant?
Sundew, Mistletoe, Cactus, Orchid
questions.explanation: Sundew plants use a sticky substance to attract and entrap insects and other small prey.

questions.question: A solution used to test the presence of starch in a leaf is ________.
questions.explanation: Iodine solution is used to test the presence of starch. Starch turns into an intense "blue-black" colour upon addition of iodine solution.

questions.question: Which one is a parasitic plant?
questions.explanation: Cuscuta is a parasitic plant. It has no chlorophyll and cannot make its own food by photosynthesis. It derives some or all of its nutritional requirement from another living plant.

questions.question: Food prepared in plants is stored as ________.
questions.explanation: The food prepared by the leaves, using air and water, in the presence of sunlight is in the form of starch.

questions.question: The energy change in photosynthesis is from
questions.explanation: Light energy to chemical energy

options.option: the loss of food material from the plant
the loss of water from the plant
the loss of air from the plant
the loss of energy from the plant

questions.explanation: Guard cell

questions.explanation: food

questions.explanation: Protein

questions.explanation: Mistletoe

questions.explanation: Cuscuta

questions.explanation: Mushroom

questions.explanation: Fungus

questions.explanation: Stoma

questions.explanation: Glucose
