questions.explanation: Lichen

questions.question: What happens to the colour of Iodine when it is added to starch?
questions.explanation: Blue-black

questions.question: Which one is an herbivorous organism?
questions.explanation: Herbivores are animals that eat plants. The organisms that feed on the only plant or plant parts are called herbivores. The cow is an herbivores organism.

questions.question: Animals which eat the flesh of other animals are called ________.
questions.explanation: The animals which eat the flesh of other animals are called carnivores. Tiger, Lion, etc are examples of carnivorous animals.

questions.question: Mode of nutrition in an animal is:
questions.explanation: Animals obtain their food from plants or other animals. So, the mode of nutrition in animals is heterotrophic.

questions.question: The mode of nutrition in which organism take food inside the body and digest using enzyme is called ________.
questions.explanation: The mode of nutrition in which organisms take complex food inside the body and digest using enzymes is called holozoic nutrition.

questions.question: Ingestion is the process in which an organism:
questions.explanation: The process of intake of food inside the body is known as ingestion. Ingestion is the first step in the process of holozoic nutrition.

questions.question: Mode of nutrition in amoeba is ________.
questions.explanation: Amoeba intakes the complex food inside the body with the help of pseudopodia. So, the mode of nutrition in amoeba is holozoic.

questions.question: Peristalsis is the:

options.option: Contraction and expansion of muscles of oesophagus

questions.explanation: Peristalsis is the contraction and expansion of muscles of the oesophagus for the passage of food towards the stomach. As the food passes through oesophagus the wall of it expands to allow passage of food.

questions.question: Which one is a ruminant animal?
questions.explanation: Those animals whose stomach is divided into four chambers to store different kinds of food are called a ruminant animal. Deer is a ruminant animal.

questions.question: Which one is a unicellular organism?
questions.explanation: Those organisms whose body is made up of only one cell are called a unicellular organism. Amoeba is a unicellular organism.

questions.question: Amoeba obtains the food using a finger-like projection called ________.
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular organism. Amoeba forms a finger-like projection on their body to intake the food inside the body, called pseudopodia.

questions.question: Which of the following component of food do not provide energy for bodybuilding?
questions.explanation: Roughage is a component of food which do not provides energy or help in bodybuilding. Roughage helps in the movement of food in the alimentary canal.

questions.question: The type of digestion which takes place within the cell is termed as:
questions.explanation: The mode of digestion which takes place inside the cell is called intracellular digestion. Most multicellular organisms digest food inside the cell.

questions.question: In amoeba, digestion of food takes place inside:
questions.explanation: The mode of nutrition in amoeba is holozoic. Amoeba digests the food inside food vacuole that contains the enzyme for digestion.

questions.question: Which one is the largest gland in the human body?
questions.explanation: The liver is the largest gland in the human body. The liver secretes bile juice that contains bile salt. Emulsification of fat occurs by the action of bile salt.

questions.question: The upper jaw of human being is ________.
questions.explanation: The upper jaw of a human being is immoveable and the lower jaw is movable as upper jaw attached with the skull bone.

questions.question: During mastication:

options.option: Food is converted into a simpler form

questions.explanation: During mastication of food saliva released from salivary glands mix with food to start the process of digestion.

questions.question: The function of villi in the small intestine is to:

options.option: Release of the enzyme for digestion of food
Decrease the surface area for the slow movement of food
Increase the surface area for absorption of digested food

questions.explanation: The small intestine of the alimentary canal contains a small finger-like projection in the inner wall called villi. Villi increase the surface area for absorption of digested food.

questions.question: The lower part of the small intestine is called ________.
questions.explanation: The small intestine is the longest part of the alimentary canal which is divided into three parts jejunum, duodenum, and ileum. The lower part of the small intestine is called the ileum.

questions.question: Pepsin is released from gastric glands in an inactive form, it gets activated due to:
questions.explanation: Gastric glands present in the stomach release gastric juice that contain pepsin, HCl, and mucus. Pepsin is get activated due to HCl.

questions.question: Amylase enyme is present in
questions.explanation: Saliva

questions.question: Rumination is the:
questions.explanation: Rumination is chewing something throughout the day. Ruminant animals store the food in one chamber of the stomach and chew the stored food while resting.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a digestive gland?
questions.explanation: The digestive gland releases enzymes or bile to help in the digestion of food. Oesophagus or food pipe does not secrete an enzyme to help in the digestion of food.

questions.question: The process of ingestion of food in amoeba is called ________.
questions.explanation: The mode of nutrition in amoeba is holozoic and the process of ingestion of food is called phagocytosis.

questions.question: Villi present in the small intestine are richly supplied by blood for:

options.option: Supply of food to upper parts of the body.
Transport of absorbed food to all parts of the body

questions.explanation: Small finger-like projections present in the small intestine to increase the surface area for absorption of food are called villi. Villi are richly supplied by blood for the transport of absorbed food to all parts of the body.

questions.question: Tooth decay is a common problem in today’s lifestyle, the acid that causes tooth decay is ________.
questions.explanation: Lactic acid released from lactobacillus bacteria is responsible for tooth decay in human beings.

questions.question: Toothpaste is generally alkaline in nature as it:
questions.explanation: When an alkali is added to acid, neutralisation reaction takes place and the acidity is removed.

questions.question: Carbohydrate: Glucose:: Protein : ?
questions.explanation: Complete digestion of carbohydrates produces glucose molecules. Similarly, the digestion of protein molecules leads to the formation of amino acids.

questions.question: The pancreas is a dual gland because it:
questions.explanation: The pancreas is a dual gland because it releases both enzymes and hormones. Pancreatic amylase help in digestion of carbohydrate and insulin hormone controls the sugar level in blood.

questions.question: How Stomach is protected from the action of HCl acid?

options.option: The inner wall of the stomach is acid-resistant.

questions.explanation: Hydrochloric acid released from the gastric gland can cause corrosion in the stomach wall but mucus released with HCl forms a protective layer on the inner wall.

questions.question: Longest part of alimentary canal having highly coiled structure is ________.
questions.explanation: Small intestine is the longest part of alimentary canal having highly coiled structure. Digestion completes in small intestine.

questions.question: Breaking down of food into simpler form using enzyme is called ________.
questions.explanation: Breaking down of complex food into simpler form using enzyme is called digestion. Enzymes are released from glands.

questions.question: The part of digestive system that contain taste buds is ________.
questions.explanation: Tongue present in buccal cavity contains taste buds to identify different kinds of tastes. Different taste like sweet, sour, salt are detected by different parts of tongue.

questions.question: The condition of frequent passage of watery stool is called ________.
questions.explanation: Diarrhoea is the condition where one passes frequent watery stool which indicates that the water content in the stool is higher than normal. This may be caused due to malabsorption or intake of contaminated food or water.

questions.question: Which one is not a function of hydrochloric acid produced in the stomach?

options.option: Killing the microorganisms that enter along with food

questions.explanation: The hydrochloric acid produced in the stomach provides acidic medium, kills the germs that enter along with food, and activates the inactive pepsin.

questions.question: Amoeba: Pseudopodia:: ? : tentacles
questions.explanation: Amoeba captures food with the help of a finger-like projection present on their body called pseudopodia. Hydra captures its prey by tentacles attached to their mouth.

questions.question: Cell-wall of a plant cell is made up of cellulose. Which of the following is not able to digest cellulose?
questions.explanation: The cell wall of the plant cell is made up of cellulose. Cellulose is not digested by human beings due to the absence of enzyme cellulase.

questions.question: Energy is obtained by using glucose during the process of:
questions.explanation: During the process of respiration oxidation of glucose takes place in which energy is released in the form of ATP.

questions.question: The longest part of the human alimentary canal is:
questions.explanation: The human alimentary canal is about 9 metres long. The small intestine is the longest part of the human alimentary canal. Most digestion takes place in the small intestine.

questions.question: The false feet of Amoeba are used for
questions.explanation: When the amoeba senses food, it pushes out the false feet which is used capture of food and movement.

questions.question: The basic process of digestion of food and release of energy is:

options.option: Process of release of energy is same

questions.explanation: The basic process of digestion of food and the release of energy during respiration is the same in all organisms.

questions.question: The sequence of steps in holozoic nutrition is:
questions.explanation: The mode of nutrition in which organism engulfs the complex food inside the body and digest with the help of enzymes is called holozoic nutrition. The steps are Ingestion, digestion, absorption, assimilation, and Egestion.

questions.question: Which of the following have the longest tongue?
questions.explanation: The tongue is a long muscular organ that helps in the mastication of food. Frogs use tongue to catch the insects. Frogs have the longest tongue.

questions.question: The human alimentary canal is about 9 m long but the human body is less than 3 m long.  How the alimentary canal is adjusted inside the body?
questions.explanation: In human beings, the alimentary canal is about 9 m long but the human body is less than 3 m long. The entire small intestine is coiled like a hose which has full of ridges and folds and these are used to increase the digestion of food and absorption of nutrients.

questions.question: Longest part of human alimentary canal is ________.
questions.explanation: The human alimentary canal is about 9 metre long. The small intestine is the longest part of the human alimentary canal. Most digestion takes place in the small intestine.

questions.question: Epiglottis is meant for protecting:
questions.explanation: Epiglottis is a small muscular flap that prevents the entry of food into windpipe or larynx.

questions.question: Chewing is required for:
questions.explanation: Chewing of food increases the surface area of food by breaking the food into smaller pieces.

questions.question: Protein digestion is necessary because:

options.option: Proteins have a number of amino acids.

questions.explanation: Digestion of protein is necessary for the absorption of protein as these are large molecules made up of a large number of amino acids.

questions.question: Removal of the gall bladder in man would lead:
questions.explanation: The liver secretes bile juice that contains bile salt. Bile salt is stored in the gall bladder. Removal of the gall bladder in man would lead to impairment of the digestion of fats.

questions.question: Conversion or digestion of large food molecules into smaller ones is due to:
questions.explanation: Digestion or breaking down of large food molecules into smaller molecules takes place by the action of enzymes released from exocrine glands.

questions.question: Which part of the human alimentary canal does not release any enzyme?
questions.explanation: Stomach, mouth, and duodenum (part of the small intestine) release enzymes for digestion of food but Oesophagus does not release any enzyme.

questions.question: Excess intake of the following causes of obesity:
questions.explanation: Excess intake of carbohydrates causes obesity. Obesity may lead to a number of diseases in human beings.

questions.question: During prolonged fasting:

options.option: First fats are used up followed by carbohydrates and proteins.
carbohydrates are used up first followed by fats and proteins
First carbohydrates are used up followed by protein and fats.
First proteins are used up followed by fats and carbohydrates.

questions.explanation: During prolonged fasting, the carbohydrates stored in body are used up first followed by fats and proteins and carbohydrates provide instant energy to the body.

questions.question: Part of the tongue that gives a feeling of sweetness is ________.
questions.explanation: Different parts of the tongue contain different kinds of taste buds to taste different tastes. The tip of the tongue gives a feeling of sweetness.

questions.question: Insects suck the food in liquid form with the help of:
questions.explanation: Insects suck the food in liquid form with the help of proboscis which is attached to the mouth of insects having sharp needle like structure.

questions.question: Biological catalysts​ that breakdown the food into simpler form is called
questions.explanation: Enzymes are biological catalyst that breakdown the food into simpler form. They are released from glands.

questions.question: Which of the following are also called the tearing teeth?
questions.explanation: There are four main types of teeth. Each has a slightly different shape and function. The canine teeth in the corners of your mouth help you tear food. Also called cuspids, the canines are often the largest teeth in the mouth. Incisors are the teeth in the front and center that you use to take bites of your food.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a part of the digestive system?
questions.explanation: Trachea and Lungs are not a part of the digestive system. Trachea and Lungs are part of the respiratory system.

questions.question: What is the mode of nutrition in animals?
questions.explanation: The mode of nutrition in which organisms take their food from other plants or animals is called heterotrophic nutrition. Heterotrophic nutrition is the mode of nutrition in animals.

questions.question: Finger-like projection called villi is found in the inner wall of:
questions.explanation: The inner walls of the small intestine are thrown into many folds which have millions of small finger-like projections called villi. Villi increase the surface area for digestion as well as absorption of digested food by eight times. Small intestine also helps in the process of absorption.

questions.question: Which of the following is a dual gland?
questions.explanation: The pancreas is referred to as a dual gland because it has two sets of glandular cells that help it carry out both endocrine and exocrine functions.

questions.question: Animals that chew cud are called ________.
questions.explanation: Ruminants swallow the food without chewing. After feeding, they bring the food from the stomach back into the mouth and chew it leisurely. This process is called rumination and such animals are called ruminants. Rumination is also called second chewing. The partially digested food in the stomach of ruminants is called cud.

questions.question: A total number of canines teeth in adult human beings are ________.
questions.explanation: Canines are sharp and pointed and are used to pierce or tear food located next to Incisors. There are 2 canines in each jaw. So, total number of canines teeth in adult human beings are 4.

questions.question: Water from undigested food is absorbed by
questions.explanation: Large intestine comprises of colon and rectum. The length of large intestine is about 1.5 meter. Large intestine receives undigested food from small intestine.   Water from the undigested food is absorbed to a great extent in the large intestine.  Semi-solid undigested waste is stored in the rectum for defecation.

questions.question: Utilization of digested food to obtain energy is called ________.
questions.explanation: Process in which absorbed food is taken by body cells and used for energy, growth and repair is called Assimilation.

questions.question: Enzymes are ________.
questions.explanation: Enzymes are soluble protein molecules that can speed up chemical reactions in cells. These reactions include respiration, photosynthesis, and making new proteins. For this reason, enzymes are sometimes called biological catalysts.

questions.question: Which of the following is the longest part of the digestive system?
questions.explanation: The small intestine is the longest part of the digestive system. It is about 20 feet or seven meters long in an adult human. The small intestine is a highly coiled tube. It consists of three parts: duodenum, jejunum, and Ileum.

questions.question: Which one is not a ruminant animal?
questions.explanation: Ruminants swallow the food without chewing. After feeding, they bring the food from the stomach back into the mouth and chew it leisurely. This process is called rumination and such animals are called as ruminants. Rumination is also called second chewing. Ruminants include, cows, buffaloes, sheep, giraffe, camel, deer etc. Dog is not a ruminant animal.

questions.question:
Given below from (i) to (iv) are some food items.

  1. Boiled and mashed potato
  2. Glucose solution
  3. A slice of bread
  4. Mustard oil

Which of the above will give blue-black colour when tested with iodine?
questions.explanation:
Starch gives blue-black colour when tested with iodine. Among the given options, boiled and mashed potato and bread slice will have starch in them therefore, they will give blue-black colour with iodine.
While mustard oil and glucose solution don't have starch content in them. They have fats and glucose content respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following pair of teeth differ in structure but are similar in function?
questions.explanation:
Molars: These are the largest teeth of all. They are 12 in number. Their function is to grind and crush food.
Premolars: The teeth present next to molars are the premolars. They are 8 in number. They also perform the same function as that of molars i.e. grinding and crushing of food.
*premolars and molars have a flat top surface.
Canines: These are the next set of teeth. They are 4 in number. Their main work is to tear the food. These are present mainly in the carnivorous animals.
Incisors: These are the first set of teeth that develop in a baby. They are a total 8 in number and are located exactly in front of the mouth. They are used for biting.

questions.question: The swallowed food moves downwards in the alimentary canal because of

options.option: the flow of water taken with the food
the contraction of muscles in the wall of food

questions.explanation: The food we take from our buccal cavity moves down the stomach through the oesophagus because of the contraction of the muscle present in the food pipe. This process of muscle contraction is known as peristalsis.

questions.question: The acid present in the stomach

options.option: protects the stomach lining from harmful substances.
kills the harmful bacteria that may enter along with the food.

questions.explanation: The acid present in the stomach is Hydrochloric acid which is secreted by the oxyntic or the parietal cells of the stomach. They help in killing the harmful bacteria that may enter our body along with the food. With this, they also make the medium acidic which further helps in protein digestion.

questions.question: The finger-like outgrowths of Amoeba help to ingest food. However, the finger-like outgrowths of the human intestine help to
questions.explanation: The small finger-like projections which are present in the small intestine are known as villi. Villi has many infoldings that increase the surface area for absorption. It increases the absorption area to many folds and the digested food in the small intestine is absorbed easily.

questions.question: The enzymes present in the saliva convert
questions.explanation: Saliva consists of salivary amylase enzyme which breaks starch into simple sugar which is further digested by enzymes in the stomach and small intestine.

questions.question: Cud is the name given to the food of ruminants which is
questions.explanation: The animals every time cannot get food whenever they are hungry instead they need to store the food whenever it is available to them. So, some animals like cows swallow a large amount of food at once and it gets stored in a different part of the stomach known as rumen. Here the food is stored as well as partially digested. Now this swallowed, stored and partially digested food is known as cud which returns to the mouth for chewing. The animals having this practice are known as ruminants and the process is known as rumination.

questions.question: Cellulose-rich food substances are a good source of roughage in human beings because

options.option: the cellulose-digesting bacteria convert cellulose into fibres.
cellulose gets absorbed in the human blood and converts into fibres.
cellulose breaks down into smaller components which are egested as roughage.
human beings do not have cellulose-digesting enzymes.

questions.explanation: Cellulose is a component present in plant cell wall. Human beings are unable to digest this cellulose because of absence of enzyme cellulase in the body which helps in cellulose digestion.

questions.question: Sugar by the view point of chemistry is-
questions.explanation: As a chemical term, ‘sugar’ usually refers to all carbohydrates of the general formula Cn(H2O)n. Sucrose is a disaccharide, or double sugar, being composed of one molecule of glucose linked to one molecule of fructose.

questions.question: Which vitamin is made most quickly or vigorously in our body?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is formed by the liver in sunlight or other sources of light and it is formed in the human body much faster than other vitamins.

questions.question: Fat is completely digested in the
questions.explanation: small intestine

questions.question:
Select the INCORRECT match.
Humming bird - Sucking, Buccal cavity - Alimentary canal, Rumen - Tiger, Contractile vacuole - Amoeba
questions.explanation: Rumen - Tiger

questions.question: The foodpipe is also called the ________.
questions.explanation: oesophagus

questions.question: How many pairs of salivary glands are there in human beings?
questions.explanation: Three

questions.question: The hormones insulin and glycogen are secreted by ________.
questions.explanation: The pancreas secretes insulin and glucagon.

questions.question:
Which one of the following nutrients cannot be digested by human beings?
Proteins, Carbohydrates, Fats, Cellulose
questions.explanation: Humans cannot digest cellulose in their food due to the absence of rumen.

questions.question: The given figure shows the movement of food through the oesophagus. This movement is aided by the muscles of the oesophagus in a wave-like action called ________.
questions.explanation: peristalsis

questions.question: The given figures show different types of teeth. Which of these is used for biting and cutting of food?
questions.explanation: X : Incisor : used for biting and cutting of food.

questions.question:
Which of the following pair of teeth differ in structure but are similar in function?
Canines and Incisors, Molar and Premolars, Incisors and Molars, Premolars and canines
questions.explanation: Molar and Premolars

questions.question: Frog uses its long, sticky ________ to catch insects.
questions.explanation: tongue

questions.question: The process of breaking complex molecules into simple molecules is called ________.
questions.explanation: ingestion

questions.question: The chemicals that help to digest the food are called ________.
questions.explanation: Digestive enzymes are substances produced by our bodies that help us to digest the food we eat.

questions.question: Glycogen formed from excessive glucose in the blood gets stored in ________.
questions.explanation: liver

questions.question: The hardest substance in the human body is ________.
questions.explanation: enamel

questions.question: The pointed teeth in your mouth are
questions.explanation: canines

questions.question: The teeth that are used for biting an apple are
questions.explanation: incisors

questions.question: What kind of teeth do you think are especially developed in carnivores?
questions.explanation: Canines

questions.question: Which of the following helps in the digestion of food?
questions.explanation: Enzymes

questions.question: The teeth adapted for flesh eating are
questions.explanation: canines

questions.question: Saliva contains an enzyme called
questions.explanation: amylase

questions.question: During digestion, fats are changed into
questions.explanation: fatty acids and glycerol

questions.question: Bile is produced in the
questions.explanation: liver

questions.question: Absorption of food takes place in
questions.explanation: small intestine

questions.question: Which of the following organs does not secrete digestive juices?
questions.explanation: Oesophagus

questions.question: The greatest amount of digestion of food, in humans takes place in the
questions.explanation: small intestine

questions.question: A baby passed watery stool for two days. The doctor suggested oral rehydration solution (ORS) for the baby. How would the ORS help the baby?

options.option: It would kill the bacteria that caused the infection
It would replace the fluids and minerals lost from the body
It would prevent water from moving out of the body

questions.explanation: It would replace the fluids and minerals lost from the body

questions.question: Where does digestion of food start in the human digestive system?
questions.explanation: Mouth

questions.question:
An amoeba ingests a food particle.
Which of these shows the correct sequence of nutrition in the amoeba?

options.option: Egestion {tex}\to{/tex} Digestion {tex}\to{/tex} Assimilation {tex}\to{/tex} Absorption
Digestion {tex}\to{/tex} Absorption {tex}\to{/tex} Assimilation {tex}\to{/tex} Egestion

questions.explanation: Digestion {tex}\to{/tex} Absorption {tex}\to{/tex} Assimilation {tex}\to{/tex} Egestion

questions.question: What are proteins broken down into?
questions.explanation: Amino acids

questions.question: A decomposer is an organism that obtains nourishment by
questions.explanation: remains of plants and animals.

questions.question: When digested, starch in the food is broken down into
questions.explanation: cellulose

questions.question: Which of the following is the simplest form of protein?
questions.explanation: Amino acids

questions.question: Which of the following is a scavenger?
questions.explanation: Vulture

questions.question: When digested, protein in the food is broken down into
questions.explanation: amino acids

questions.question: During digestion, fat in the food is broken down into
questions.explanation: fatty acids

questions.question: In a symbiotic relationship:
questions.explanation: both the organisms benefit

questions.question: How many canines are present in an adult human?
Which of the following is known as tearing teeth?
questions.explanation: Canines

questions.question: Which enzyme is present in the saliva?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase

questions.question: What protects the inner lining of the stomach?
questions.explanation: Mucus

questions.question: Which of the following enzymes digest the protein in the stomach?
questions.explanation: Pepsin

questions.question: The chamber of the ruminant stomach that has a large number of microorganisms is the
questions.explanation: 1st chamber

questions.question: In which part of the digestive system gastric juice is produced?
questions.explanation: Stomach

questions.question: The walls of which organ is lined with a membrane called mucosa?
questions.explanation: Stomach

questions.question: In which part of the digestive system is bile stored?
questions.explanation: Gall bladder

questions.question: Glucose from digested food and oxygen from the inhaled air react in the cells to produce
questions.explanation: carbon dioxide

questions.question: Silkworms feed on leaves of:
questions.explanation: Silkworms can only survive on mulberry leaves (genus Morus), sometimes Osage orange (Maclura pomifera) or silkworm food, which is made from mulberry leaves.

questions.question: Which of the following process is NOT part of wool extraction?
questions.explanation: Reeling

questions.question: Which country is the most producing silk in the world?
questions.explanation: China

questions.question: The fleece are combed to remove
questions.explanation: Burrs

questions.question: The industry related to fabric and clothing is called:
questions.explanation: The textile industry is related to fabric and clothing. The textile industry is primarily concerned with the design and production of yarn, cloth, clothing, and their distribution. The raw material may be natural, or synthetic using products of the chemical industry. The textile industry is one of the largest industries employing a large number of people.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a type of silk?
questions.explanation: Moth silk

questions.question: Science of rearing silk is called ________.
questions.explanation: The science of rearing silk is called sericulture. Sericulture includes rearing, processing, and reeling silk.

questions.question: Paheli wanted to buy a gift of animal fibre obtained without killing the animal. Which of the following would be the right gift for her to buy?
questions.explanation: Woollen shawl

questions.question: Wool fibre cannot be obtained from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Moth

questions.question: The general process that  take place at a sheep shearing shed is

options.option: washing of sheep fibre to remove grease

questions.explanation: removal of fleece

questions.question: Weavers weave the silk thread into:
questions.explanation: Weavers weave the silk thread into silk cloth. Silk cloth is used to make garments. Extracting raw silk starts by cultivating the silkworms on mulberry leaves. Once the worms start pupating in their cocoons, these are dissolved in boiling water in order for individual long fibres to be extracted and fed into the spinning reel.

questions.question: The term sericulture is used for
questions.explanation: rearing of silkworm

questions.question: Why silk does not detangle like wool?

options.option: Silk is obtained directly as a thread from the cocoon

questions.explanation: Silk thread does not detangle like wool because silk is obtained directly as a thread from the cocoon. Silk thread is made up of protein whereas wool after being carded, the wool fibers are spun into yarn. Spinning for woolen yarns is typically done on a mule spinning machine, while worsted yarns can be spun on any number of spinning machines. After the yarn is spun, it is wrapped around bobbins, cones, or commercial drums.

questions.question: Sheep are reared by:
questions.explanation: Sheep are reared by Shepherds. Shepherds move with a herd of sheep from one place to another. Sheep farming is the raising and breeding of domestic sheep. It is a branch of animal husbandry. Sheep are raised principally for their meat (lamb and mutton), milk (sheep's milk), and fiber (wool). They also yield sheepskin and parchment.

questions.question: Why Scoring is not done for cotton but it is done for wool?

options.option: Because fleece is hard but the cotton fibre is soft
Because cotton in natural fibre but wool is semi-synthetic
Because fleece is body hair containing germ and grease

questions.explanation: Scoring is the washing of wool with the skin of sheep to remove grease and dust. Scoring is not done for cotton but it is done for wool to remove germs, grease, and dirt. Greasy wool contains large sums of contaminants. Before it can be used as a textile, the contaminants must be removed by scouring (washing). whereas cotton is available from plants that do not contain contaminants when compared to wool.

questions.question: Protein is made up of:
questions.explanation: Proteins are made up of smaller building blocks called amino acids, joined together in chains. There are 20 different amino acids. Some proteins are just a few amino acids long, while others are made up of several thousand. These chains of amino acids fold up in complex ways, giving each protein a unique 3D shape.

questions.question: What do you mean by shearing?

options.option: Process of removing hair from the body of animals
Process of making woollen fabrics from fibre

questions.explanation: The process of removing hair from the body of animals to obtain wool is called shearing. Sheep shearing is the process by which the woollen fleece of a sheep is cut off. The person who removes the sheep's wool is called a shearer.

questions.question: Silk is better than cotton because:

options.option: It can be used in summer and winter both

questions.explanation: Silk is better than cotton because silk can be used in summer as well as winter. Silk absorbs sweats in summer and warm in winter. Bamboo sheets feel soft, silky, and are highly durable – while also boasting eco-credentials. Breathable, antimicrobial, and anti-allergenic, silk is a great choice for allergy sufferers and those with skin conditions such as asthma. It feels luxurious and is considered a better insulator than Egyptian cotton.

questions.question: Soft under fur of Kashmiri goat is used to woven shawls called:
questions.explanation: Wool is also obtained from goat hair. The underfur of Kashmiri goat is soft, it is woven into fine shawls called Pashmina shawls.

questions.question: Sheep body is covered with a thick layer of hair because:

options.option: Hairs trap air and keep them warm

questions.explanation: Wool is obtained from the fleece (hair ) of sheep, goat, camel, yak, llama, alpaca, and other animals. These animals have a thick coat of hair on their bodies because the hair traps air and air is a poor conductor of heat. So the hair keeps their body warm.

questions.question: Woollen yarn is used to:
questions.explanation: Woolen is a soft, short-staple, carded wool yarn typically used for knitting. In traditional weaving, woolen weft yarn (for softness and warmth) is frequently combined with a worsted warp yarn for strength on the loom.

questions.question: The disease associated with works a person perform is called ________.
questions.explanation: An occupational disease is a disease or disorder that is caused by the work or working conditions. This means that the disease must have developed due to exposures in the workplace and that the correlation between the exposures and the disease is well known in medical research.

questions.question: Sheep are reared for:
questions.explanation: Sheep farming is the raising and breeding of domestic sheep. It is a branch of animal husbandry. Sheep are raised principally for their meat (lamb and mutton), milk (sheep's milk), and fiber (wool). They also yield sheepskin and parchment.

questions.question: Wool: Sheep:: Silk : ?
questions.explanation: Wool is obtained from sheep. Similarly, silk is obtained from silk moth that produces cocoon having silk thread.

questions.question: Sheep of better varieties are obtained by:
questions.explanation: Sheep of better varieties are obtained by selective breeding of desired varieties of sheep. Selective breeding (also called artificial selection) is the process by which humans use animal breeding and plant breeding to selectively develop particular phenotypic traits (characteristics) by choosing which typically animal or plant males and females will sexually reproduce and have offspring together.

questions.question: Shearing of sheep is done in:
questions.explanation: Sheep shearing is always done after winter because in summer season the day is hot so the sheep can survive without their fur and before winter comes they can grow their fur again.

questions.question: Which breed of sheep produces good quality wool?
questions.explanation: The Lohi sheep are found in southern Punjab in Pakistan. It is used for its carpet quality wool and meat production. The mature weight of the males is 65kg with the female at 45kg. The average annual wool clip is 3.0kg of medium wool (39.8m fiber diameter). The average daily milk production from the Lohi ewes is 1.2 liters. The very large lop ears, which are usually also tasseled, are the most distinctive feature of the breed.

questions.question: Sorter’s job is risky because he may get:
questions.explanation: Sorter’s job is risky because he may get a sorter’s disease which is a fatal blood disease. sorter's job leads to get infected by a bacteria called anthrax which causes fatal blood disease called sorter's disease.

questions.question: Male silk moth is:

options.option: Some time smaller, some time larger than female moth

questions.explanation: Male silk moth is smaller than the female moth. Female silk moth lays eggs that grow into cocoons to produce silk.  male silk moths, who born from their cocoons have the capacity to copulate with hundreds of female silk moths before tiring. Within minutes of emerging from their silky cocoons, the male silk moth must be separated and collected before it copulates with the females.

questions.question: The scientific name of the mulberry silk moth is:
questions.explanation: The scientific name of the mulberry silk moth is Bombyx mori. Silkworm is the larva or caterpillar of Bombyx mori. The silkworm is the larva or caterpillar of the domestic silkmoth, Bombyx mori (Latin: "silkworm of the mulberry tree"). It is an economically important insect, being a primary producer of silk.

questions.question: Reeling in the process of making:
questions.explanation: The process of making yarn from fibre is called reeling. The thread obtained from reeling is used to make cloths by weaving. For example, The process of drawing silk fibre from the cocoon is called 'reeling'. The cocoons are cooked in hot water and the silk fibre is unwound from the cocoons.

questions.question: Big reels on which yarn is wound is called ________.
questions.explanation: The big reel on which yarn is wounded is called bobbins. Bobbins are used to weave the cloth using a machine or handloom. The process of arranging two sets of yarn together to make a fabric is called weaving. The big reels of yarn are called bobbins

questions.question: The rearing of silkworms for obtaining silk is called
questions.explanation: Sericulture is the science that deals with the production of silk by rearing of silkworm. Silk originating in the spittle of an insect is a natural fibrous substance and is obtained from pupal nests or cocoons spun by larvae known as silkworm.

questions.question: Why sheep after shearing is deepened into an alcohol solution?
questions.explanation: Sheep is deepened into an alcohol solution after shearing to prevents the sheep from infection as the outer skin is also removed during shearing.

questions.question: Hair of sheep traps the air that helps them to keep warm as:

options.option: Air is a poor conductor of heat
Air is a good conductor of heat

questions.explanation: Some animals that live in cold places generally have a thick coat of hair on their body. These coating helps the animals to trap air in it and keep them warm as air is a poor conductor of heat. Air trapped by the hair on the body of animals does not let the warmth to escape from the body. Hence, these thick covering of hair on the animal body protects them from cold.

questions.question: Selective breeding is the method to:
questions.explanation: The selective breeding method is used to obtain sheep with specific traits such as sheep with soft under hair. Selective breeding, also known as artificial selection, is a process used by humans to develop new organisms with desirable characteristics. Breeders select two parents that have beneficial phenotypic traits to reproduce, yielding offspring with those desired traits.

questions.question: Yak wool is common in:
questions.explanation: Yak wool is common in Tibet and Ladakh. Angora wool is obtained from angora goats, found in hilly regions such as Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.question: Shearing does not hurt the sheep because:
questions.explanation: Shearing is removing of upper skin along with hair to obtain wool. Shearing does not hurt the sheep because the upper most skin of sheep is dead. Shearing of sheep involves removing wool (hair of sheep). wool is a dead tissue just like our hair. So, shearing of sheep does not hurt it.

questions.question: Which two Indian breeds of sheep are mostly found in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Two common breeds of sheep mostly found in Gujarat is Marwari and Patanwadi that give a better quality of wool.

questions.question: Mulberry silk is the best silk because:

options.option: It is soft and can be coloured easily
It is elastic and have long fibre

questions.explanation: Mulberry silk threads that are rounder, finer, smoother, lighter, and more uniformly colored than any other type of silk in the world. Mulberry silk fibers are also extremely strong,

questions.question: Sericulture is an old occupation in:
questions.explanation: Sericulture is the method of rearing silkworms to obtain silk. Sericulture is an old occupation in India and China. Sericulture, or silk farming, is the cultivation of silkworms to produce silk. Silk was first produced in China as early as the Neolithic period and It is a very old occupation in India.

questions.question: For obtaining silk:

options.option: Moths are reared and cocoons are collected
Moths are reared and caterpillar is collected

questions.explanation: For obtaining silk, silk moths are reared and cocoons are collected. Collected cocoons are boiled in water to loosen the silk to obtain silk thread. or The silk thread or yarn is obtained from the silk moth's cocoon. Sericulture, or silk farming, is the rearing of silkworms for the production of raw silk. Silkworms are reared under suitable conditions of temperature and humidity to obtain silk threads from their cocoons.

questions.question: Silkworms feed on leaves of
questions.explanation: Silkworms can ONLY survive on mulberry leaves (genus Morus), sometimes Osage orange (Maclura pomifera) or silkworm food, which is made from mulberry leaves.

questions.question: Sorter disease is a:
questions.explanation: Sorter disease is a fatal blood disease that enters the bloodstream of the sorter while processing wool. A pulmonary form of anthrax that results from the inhalation of spores of the bacterium Bacillus anthracis in the wool of contaminated sheep.

questions.question: Selective breeding of sheep is done to obtain:

options.option: Both soft under and coarse hair sheep

questions.explanation: Selective breeding is a process of selecting the parents for obtaining special characters in the offspring. Selective breeding is done to have sheep with soft under hair.

questions.question: What is the difference between wool and beard of sheep?

options.option: Beard is long and wool is shorter
Both of them are of the same quality

questions.explanation:
Skin of the sheep has two types of fibres that form its fleece:

  1. the coarse beard hair, and
  2. the fine soft under-hair close to the skin.

questions.question: The favourable climatic condition for sheep is:
questions.explanation: The cold and dry condition is favourable climatic condition for sheep for breeding.

questions.question: Air is a poor conductor of heat that is used to:

options.option: Keep the body cool by cotton clothes

questions.explanation: Woollen clothes keep our body warm because air trapped in the woollen fabric is a poor conductor of heat. Sheep grow wool as protection for themselves. As a result, they have evolved to grow just enough wool for protection from the cold and to keep cool in the summer.

questions.question: Wool commonly available in the market is:
questions.explanation: Wool can be obtained from sheep, goat, yak, and camel but the most commonly available wool in the market is sheep wool. Though we get wool from many other animals sheep wool is more common because it is widely available and approximately 90 percent of the world's sheep produce wool. One sheep produces anywhere from 2 to 30 pounds of wool annually.

questions.question: Where was silk first produced in 3000 B.C?
questions.explanation: China

questions.question: Sheep are reared for getting
questions.explanation: Sheep are reared for getting wool and milk. Sheep is also important source of meat in some part of India.

questions.question: Fleece is the
questions.explanation: The hair of sheep is called as fleece. Fleece is processed to obtain wool fibres.

questions.question: The machine that comb loose wool in sheet is called
questions.explanation: Carding machine is used to comb loose wool in sheet is called as carding machine. The carding machine is located in the spinning process between the blow room and the drawing frame. It cleans and parallelizes the fibers. The resulting card web is then formed into a tape and placed into a can. The working elements are taker-in, main cylinder, card top and doffing cylinder.

questions.question: Thick coat of hair on sheep’s body is called
questions.explanation: Thick coat of hair on sheep’s body is called as fleece. It is used to make wool fibres.

questions.question: The process of obtaining silk fibre from cocoons is called
questions.explanation: The process of obtaining silk fibre from cocoons is called reeling. It involve boiling the cocoon to loosen the thread.

questions.question: Silk yarn is as strong as
questions.explanation: Silk yarn is as strong as steel and good absorber of sweat so, comfortable in all seasons.

questions.question: Wool is obtained from fleece of
questions.explanation: Wool is obtained from fleece of sheep and goat to obtain woollen fabrics.

questions.question: The fibres obtain from cocoon of silk worm is
questions.explanation: Silk fibre is obtained from cocoon of silk worm.

questions.question: The process of removal of fleece from the body of sheep is called
questions.explanation: The process of removing of fleece from the body of sheep is called as shearing. It is done in hot weather. Sheep shearing is the process by which the woollen fleece of a sheep is cut off. The person who removes the sheep's wool is called a shearer. Typically each adult sheep is shorn once each year (a sheep may be said to have been "shorn" or "sheared", depending upon dialect).

questions.question: The silk and wool are made of a kind of
questions.explanation: The silk and wool are made of a kind of proteins arranged in a particular manner. Silk is a "natural" protein fiber, some forms of which can be woven into textiles where as Wool fibre is chiefly composed of the animal protein keratin.

questions.question: Small fluffy fibres present in wool is called
questions.explanation: The small fluffy fibres present in wool are called burrs. These are the same fluffy fibres which appear on woollen clothes as small threads.

questions.question: The route through which traders and travellers uses to visit china is known as
questions.explanation: The Silk Road or Silk Route was an ancient network of trade routes that were for centuries central to cultural interaction originally through regions of Eurasia connecting the East and West and stretching from the Korean peninsula and Japan to the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.question: Why are cotton clothes more common than silk clothes?
questions.explanation: Cotton clothes are more common than silk clothes as cotton clothes are less expensive and easy to maintain when compared to that of silk.

questions.question: Why is woollen yarn stronger than cotton yarn?

options.option: More fibres are twisted together to form woollen yarn
Woollen yarn is made up of protein but cotton yarn is made of cellulose.
Woollen fibres are smaller then cotton yarn

questions.explanation: In woollen yarn a number of fibers are twisted together to form woolen yarn to make it stronger than cotton yarn. Woolen yarn is stronger than cotton yarn because more fibres are twisted together to make the woolen yarn as compare to cotton yarn.

questions.question: Why Scouring is not done for cotton but it is done for wool?

options.option: Because fleece is body hair containing germ and grease
Because fleece is hard but cotton fibre is soft
Because cotton in natural fibre but wool is semi-synthetic

questions.explanation: Scouring is not done before using cotton because cotton does not contain so much of dirt and harmful organisms but wool contains.

questions.question: Cellulose: cotton:: protein :?
questions.explanation: Cotton is made of cellulose molecules while the fiber of silk mainly composes of the protein fibroin and is produced by certain insect larvae to form cocoons.

questions.question: The process of growing silk moth on mulberry leaf is called
questions.explanation: Sericulture, or silk farming, is the cultivation of silkworms to produce silk. Although there are several commercial species of silkworms, Bombyx mori (the caterpillar of the domesticated silk moth) is the most widely used and intensively studied silkworm.

questions.question: Angora goats are found in:
questions.explanation: Angora goats are found in hilly areas such as Jammu and  Kashmir. The Angora goat (Turkish: Ankara keçisi) is a breed of domestic goat that is named after Ankara, Turkey, historically known as Angora. Angora goats produce the lustrous fibre known as mohair.

questions.question: Which is not related to silk industry?
questions.explanation: Apiculture or Beekeeping is the maintenance of hives of honey bees for the production of honey.

questions.question: Silk thread is obtained from
questions.explanation: The third stage in the life cycle of silk moth is pupa. The grown up silk worm or larva stops eating and secrets a sticky fluid, a protein called fibroin from its salivary gland which hardens on exposure to air and  becomes silk fibre.. This silk fibre is wrapped around its body to form a ball-like structure called cocoon. At this stage, the larva becomes a pupa.

questions.question: The process of rearing silkworms in order to obtain silk is called:
questions.explanation: The rearing of silkworms for obtaining silk is called sericulture. In this process, farmers take care of the silkworms, keep their eggs under hygienic conditions, and suitable conditions of temperature and humidity.

questions.question: Which among the following is an animal fibre?
questions.explanation: Silk fibres are animal fibres. Silkworms spin the ‘silk fibres’, as the female silk moth lays eggs, from which hatch larvae which are called caterpillars grow. When the caterpillar is ready to enter the next stage of its life history called pupa, it first weaves a net to hold itself. Then it swings its head from side to side. During these movements of the head, the caterpillar secretes fibre made of a protein which hardens on exposure to protein air and becomes silk fibre. Soon the caterpillar completely covers itself by silk fibres. This covering is known as a cocoon.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a breed of sheep?
questions.explanation: Murrah is a breed of buffalo, and all the other are a breed of sheep, Marwari type of sheep are generally found in Gujarat that has coarse wool. Lohi found in Rajasthan, Punjab has good quality wool and Nali in Rajasthan, Haryana, Punjab have wool to weave carpets. Murrah is a breed of buffalo.

questions.question: Which of the following do not yield wool?
questions.explanation: Wool comes from sheep, goat, yak and some other animals. These wool-yielding animals bear hair on their body. For obtaining wool, sheep are reared, in some areas camel, yak are also reared to obtain wool. Their hair is cut and processed into wool.

questions.question: The silkworm is
questions.explanation: The silkworm is both a caterpillar and a larva.

questions.question: Selective breeding is a process of

options.option: selecting the off springs with desired properties.
selecting fine hair for good quality wool.

questions.explanation: Selective breeding is the process of the crossing of two selected varieties having different traits to produce a hybrid having good traits of both, e.g. some breeds of sheep possess only soft under-hair. Their parents are specially selected to give birth to sheep which have only soft under-hair.

questions.question: Reeling of silk is

options.option: the process of taking silk threads from cocoon.

questions.explanation: The process of unwinding the silk filaments from the cocoon and combining them together to make a thread of raw silk is called reeling. For commercial purposes, 3-10 fine filaments are reeled together at a time to produce desired diameters of raw silk or reeled silk. The final viable length of reeled filament is 300 - 600m.

questions.question: Silkworms secrete fibre made of
questions.explanation: Salivary gland of the silkworm secretes fibre made up of protein sericin and fibroin.

questions.explanation: Carding
