questions.question: When matter gets warner, the atoms and molecules in that matter
questions.explanation: move faster

questions.question: At night, how do the temperatures of land and water compare?

options.option: It depends on the type of land

questions.explanation: The people living in coastal areas experience an interesting phenomenon. During the day, the land gets heated faster than the water. The air over the land becomes hotter and rises up. The cooler air from the sea rushes in towards the land to take its place. The warm air from the land moves towards the sea to complete the cycle. The air from the sea is called the sea breeze. At night it is exactly the reverse. The water cools down more slowly than the land. So, the cool air from the land moves towards the sea. This is called the land breeze.

questions.question: The freezing point of water is:
questions.explanation: The temperature at which water starts changing into solids is called the freezing point of water. Water freezes at 0 degrees Celcius.

questions.question: Stainless steel pans are usually provided with copper bottoms. The reason for this could be that

options.option: copper is a better conductor of heat than the stainless steel
copper bottom makes the pan more durable
copper is easier to clean than the stainless steel

questions.explanation: copper is a better conductor of heat than the stainless steel

questions.question: Bodies transmitting heat via radiation

options.option: make heat travels in one direction only

questions.explanation: do not require any medium

questions.question: A Themos flask prevents loss or gain of heat by
questions.explanation: All the above

questions.question: Clinical thermometer has marking from 35{tex}^0{\text{C}}{/tex} to
questions.explanation: Clinical thermometer is used to measure body temperature. It has marking from 35 degree C to 42 degree C. A clinical thermometer consists of a long, uniform glass tube having a bulb at one end.This bulb contains a mercury.We should always wash a clinical thermometer with an antiseptic solution before using.

questions.question: A marble tile would feel cold as compared to a wooden tile on a Winter morning, because the marble tile

options.option: is a poor conductor of heat than the woodem title
is a better conductor of heat than the wooden tile
is polished while wooden tile is not polished

questions.explanation: is a better conductor of heat than the wooden tile

questions.question: Ventilation in the room is due to:
questions.explanation: Ventilation in the room is due to convection as it circulates air in the house. There is a constant supply of fresh air and this removes the air present before. The process of convection takes place inside the room. Hot air moves upward and the fresh air from the ventilation comes in to takes its place.

questions.question: A beggar wrapped himself with a few layers of newspaper on a cold winter night. This helped him to keep himself warm because

options.option: friction between the layers of newspaper produces heat
air trapped between the layers of newspaper is a bad conductor of heat
newspaper is at a higher temperature than the temperature of the surrounding

questions.explanation: air trapped between the layers of newspaper is a bad conductor of heat

questions.question: Temperature changes with change in:
questions.explanation: With the increase in heat energy, the particles of matters start moving faster that increases the temperature of the body.

questions.question: Which of the following is an insulator:
questions.explanation: Those substances that do not allow heat to pass through them easily are poor conductors of heat and they are known as insulators. Copper, aluminium, and graphite is conductor but the air is a poor conductor of heat, so it is an insulator.

questions.question: Normal body temperature is ________.
questions.explanation: Normal body temperature is about 37 Degree Celcius which may vary with person to person. It could be slightly higher or slightly lighter. Normal temperature is the average body temperature of a large number of healthy persons.

questions.question: Paheli and Boojho measured their body temperature. Paheli found her's to be 98.6oF and Boojho recorded 37oC. Which of the following statement is true?

options.option: Paheli has a higher body temperature than Boojha
Paheli have lower bodt temperature than Boojha

questions.explanation: Both have normal body temperature

questions.question: Which precaution is essential while measuring the body temperature using a thermometer?
questions.explanation: While measuring the body temperature using a thermometer we should ensure the mercury levels below 35 degrees Celsius. We should wash the thermometer with an antiseptic solution and hold it firm and give it a few jerks. The jerks will bring the mercury level down. The normal body temperature is 37 degrees Celsius.

questions.question: Handles of saucepans and other cooking utensils are
questions.explanation: thermal insulators

questions.question: In the weather report, the daily temperature is measured with:
questions.explanation: In weather reports, the daily temperature is measured with the maximum-minimum temperature that records maximum and minimum temperatures of the previous day.

questions.question: Heat flows from:
questions.explanation: Heat flows from hotter region to colder region through conduction, convection, or radiation. In solids, heat is transferred by the process of conduction. In liquids and gases, heat is transferred by the process of convection and no medium is required for the transfer of heat by radiation.

questions.question: The range of laboratory thermometer is ________.
questions.explanation: The range of laboratory thermometer is -10 degrees Celsius to 110 degrees Celsius. Laboratory thermometer is used in laboratories to measure temperature with a high level of accuracy other than the human body.

questions.question: The process by which heat is transferred from hotter end to colder end is called ________.
questions.explanation: The process by which heat is transferred from hotter end to colder end within the object by passing heat to an adjacent molecule is called conduction. Heat is transferred by the process of conduction generally in solids.

questions.question: The windows of the house in coastal areas are made to face the sea to:

options.option: To see the beauty of the sea

questions.explanation: In coastal areas, the sea breeze blows during the day so, the windows of houses in coastal areas are made to face the sea to receive the cooler sea breeze. During the day, the land gets heated faster than the water. The air over the land becomes hotter and rises up and the cooler air from the sea rushes in towards the land to takes its place. The warm air from the land goes out and the cool air from the sea moves towards the land. This is why the windows of houses in coastal areas are made to face the sea to receive the cooler sea breeze.

questions.question: The temperature of hot milk can not be measured by clinical thermometer because:

options.option: Clinical thermometer is used for body temperature only
Hot milk temperature is less than the minimum range
The clinical thermometer can not be used for liquid.
Hot milk has a temperature higher than the maximum range

questions.explanation: The temperature of hot milk cannot be measured by a clinical thermometer because hot milk has a temperature higher than 42 degrees Celsius which is the maximum range of the clinical thermometer. The clinical thermometer is used to measure the body temperature so the temperature range is 35 degrees Celsius to 45 degrees Celsius.

questions.question: Level of mercury change when coming in contact of the hotter object as:
questions.explanation: In mercury thermometer, the level of mercury change when come in contact with the hotter object as mercury expands on heating. When the bulb of the thermometer comes in contact with a hot object, the mercury expands and its level rises in the glass tube, indicating the temperature. Mercury has a high coefficient of expansion.

questions.question: An iron ball at 40 degrees is dropped in a mug containing water at 40 degrees, the temperature of water:
questions.explanation: As the temperature of both water and iron ball is the same, there will be no change in the temperature of the water and remain at 40 degrees Celsius. As the temperature of both the water and the iron ball are the same, heat will not flow from iron ball to water or from water to iron ball.

questions.question: One litre of water at 30oC is mixed with one litre of water at 50oC the temperature of the mixture will be:
questions.explanation: When one litre of water at 30 degree Celsius is mixed with one litre of water 50 degree Celsius, the temperature of mixture will be more than 30 degree Celsius but less than 50 degree Celsius.As heat will flow from the hotter water to the colder water.

questions.question: Heat is defined as:

options.option: Energy transferred between objects at different temperature

questions.explanation: Energy transferred between objects at different temperatures is called heat. Heat is the form of energy that flows between two bodies/objects due to their difference in temperature. It is transferred from hotter to colder objects.

questions.question: In summer, people paint their cars with silver color in order to prevent
questions.explanation: absorption of heat

questions.question: In localities where winter temperature drops below – 40 degree Celsius, the liquid in liquid-in-glass thermometer must be:
questions.explanation: Alcohol is used as the liquid in liquid-in-glass thermometer in the localities where winter temperature drops below -40 degrees Celsius in winter. Alcohol thermometers are preferred to mercury for meteorological measurement of minimum temperatures. Because the freezing point of alcohol is very low, it can even measure temperature down to as low as -70 degrees Celsius.

questions.question: An increase of one Celsius degree corresponds to increase of:
questions.explanation:
An increase of one Celsius degree temperature corresponds to increase of 1 Kelvin temperature. The relation between Kelvin and degree Celsius is , temperature T = t (temperature in degree Celsius) + 273.15 K
When the temperature on the Celsius scale shows 0 degree C, the corresponding temperature in Kelvin is 273.15K.
When the temperature on the Celsius scale shows 1 degree C, the corresponding temperature in Kelvin is 274.15K.

questions.question: Within the Earth-atmosphere system, heat convection takes place in:
questions.explanation: In the earth's atmosphere, the transfer of heat takes place by convection in liquid and gases. Liquids and gases gets heated by the process of convection. Convection is the major factor in weather. The sun heats the earth's surface, then, when cooler air comes into contact with it, the air warms and rises, creating an upward current in the atmosphere. That current can result in the wind, clouds, or other weather.

questions.question: In response to the same input of heat, which one of the following substance exhibits the smallest rise in temperature?
questions.explanation: When the same amount of heat is given to water, ice, aluminum, and brick, the temperature of water increases the least due to high specific heat capacity. Specific heat capacity is the amount of energy required to increase the temperature of the substance by 1 degree Celsius.

questions.question: Which of the following heat transfer takes place even in a vacuum?
questions.explanation: Radiation is the mode of heat transfer that does not require any medium. It can take place whether a medium is present or not. The heat of the sun reaches to earth by radiation.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Metals are the best conductor of heat and electricity. Metals allow heat to pass through them easily. This process is known as conduction. Metals are good conductors of heat. Water, alcohol, and water vapour are poor conductors of heat.

questions.question: When two objects are in direct contact, heat always flows from:
questions.explanation: When two objects are in direct contact with each other, heat always flows from hotter to the colder body bypassing the heat to adjacent molecules.

questions.question: The heat of the sun reaches to earth by:
questions.explanation: The heat of the sun reaches to earth by the process of radiation as there is no medium such as air in most part of the space between the earth and the sun. The transfer of heat by radiation does not require any medium. It can take place whether is present or not.

questions.question: Which of the following is the fastest mode of heat transfer in the solid?
questions.explanation: Conduction is the fastest mode of heat transfer in solid because in conduction heat is transferred to adjacent molecules that are in direct contact. The heat is transferred from the hotter end to the colder end.

questions.question: Ventilation is provided near the roof in houses because:
questions.explanation: Ventilation is provided near the roof in the house because warms air being lighter in weight tends to rise above and escape from ventilators at the top. This allows the cool air to come into the room to take its place.

questions.question: In coastal areas, during night wind moves from:

options.option: Winds move from lower to the higher atmosphere

questions.explanation: In coastal areas, at night the water cools down more slowly than the land. So, the cool air from the land moves towards the sea.

questions.question: At night, the ground surface is cooler than the air over the ground therefore, heat is conducted from the:
questions.explanation: At night, the ground surface is cooler than the air over the ground therefore, heat is conducted from the atmosphere to the ground.

questions.question: In the atmosphere, the wind blows due to:
questions.explanation: In the atmosphere, the wind blows due to unequal heating at two places. In the atmosphere, the pressure is not constant as the sun heats the earth's surface unevenly. This difference in pressure turns the earth's surface into regions of low and high pressure and makes a force that causes the wind to blow.

questions.question: Mercury is a highly toxic element although it is used in thermometer because it:

options.option: It is easily visible and found in a liquid state
It has a dark colour and melts at low temperature

questions.explanation: Although mercury is a highly toxic element, it is used in thermometer because of its shining surface and expands uniformly. It has a high coefficient of expansion. The slightest change in temperature is notable.

questions.question: A wooden spoon is dipped in a cup of ice cream. Its other end
questions.explanation: Wooden is a bad conductor of heat so, heat or cold will not pass though easily  a wooden spoon dipped in cup of ice-cream does not become cold.

questions.question: Woollen sweaters have small pore although, they keep us warm because pore contains:

options.option: Air, which is a bad conductor of heat
Air, which is a good conductor of heat

questions.explanation: Woollen sweaters have small pore although they keep us warm because pore contains air which is trapped in between the woollen fibres and the air is a bad conductor of heat which prevents the flow of heat from our body to the cold surroundings.

questions.question: In conduction:
questions.explanation: In Conduction, heat is transferred from the hotter end to the colder end of an object. Generally, it happens in solids, metals are good conductors. The molecules are in contact to each other heat the adjacent molecules and thereby heat is transferred.

questions.question: Hotness and coldness are:
questions.explanation: Hotness and coldness are comparative quantities that cannot be measured in value. the hotness and coldness we feel is a physiological response to more heat or less heat flowing out of you into the object.

questions.question: Clinical thermometer is used to measure the temperature of the body because:

options.option: It is smaller can be easily carried
It has kink and suitable temperature range

questions.explanation: Clinical thermometer is used to measure the temperature of the body because it has a kink near the bulb to prevent mercury from falling on its own and also has a suitable temperature range corresponding to body temperature of 35 degrees Celsius to 42 degree Celsius.

questions.question: Heat: Calorie :: Temperature : ?
questions.explanation: The common unit of heat is calorie similarly common unit of temperature is Celsius.

questions.question: We should wash the thermometer before use with an antiseptic solution to:
questions.explanation: The thermometer should be washed before use with an antiseptic solution to make it germ-free as a thermometer should be placed inside the mouth under the tongue. And the same thermometer is used by different persons, so it should be made germ free before use.

questions.question: S.I. unit of heat is:
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of heat is Joule. Heat is measured by its effect on the states of interacting bodies. Another common unit for measuring heat is calories.

questions.question: We should avoid keeping the thermometer in the sun or near the flame:

options.option: To make it unsuitable for human use

questions.explanation: When the thermometer is kept in sun or near the flame, it may break due to the exposure of excess heat that exceeds its maximum range.

questions.question: Which of the following thermometer do not use mercury?
questions.explanation: In a digital thermometer, meter mercury is not used. It contains chips that respond to body temperature. Nowadays, digital thermometers are preferably used than clinical thermometer because of the presence of the mercury which is a toxic substance.

questions.question: Land Breeze: Night:: Sea Breeze :?
questions.explanation: The land breeze blows cool air from land to sea during the night while the sea breeze blows cool air from sea to land during the day to keep the temperature around coastal area cool.

questions.question: If two containers of same metals, one having a black bottom and other with white bottom, which will heat up early?
questions.explanation: The container with black bottom will heat up early because dark surfaces absorb more heat than light coloured surfaces. So, the black bottom container being dark in colour will absorb more heat while the white bottom container will reflect most of the heat that falls on it.

questions.question: We feel more comfortable with dark coloured clothes during winter because:
questions.explanation: Dark coloured clothes absorbs more heat than light coloured clothes to keep our body warm so, we feel more comfortable wearing dark coloured clothes during winter. While light coloured clothes reflects most of the heat that falls on them, so we feel comfortable wearing them in summer.

questions.question: The bottom of the stainless steel pan has copper because:
questions.explanation: The bottom of the stainless steel pan has copper because copper is a better conductor than steel. Copper will allow more heat to pass through easily heat the pan faster which results in the food getting cooked faster than a steel pan without a copper bottom.

questions.question: Thermos flask keeps hot liquid hot and cold liquid cold by
questions.explanation: The thermos flask keeps hot liquid hot and cold liquid cold by preventing heat loss. Thermos flask has double layered wall in which air is evacuated to prevent heat loss.

questions.question: 10oC is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Degree Celsius +273 = Temperature in Kelvin
10 degree Celsius +273 = 283K

questions.question: The bottom of the stainless steel pan is made up of copper because:
questions.explanation: Since Copper is a better conductor of heat therefore to allow the food to cook properly, the bottom of the pan is made up of copper.

questions.question: Which of the following is an insulator?
questions.explanation: Air is a poor conductor of heat and is therefore termed as an insulator.

questions.question: Conduction is the method of transfer of heat in:
questions.explanation: In solids, heat travels from the hotter end of the body to the colder end with the method of conduction.

questions.question: Which kind of clothes will you prefer most in summers?
questions.explanation: Thin clothes with white colour absorbs least of the sun rays and will keep us cool in summers.

questions.question: Glass, plastic, wood are examples of:
questions.explanation:
Insulators are materials that do not conduct heat.
Glass, plastic, and wood are categorized under Insulators as they do not allow heat to pass through them.

questions.question: Method of heat transfer in steel rod is ________.
questions.explanation: The heat gets transferred from the hotter end of the steel rod to the colder end by the process of conduction due to vibration of its molecules.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a reliable measure?
questions.explanation: Temperature is a measure of hotness or coldness of a body and is the most reliable physical quantity.

questions.question: Name the device which is used to measure the hotness or coldness of an object.
questions.explanation: Thermometer is an instrument which measures the temperature of a body and gives us a degree of hotness or coldness of a body.

questions.question: Heat always flows:

options.option: From a colder object to a hotter object
From a hotter object to a colder object
Heat never flows from one object to other

questions.explanation: When you bring two objects of different temperatures together, energy will always be transferred from the hotter to the cooler object. The objects will exchange thermal energy until their temperatures are equal. We say that heat flows from the hotter to the cooler object.

questions.question: During the night:

options.option: Land cools more quickly than the sea

questions.explanation: Land cools more quickly than the sea during the night because water is a slow conductor of heat and takes more time to cool down in comparison to land.

questions.question: The heat of sun reaches to earth by:
questions.explanation: The sun heats the earth through radiation. Since there is no medium (like the gas in our atmosphere) in space, radiation is the primary way that heat travels in space.

questions.question: The clinical thermometer has to mark from 35oC to:
questions.explanation: The range of a clinical thermometer is from 35oC to 42oC because it is the range of human body temperature.

questions.question: A wooden spoon is dipped in a cup of ice-cream, its other end:
questions.explanation: As wood does not allow heat to pass through it, so the other end of the wooden spoon has no effect on it when the other end is dipped in ice cream. Wood is a bad conductor of heat.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of temperature?
questions.explanation: The unit of temperature is degrees Celsius (oC), Fahrenheit, and Kelvin. The SI unit of temperature is the Kelvin and is assigned the unit symbol K.

questions.question: What is the range of a clinical thermometer?
questions.explanation: Clinical thermometer is used to measure the body temperature. The scale of temperature is graduated from 35°C to 42°C and or from 94°F to 108°F.

questions.question: What is filled in the bulb of a thermometer?
questions.explanation: Mercury is a metal that is in liquid state at room temperature and it readily expands or contracts at the slightest change in temperature. Hence, mercury is used in thermometer.

questions.question: What is the normal body temperature of a man?
questions.explanation: The normal body temperature is 37oC. This can differ from person to person.

questions.question: Conduction takes place in:
questions.explanation: Transfer of heat from one particle to the adjacent particle is known as the conduction of heat. Only in solids, heat can be transferred by the process of conduction.

questions.question: In figure (a–d) shows the readings on four different thermometers. Indicate which of the reading shows the normal human body temperature?
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is 37o.

questions.question: An iron ball at 40°C is dropped in a mug containing water at 40°C. The heat will

options.option: not flow from iron ball to water or from water to iron ball

questions.explanation: Because the temperature of both substances are same.

questions.question: One litre of water at 30°C is mixed with one litre of water at 50°C. The temperature of the mixture will be
questions.explanation: Heat gained by cold water will be equal to the loss by hot water.

questions.question: Generally happens, when gases are mixed-
questions.explanation: When gases are mixed the energy is released.

questions.question: Which is correct from the following for Adiabatic process?
questions.explanation: A process without transfer of heat to or from a system, so that Q = 0, is called adiabatic, and such a system is said to be adiabatically isolated

questions.question: Which of the region of a flame is called luminous zone?
questions.explanation: The innermost zone of candle flame is called the dark zone of the flame. The middle zone of the flame is the luminous zone of the candle flame and the non - luminous zone is the outermost and hottest of all zones in the candle flame.

questions.question: On heating one end of iron rod, other end also gets heated. Such materials are called
questions.explanation: good conductor of heat

questions.question: The freezing point of water at atmospheric pressure in fahrenheit system is:
questions.explanation: The Fahrenheit scale defines the freez ng point of water as 32°F and the boiling point as 212°F. The Celsius scale sets the freezing point and boiling point of water at 0°C and 100°C respectively.

questions.question: The SI unit of work is
questions.explanation: The SI unit of work is the joule (J), named after the 19th-century English physicist James Prescott Joule, and is defined as the work required to exert a force of one newton through a displacement of one metre.

questions.question: The temperature of human body is-
questions.explanation: The average normal body temperature is generally accepted as 98.6°F (37°C). Studies have shown that the normal body temperature can have a wide range, i.e., from 97°F (36.1°C) to 99°F (37.2°C).

questions.question: When any radioactive element emits {tex}\beta{/tex}-particles, then
questions.explanation: Beta decay - A common mode of radioactive decay in which a nucleus emits beta particles. The daughter nucleus will have a higher atomic number than the original nucleus.

questions.question: Camphor is purified by following procedure-
questions.explanation: Camphor can be easily purified by the process of sublimation. Sublimation is used for separating substances with on heating change directly into vapour. Camphor gets directly converted to its gaseous state, leaving the salt behind. Camphor is separated from NaCl by Sublimation.

questions.question: In the nucleus of atom is-
questions.explanation: The nucleus is a collection of particles called protons, which are positively charged, and neutrons, which are electrically neutral. Protons and neutrons are in turn made up of particles called quarks. The chemical element of an atom is determined by the number of protons, or the atomic number, Z, of the nucleus.

questions.question: That temperature at which the reading  is same at fahrenheit and Celsius.
questions.explanation: The two scales have different zero points and the Celsius degree is bigger than the Fahrenheit, However, there is one point on the Fahrenheit and Celsius scales where the temperatures in degrees are equal. This is -40 °C and -40 °F.

questions.question: Laboratory thermometer cannot be used to measure the temperature of ________.
questions.explanation: human body

questions.question: One litre water of 60oC is mixed with one litre water of 30oC. The temperature of mixtures will be ________.
questions.explanation: Tempereture will be actually 45o C

questions.question: Which one of the following is a poor conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Air

questions.question: Cooking vessels cannot be made of ________.
questions.explanation: Wood insulates from heat

questions.question: On the celsius scale, the freezing point of water is ________.
questions.explanation: 0oC

questions.question: Thermometer is used to measure ________.
questions.explanation: temperature

questions.question: Which one of the following packaging is most suitable to carry ice for longer distances?
questions.explanation: Thermocol

questions.question:
Read the statement given below and select the CORRECT option which completes the statement.
Higher the temperature of the body, higher is the ________ of molecules of that body.
questions.explanation: kinetic energy

questions.question: In case of ________, the cool air moves from the land towards the sea.
questions.explanation: land breeze

questions.question: Heat brings ________ change.
questions.explanation: Both physical and chemical

questions.question: Air over the land is heated, becomes lighter and rises while the cooler air from the sea blows towards land to take its place which is called ________.
questions.explanation: sea breeze

questions.question: You put a metal key into its metal lock and find that the key is too tight. Which of the following methods will you adopt to make the key fit properly?

options.option: Cooling the key and heating the lock
Heating the key and cooling the lock
Cooling both the key and the lock
Heating both the key and the lock

questions.explanation: We have to cool the key and heat the lock since cooling causes contraction of key and heating causes expansion of the keyway of the lock.

questions.question: Which process causes the air above the hot tea to get heated?
questions.explanation: Convection

questions.question: What must be true about two given objects for energy to be transferred as heat between them?

options.option: The objects must contain a large amount of energy.

questions.explanation: The objects must have different temperatures.

questions.question: If two objects are in thermal equilibrium with each other
questions.explanation: they cannot be at different temperatures.

questions.question: 1 calorie is about
The boiling point of liquid hydrogen is -252.87oC. What is the value of this temperature in kelvins?
A cake has to be baked at a temperature of {tex}350.6^{\circ} \mathrm{F}{/tex}. What is this temperature on the Celsius scale?
questions.explanation: 177oC

questions.question: The temperatures of the two glasses of water are {tex}30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex} and {tex}0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex}. The difference in temperature of the two glasses of water is {tex}30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex}. What is their difference in temperature on the Kelvin scale?
Room temperature is about 20 degrees on the
questions.explanation: Celsius scale

questions.question: A thermometer indicates 98.6oC. It may be
questions.explanation: in a cup of hot tea.

questions.question: Which of these thermometers would you use to measure your body temperature?
questions.explanation: Clinical thermometer

questions.question:
The figure shown below shows particles in four different containers.

Which container most likely contains a gas?
questions.explanation: Container B

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the particles in matter?

options.option: They are larger in solids than in liquids or gases.
They are larger in gases than in solids or liquids.

questions.explanation: They are always moving.

questions.question: The temperatures of one glass of water is {tex}30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex} and the temperature of another glass of water is {tex}0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex}. Both glasses contain the same amount of water. Which is a correct statement about the two glasses of water?

options.option: The molecules of the warm water have a higher average speeds.
The molecules of the warm water are larger.
The molecules of the cold water are larger.
The molecules of the cold water have a higher average speeds.

questions.explanation: The molecules of the warm water have a higher average speeds.

questions.question: What happens to most materials when they become warmer?
questions.explanation: They expand.

questions.question: A metal spoon is placed in a cup of hot coffee. Why does the temperature of the cup with spoon decrease after a short time?

options.option: Energy is removed from the coffee mostly by conduction through the spoon.
Energy is removed from the coffee mostly by radiation through the spoon.
The metal in the spoon has an extremely large specific heat.
Energy is removed from the coffee mostly by convection through the spoon.

questions.explanation: Energy is removed from the coffee mostly by conduction through the spoon.

questions.question: Which occurs if two objects at different temperatures are in contact?
questions.explanation: Conduction

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of heat transfer by conduction?

options.option: The warmth you feel sitting near a fire.
The warmth you feel holding a cup of hot milk.
Water moving in a pot of boiling water.

questions.explanation: The warmth you feel holding a cup of hot milk.

questions.question: Sea breeze

options.option: is caused by air currents set up due to conduction
is caused by air currents set up due to radiation
is caused by air currents set up due to convection
is caused by air currents set up due to vaporisation

questions.explanation: is caused by air currents set up due to convection

questions.question: Heat being transferred from the Sun to Earth is an example of which process?
questions.explanation: Radiation

questions.question: Woolen sweaters keep us warm by,

options.option: increasing the loss of heat from our body.
trapping a layer of air between our body and sweater.

questions.explanation: trapping a layer of air between our body and sweater.

questions.question: Shyam wants his noodles to cool down very soon. He must put it on a plate made of
questions.explanation: Metal

subtopics.name: Kinds of Clothes We Wear in Summer and Winter

questions.question: Which one of the given substance can be used to make handle of pressure cooker?
questions.explanation: Bakelite

questions.question: Which of these is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper

questions.question: Which of these is a bad conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Wood

questions.question: Many insulating materials contain spaces filled with air because air is what type of material?
questions.explanation: Insulator

questions.question: Which of the following is the normal body temperature?
questions.explanation: 37 degree Celsius

questions.question: Which of the following scales is used to measure temperature?
questions.explanation: Both celsius and fahrenheit

questions.question: What is transfer of heat through energy waves called as?
questions.explanation: Radiation

questions.question: What is transfer of heat through movement of the fluid itself called as?
questions.explanation: Convection

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a poor conductors of heat?
questions.explanation: Steel

questions.question: What happens when two objects having different temperatures come in contact with each other?
questions.explanation: Conduction

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Steel

questions.question: Which of the following is the poor conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Wood

questions.question: What gives rise to the breeze in coastal regions?
questions.explanation: Convection

questions.question: What is the freezing point of water in Fahrenheit scale?
What is the boiling point of water?
questions.explanation: 100 degree Celsius

questions.question: Acid turns blue litmus:
questions.explanation: When an acidic substance comes into contact with blue litmus paper, it causes a chemical reaction that turns the blue paper red. This change in color is a characteristic reaction of acids and is a simple way to identify the presence of an acid in a solution or substance.

questions.question: Which of the following is a strong acid?
questions.explanation: Sulphuric acid is a strong acid because it is mineral acid and dissociates completely into ions.

questions.question: Which gas is released when an acid reacts with a metal?
questions.explanation: When acids react with metals, hydrogen gas is released along with the formation of salt.

questions.question: Which one is an organic acid?
questions.explanation: Lactic acid is an organic acid because it is obtained from plants and animals naturally.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a natural indicator?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is a natural indicator as it turns bases red. Soap containing bases change turmeric red.

questions.question: Product of a neutralisation reaction are always
questions.explanation: a salt and water

questions.question: In case of indigestion, we use:
questions.explanation: In the case of indigestion, we use antacids. Antacids containing bases react with acids present in the stomach to neutralise them.

questions.question: All acids contain
questions.explanation: All acids contain hydrogen in common. When acid is mixed in water, hydrogen ions will produce.

questions.question: Reaction between acids and base to form salt and water is called:
questions.explanation: The reaction between acids and base to form a salt, is called a  neutralization reaction. Water is also formed along with salt. The neutalization of a strong acid and strong base has a pH value equal to 7.

questions.question: What is the common name of sodium carbonate?
questions.explanation:
The common name of sodium carbonate is washing soda. washing soda is used in the preparation of soap and detergent. Its chemical formula is Na2CO3.
It commonly occurs as a crystalline decahydrate which readily effloresces to form a white powder.

questions.question: Which one of the following metal does not release hydrogen gas with acids?
questions.explanation: Copper is lower than hydrogen in the reactivity series so it is less reactive and even does not react with acids to displace hydrogen. The metals above in the hydrogen in the activity series react with acids to liberate hydrogen. Copper fall below hydrogen in the series hence does not liberate hydrogen.

questions.question: Turmeric is a natural indicator. On adding its paste to acid and base separately, which colour would be observed

options.option: Yellow in acid and red in base.
Red in acid and blue is base.
Pink in acid and yellow in base.

questions.explanation: Yellow in acid and red in base.

questions.question: Which of the following is a strong Base?
questions.explanation: Strong bases are bases that completely dissociate in water into the cation and OH- (hydroxide ion). The hydroxides of the Group I and Group II metals usually are considered to be strong basses. Sodium hydroxide is a strong base and highly corrosive in nature.

questions.question: Sour things contain
questions.explanation: Sour things contain acids. All organic acids obtained from plants and animals are sour in taste. Mineral acids should not be tasted.

questions.question: When the soil is too basic, plant do not grow well in it. To improve its quality what must be added to the soil?
questions.explanation: Organic matter

questions.question: A solution changes the colour of tumeric indicator from yellow to red. The  solution is
questions.explanation: basic

questions.question: Which of the following set of substances contain acids?
questions.explanation: Curd, vinegar

questions.question: Which gas is released when lime water reacts with sulphuric acid?
questions.explanation: When lime water reacts with sulphuric acid, carbon dioxide gas is released. Calcium sulphate is formed. Also, heat is released in this process. When Carbon dioxide is passed through lime water, the lime water turns milky. This confirms that the gas liberated is carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Milk of magnesia contains:
questions.explanation: Milk of magnesia contains Magnesium hydroxide which is a base used as antacids. A base generally used to neutrlaization the hydrochloric acid present in our stomach.

questions.question: A solution of NaCl turns:
questions.explanation: A solution of NaCl turns pH paper green as NaCl is salt and neutral in nature.

questions.question: Eggshell is made up of:
questions.explanation: Eggshells are made up of calcium carbonate It is a semipermeable membrane which means air and moisture can pass through its pores. The shell also has a thin outermost coating called the bloom or cuticle that helps to keep out bacteria and dust.

questions.question: Curd should be stored in:
questions.explanation: Curd should be stored in glass vessels because acids present in curd react with the metal containers to form salts which may be toxic for human beings.

questions.question: Sodium carbonate is a basic salt because it is salt of:
questions.explanation: Sodium carbonate is a basic salt because it is salt formed by the reaction between a weak acid and a strong base.

questions.question: One of the constituents of baking powder is baking soda and other is ________.
questions.explanation: Baking powder is used in the bread and cake industry consists of baking soda and a mild acid called tartaric acid to neutralise the base.

questions.question: Which of the following is a olfactory indicators?
questions.explanation: Those indicators that give different smells with acid and base are called olfactory indicators. Vanilla is an olfactory indicator.

questions.question: Which of the following is acidic in nature?
questions.explanation: Lime juice contains citric acid so, it is acidic in nature.

questions.question: Tartaric acid is used in baking powder to remove
questions.explanation: Tartaric acid is used in baking powder to remove bitterness due to sodium carbonate produced on heating baking powder.

questions.question: Soda acid fire extinguisher extinguishes the fire by:
questions.explanation: Soda acid fire extinguisher extinguishes the fire by cutting the supply of oxygen as carbon dioxide gas is released when baking soda reacts with acid.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is evolved when baking soda reacts with acids?
questions.explanation: When baking soda reacts with acids, carbon dioxide gas is produced along with sodium carbonate.

questions.question: A common substance that contains acetic acid is ________.
questions.explanation: Vinegar is 5-8% acetic acid in water. It is used as a preservative of food.

questions.question: Which of the following is a base?
questions.explanation: Potassium hydroxide (KOH) is a base as it produces OH- ions in the presence of water.

questions.question: A mixture of sodium hydrogen carbonate and tartaric acid used for making a cake is called:
questions.explanation: The mixture of sodium hydrogen carbonate (baking soda) and tartaric acid used for making a cake is called baking powder. When baking powder mixes with water then sodium hydrogen carbonate reacts with tartaric acid to evolve carbon dioxide gas which gets trapped in the wet dough and bubbles out slowly making the cake rise and hence soft and spongy.

questions.question: On adding phenolphthalein indicator to a colourless solution, no change is observed. What is the nature of this solution?
questions.explanation: Either acidic or neutral

questions.question: Common name of dilute solution of acetic acid is ________.
questions.explanation: A dilute solution of acetic acid is called vinegar. Only 5-8% acetic acid is present in vinegar and the rest is water.

questions.question: Chemical name of washing soda is ________.
questions.explanation: The chemical name of washing soda is sodium carbonate (Na2CO3). It is also known as soda ash and soda crystals. It is a basic salt that dissolves in water. It is bitter in taste. The solution of washing soda changes red litmus to blue.

questions.question: The indicator which gives reddish-pink colour with acid is ________.
questions.explanation: Methyl orange gives reddish-pink colour with acid which acts as indicator. Acidic solutions have low pH value, it will be red and transverse to orange and yellow as the acidity of the solution decreases. At pH value less than 3.1, methyl orange is red and pH value greater than 4.4 it will be yellow. Between the range of 3.1 and 4.4, it is a mixture of red and yellow colours.

questions.question: Strength of acid depends upon:
questions.explanation: The strength of an acid depends upon the concentration of Hydrogen ions produced in water. Strong acids produce more hydrogen ion due to complete dissociation. A strong acid is the one that completely ionizes in a solution provided there is sufficient solvent. Strong acids have a larger acid dissociation constant and smaller logarithmic constant than weaker acids. Some examples of strong acids are Hydrochloric acids, nitric acids, sulfuric acids, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is obtained from plants and animals and can be consumed as food are called ________.
questions.explanation: An organic acid is an organic compound with acidic properties obtained from plants and animals and can be consumed as food. The most common organic acid is carboxylic acids whose acidity is associated with the carboxyl group. In general, organic acids are weak acids that do not dissociate completely in water.

questions.question: All acids can not be tasted to know its taste because it may be:
questions.explanation: All acids cannot be tasted to know its taste because mineral acids like Sulphuric acid and Hydrochloric acid are highly corrosive and cause harm. Only some organic acids which are weak can be tasted.

questions.question: Aerated drinks are slightly pungent in taste due to the presence of ________.
questions.explanation: Aerated drinks or cold drinks are slightly pungent in taste due to the presence of carbonic acid formed by reaction between carbon dioxide gas with water at high pressure. It is a reversible reaction.

questions.question: Dry HCl gas does not change the colour of dry litmus paper because:

options.option: Acids show acidic property due to water
The hydrogen ion is produced in aqueous condition
Hydrogen gas is evolved only in the presence of water

questions.explanation: Acids show acidic character only in the presence of water by producing hydrogen ions. So, dry HCl gas does not change the colour of dry litmus paper. It is the hydrogen ions that are dissociated in the water of aqueous acid that change the colour of the paper. Gaseous HCl does not have free hydrogen ions so it will not change the colour of dry litmus paper.

questions.question: Rainwater becomes acidic because of:
questions.explanation: Due to an increase in air pollution, some oxides of nitrogen and sulphur are released in the air. When these oxides react with rainwater it forms nitric acid and sulphuric acid. These acids fall with the rainwater in the form of acid rain.

questions.question: Great care should be taken while using acid or base in the laboratory because it may be:
questions.explanation: Care should be taken while using acid or base in the laboratory because mineral acids used in the laboratory is corrosive, irritating, and harmful to the skin. Strong acids can even cause burns on the skin and permanent damage of any part of the human body.

questions.question: Antacids are used  to get relief from acidity in the stomach because antacids are:
questions.explanation: Antacids are used to get relief from acidity in the stomach because antacids are basic in nature that neutralise the acids produced in the stomach. Examples of milk of magnesia, sodium bicarbonate.

questions.question: The reaction between acid and base to form salt and water is an example of:
questions.explanation: The reaction between acid and base to form salt and water is an example of neutralisation reaction. It involves a combination of H+ ions and OH- ions to generate water. The neutralization of strong acid and the strong base has a pH value equal to 7.

questions.question: Water-soluble bases are called ________.
questions.explanation: Water-soluble bases are called alkalise. All bases are not soluble in water. Sodium hydroxide is a water-soluble base. These bases have a pH greater than 7. Alkaline solutions are slippery and soapy to touch due to the saponification of fatty substances on the surface of the skin. Alkalis are usually water-soluble although some like barium carbonate are only soluble with an acidic aqueous solution.

questions.question: HCl + NaOH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} NaCl + H2O is an example of
questions.explanation: HCl + NaOH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} NaCl + H2O is an example of neutralisation reaction and exothermic reaction. Hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide react to form sodium chloride and water. In exothermic reaction heat is produced.

questions.question: Organic matter is added to soil to neutralise the excess of the base in the soil as organic matter.
questions.explanation: Excess of the base in the soil can be neutralised by adding organic matter in the soil as organic matter releases acid on decomposition in the soil to neutralise the soil. Organic matters also supply nutrients to soil thereby increasing soil fertility. It also helps in holding water in sandy soils and prevents water lodging in clayey soils.

questions.question: Hydrochloric acid is released in the stomach from gastric glands but it does not harm the stomach because:

options.option: Hydrochloric acid is neutralised by the base in stomach
Hydrochloric acid does not harm the skin

questions.explanation: The mucus layer present in the inner wall of the stomach prevents the hydrochloric acid from acting on the stomach. Mucus is also released along with hydrochloric acid from the gastric gland. Mucus is a slippery liquid made by mucous membrane. Glands in the stomach produce mucus.

questions.question: Distilled water is neutral, it can be verified by using:
questions.explanation: Distilled water is neither acidic nor basic; it can be verified by using a universal indicator that gives green colour to the neutral substance. On the pH scale, the distilled water has a pH value of 7. A substance that has a pH value of 7 is neutral.

questions.question: ________ solution is applied on the skin when an ant bites.
questions.explanation: Calamine is a basic salt solution applied on the skin to neutralise the acid released by ants when an ant bite. When an ant bites it injects formic acids into the skin. Calamine solution contains zinc carbonate which is a base, this solution neutralises the effect of acid of ant bite when applied on the skin.

questions.question: Which oxide reacts with water to form sulphuric acid?
questions.explanation: Sulphur trioxide reacts with water to form sulphuric acid.Sulphuric acid is a highly corrosive strong mineral acid with a molecular formula of H2SO4. It is pungent, colourless to a slightly yellow viscous liquid that is soluble in water at all concentrations.

questions.question: The base present in soap is/are:
questions.explanation: The base present in soap is either sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. Soaps are cleaning agents that are usually made by reacting alkali with naturally occurring fat or fatty acids.

questions.question: Which of the following is a natural indicator?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is a natural indicator as it turns bases red. Soap containing bases changes turmeric red. The colour of the turmeric remains the same in acidic solutions and changes to red in basic solutions.

questions.question: In neutralisation reaction
questions.explanation: In neutralisation reaction, heat is always evolved along with salt and water and an acid reacts with base.A neutralisation is a reaction when an acid and base react to form water and salt and invloves the combination of H+ ions and OH- ions to generate water.The neutralization of a strong acid and a strong base has a pH equal to 7.

questions.question: The reaction between acid and salt produces salt. Acid salt is produced when:

options.option: Strong acid react with a strong base
Weak acid reacts with a weak base
Weak acid reacts with a strong base
Strong acid reacts with a weak base

questions.explanation: The reaction between acid and salt produces salt. Acid salt is produced when strong acid reacts with a weak base. Acid salt is a class of salts formed by the partial neutralization of diprotic or polyprotic acids. As parent acid is only partially neutralized, one or more replaceable hydrogen atoms remain. Typically acid salts have one or more alkali and one or more hydrogen atoms. Soe examples are sodium bicarbonate, sodium bisulfate, etc.

questions.question: Which gas is released when an acid reacts with metals?
questions.explanation: When acids react with metals hydrogen gas is released along with metal salt. Hydrogen gas is a colourless, odourless gas. For example, sulphuric acid reacts with calcium to produce hydrogen gas along with it calcium sulphate(metal salt) is also produced in the reaction.

questions.question: Tooth decay is caused due to:

options.option: Decomposition of food particle to produce acid
Decomposition of food particle to produce base

questions.explanation: Tooth decay is caused due to decomposition of food particles left between teeth after eating to produce acid by the action of bacteria. The bacteria are present in our mouth in the form of a clear and sticky substance which is known as plague. Tooth decay is caused mainly because if oral hygiene is not maintained. It can be prevented by some regular good habits like brushing the teeth twice a day, rinsing the mouth with water after every eats.

questions.question: Blue litmus paper is dipped in a solution. It remains blue. The nature of the solution is:
questions.explanation: When blue litmus paper is dipped in a solution, it remains blue. This shows that solution is either basic or neutral. Since the paper did not change the colour to red it is not acidic.

questions.question: Sodium chloride turns:
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride is a salt that does not bring any change to litmus paper or another indicator. In the form of salt, it is commonly used as a condiment and food preservative.

questions.question: The acid responsible for the inheritance of traits from one generation to other is:
questions.explanation: Deoxyribose nucleic acid (DNA) is responsible for the inheritance of traits from one generation to another generation. It is present inside the nucleus of the cell .DNA is a molecule that carries the genetic instructions used in the growth, development, functioning, and reproduction of all known living organisms and many viruses.

questions.question: Acids solution are good conductor of electricity as:
questions.explanation: Acids solution is a good conductor of electricity as acids produce hydrogen ions in water to conduct the electric current. When an acid is dissolved in water, it losses the hydrogen ions and also losses a lot of anions. Ionic solutions are the god conductor of electricity. The stronger the acid the more hydrogen ions in the solution, the better the conductor.

questions.question: Which of the following is an acid-base indicator?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is an acid-base indicator. It is yellow in acidic solution and red in basic solution.

questions.question: Products of a neutralization reaction are always
questions.explanation: Salt and water are produced as a result of neutralisation reaction, e.g. NaOH + HCl {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} NaCl + H2O

questions.question: Litmus, a natural dye is an extract of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Litmus, a natural dye used as an indicator is extracted from lichen.

questions.question: Neutralisation reaction is a
questions.explanation: Neutralisation is a chemical and irreversible change. The salt and water formed are new products and cannot be converted back to acid and base.

questions.question: Phenolphthalein is a synthetic indicator and its colours in acidic and basic solutions, respectively are
questions.explanation: Phenolphthalein remains colourless in acidic solution and turns pink in basic solution.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:

  1. Both acids and bases change colour of all indicators.
  2. If an indicator gives a colour change with an acid, it does not give a change with a base.
  3. If an indicator changes colour with a base, it does not change colour with an acid.
  4. Change of colour in an acid and a base depends on the type of the indicator.

Which of these statements are correct?
questions.explanation: All indicators do not change colour with acids as well as bases, e.g. turmeric changes its colour to pink with the base but it remains yellow with acid. Litmus paper, methyl orange are some indicators which changes colour with both acids and bases.

questions.question: Whose precipitate is yellow?
questions.explanation: The precipitates of C6H5NO2 (Nitro Benzene), CHI3 (Iodoform) and C6H3N3O? (Picric acid) are yellow.

questions.question: Which salt can be hydrolysed?
questions.explanation: The salt of strong acid and strong base does not undergo hydrolysis. So, only CH3COOK (strong base + weak acid) will undergo hydrolysis in water and gives basic solution.

questions.question: The water solution having pH = 0, is
questions.explanation: Pure water is neutral. When an acid is dissolved in water, the pH will be less than 7 (25 °C). When a base, or alkali, is dissolved in water, the pH will be greater than 7. A solution of a strong acid, such as hydrochloric acid, at concentration of 1 mol dm-3 has a pH of 0.

questions.question: That compound whose aquous solution has highest pH?

questions.question: What is the chemical name of table salt?

questions.question: When a new substance is formed with different properties than the original substance the change is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Changes in which chemical properties of substance change and a new substance is formed are called chemical changes. Some of the chemical changes are irreversible. Even in the case of reversible changes in which chemical properties of substance change and a new substance is formed are called chemical changes.

questions.question: Which is an example of physical change?
questions.explanation: A physical change is any change in matter that involves the substance going from one physical state to another.  When a substance undergoes a physical change it does not lose its original properties when changing from one phase to another. Boiling of water is physical change because it is reverse but corrosion of metals, tarnishing of copper, and burning of paper is not a physical change.

questions.explanation: During physical changes composition of substance remains same while change in shape and size may takes place.

questions.question: During the chemical change:

options.option: Energy is either absorbed or given out

questions.explanation: Chemical changes involve heat changes during the reaction. It involves the absorption and release of heat.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting involves a change of state and no new substance is formed. Burning, rusting and corroding results in the formation of a new substance.

questions.question: Which among the following is a physical change?

options.option: Cutting a log of wood in small pieces

questions.explanation: In physical changes, the original matter can be recovered and no new product is formed. Cutting a log of wood in small pieces is a physical change because no new product can be formed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Chemical Change is a process in which the substance transforms into a new substance, having different chemical compositions. Chemical changes can be Irreversible and cannot be recovered. So, bursting of a fire cracker, germination of seed and coal formation from buried trees all are chemical changes.

questions.question: What is a method to prevent rust?
questions.explanation: For rusting, both water and oxygen should come in contact with iron. If anyone of these is prevented to come in contact with iron, rusting can be prevented by painting, applying of a layer of grease, galvanization, and Crystallisation.

questions.question: How are crystals of pure substances obtained?
questions.explanation: The formation of big and pure crystal of a substance from the saturated solution is called crystallisation.

questions.question: Iron pillar near the Qutub Minar in Delhi is famous for the following facts. Which of these facts is responsible for its long stability?

options.option: It was built more than 1600 years ago.
It has not rusted after such a long period.

questions.explanation: Iron pillar near Qutub Minar in Delhi is famous for its stability because it has not rusted over a long period.

questions.question: Two drops of dilute sulphuric acid were added to 1 g of copper sulphate powder and then small amount of hot water was added to dissolve it (step I). On cooling, beautiful blue coloured crystals got separated (step II). Step I and step II are:
questions.explanation: Dissolution of copper sulphate powder in water in presence of sulphuric acid is a physical change. On cooling this hot solution, the crystals of copper sulphate are separated. This is also a physical change.

questions.question: A chemical change may involve-
questions.explanation: A chemical change may involve change in colour, temperature, smell or evolution of gas, heat, etc.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a physical change?

  1. Rusting of iron
  2. Combustion of magnesium ribbon
  3. Burning of candle
  4. Melting of wax
questions.explanation: Melting of wax is a reversible change as melted wax can be changed into solid wax by cooling it down.

questions.question: When the physical status of reactant and catalyst are same, then the catalyst is called-
questions.explanation: When physical status of reactant and catalyst are same, then the catalyst is called homogeneous catalyst

questions.question: Dry ice is-
questions.explanation: Dry ice is the solid form of carbon dioxide (CO2). a molecule consisting of a single carbon atom bonded to two oxygen atoms. Dry ice is colorless, odorless, and nonflammable, and can lower the pH of a solution when dissolved in water, forming carbonic acid (H2CO3).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a property of solid?

options.option: The expansion of solid is very slow
The density of solid is high and compressibility is less
The solid are always of crystallised nature

questions.explanation: Like liquids, solids have high intermolecular forces that hold atoms and molecules in fixed positions. A crystalline solid is one in which the molecules or atoms are arranged in highly ordered repeating patterns. Some solids do not form crystals and do not behave as solids.

questions.question:
Two chemical species A and B make a product C by mutual combination, in which A & B both are present (A + B {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} C). A & B can’t be divided into simple matters by chemical reaction. Choose the correct statement for A, B and C.

  1. C is any compound
  2. A & B are compound
  3. A & B are element
  4. C is any definite combination
questions.explanation: A & B are elements and C is any compound of any definite combination.

questions.question: Two matter X and Y by reacting make a product P (X + Y {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} P). Relating to this a reaction, choose the correct statement from the written forward:

options.option: Product P will show the property of matter X & Y.

questions.explanation: Product P had always a definite composition.

questions.question: What the conversion of water into vapour is called?
questions.explanation: The evaporation of water is a physical change. When water evaporates, it changes from the liquid state to the gas state, but it is still water; it has not changed into any other substance. All changes of state are physical changes.

questions.question: When iron nail is dropped to the solution of blue vitriol, its colour changes to ________.
questions.explanation: If we drop an iron nail to blue vitriol and allow it to stand for 30 minutes, the blue colour changes to green.

questions.question: When acetic acid is mixed with baking soda, the gas evolved is ________.
questions.explanation: The reaction between acetic acid and baking soda generates carbon dioxide.

questions.question: On electrolysis of water, the gases produced are ________.
questions.explanation: hydrogen and oxygen

questions.question: A chemical reaction is characterised by ________.
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Rust is ________.
questions.explanation: Fe2O3

questions.question: Name the insoluble product formed when two compounds react in their aqueous solution state.
questions.explanation: Precipitate

questions.question: Which of the following is not a Combination reaction?
questions.explanation: Ca (OH)2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Ca O + H2O

questions.question: ________ is known as blue vitriol.
questions.explanation: Crystals of copper sulphate

questions.question: Take some vinegar in a test tube. Add a pinch of baking soda to it. Observe the reaction carefully. Select the CORRECT option with the help of the diagram.
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question:
Look at the following reaction and choose the correct statement.
Ca (OH)2 + CO {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CaCO3 + H2O

  1. CO2 turns lime water milky.
  2. White coloured insoluble calcium carbonate is formed.
  3. It is a physical change.
  4. Both CO2 turns lime water milky and White coloured insoluble calcium carbonate is formed.
questions.explanation: Statement (iv) is correct.

questions.question: ________ is not a physical property of a substance.
questions.explanation: Flammability

questions.question: Process by which salt is obtained by the evaporation of sea water.
questions.explanation: Crystallisation

questions.question: NaHCO3 is:
questions.explanation: Baking soda

questions.question: Sagging of electric wires in summers is because of:
questions.explanation: Expansion of metals on heating.

questions.question: Which coloured light is produced when magnesium ribbon burns?
questions.explanation: Brilliant white light

questions.question: ________ is produced when anaerobic bacteria digest animal waste.
questions.explanation: Biogas

questions.question: Washing soda is:
questions.explanation: Sodium carbonate

questions.question: A chemical reaction showing single displacement reaction is:

options.option: Both Na Cl + Ag NO3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Ag Cl + Na NO3 and H2SO4 + 2Na OH {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Na2SO4 + 2H2O
Na Cl + Ag NO3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Ag Cl + Na NO3

questions.explanation: Ag NO3 + Cu {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Cu(NO3)2 + 2Ag

questions.question: Rate of rusting of iron will be more in:
questions.explanation: Coastal areas

questions.question: Malleability is a property by which:

options.option: Both Metals can be burnt and Metals can be stretched into wires.
Metal can be converted into thin sheets.

questions.explanation: Metal can be converted into thin sheets.

questions.question: Effervescence is:
questions.explanation: Bubbling in liquid

questions.question: Brown colour of the apple after cutting is ________.
questions.explanation: chemical change

questions.question:
Which of the following chemical reactions is endothermic?

  1. N2 + O2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2NO
  2. C + 2S {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CS2
  3. C + O2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CO2
  4. Both (i) and (ii)
questions.explanation: Statement (iv) is correct

questions.question: Reducing agent is that which:

options.option: Can oxidizes other substances by removing hydrogen.
Can reduce other substances by removing oxygen.
Can oxidizes other substances by providing oxygen to them.

questions.explanation: Can reduce other substances by removing oxygen.

questions.question: A substance which is burned to produce heat and light is known as ________.
questions.explanation: fuel

questions.question: Which is not a unit of measuring temperature?
questions.explanation: Pascal

questions.question: If freezing point of ice is 32° F then find the freezing point of ice in Kelvin?

Two friends Raman and Raghu measured the body temperature of Yashika. According to Raman it was 39°C and according to Raghu it was 102.2°F. If the body temperature of Yashika on Kelvin scale is 312.15 then who is correct among the following?
questions.explanation: Both Raman and Raghu are correct.

questions.question: An example of physical change is ________.
questions.explanation: Changing of water into steam only changes water from liquid to gaseous state with no change of its chemical structure. Tearing of paper is a physical change because there is no change in the substance when we tear the paper and glowing of bulb is a physical change because when electric current is passed through the bulb, the filament starts glowing and the glow stops as soon as we stop the current.

questions.question: Boiling point of water is:
Temperature of a boiling solution in the kitchen can be measured by using:
questions.explanation: Lab Thermometer

questions.question: Rising of mercury in the thermometer is due to:
questions.explanation: Expansion of mercury on heating.

questions.question: In the Process of convection:
questions.explanation: Transfer of molecules occur.

questions.question: An example of chemical change is ________.
questions.explanation: Burning of LPG is a chemical change because on burning, LPG forms Co2 and H2O.

questions.question: Kink in a clinical thermometer:

options.option: Is the main unit of measuring temperature.
Prevents the mercury from flowing back into the bulb just after measuring temperature.

questions.explanation: Prevents the mercury from flowing back into the bulb just after measuring temperature.

questions.question:
Match the Column I with the Column II and select the CORRECT option.

Column-I Column-II
a. Burning of magnesium i. Reversible change
b. Rusting of iron ii. Irreversible change
c. {tex}\mathrm{Solid}\overset{\mathrm{heat}}{\underset{\mathrm{cool}}\rightleftharpoons}\mathrm{vapour}{/tex} iii. Chemical change
d. Ripening of fruit iv. Moist air

options.option: a - iv, b - iii, c - i, d - ii
a - i, b - ii, c - iii, d - iv
a - iii, b - iv, c - i, d - ii
a - ii, b - iv, c - iii, d - i

questions.explanation: a - iii, b - iv, c - i, d - ii

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a physical change?
questions.explanation: Digestion of food

questions.question: Which one of the following is essential for rusting of iron?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: 405o Celsius is equal to ________ Fahrenheit.
questions.explanation: 761o

questions.question: During the dissolution of urea, heat
questions.explanation: is absorbed

questions.question:
Match the following:

Column A Column B
(i) Solids (a) Doctor’s thermometer
(ii) Gases (b) Radiation
(iii) Vacuum (c) Conduction
(iv) Kink (d) Convection
Ice is covered with a jute cloth to slow the process of melting because:

options.option: It is a poor conductor of heat.
Ice becomes light when covered with jute.
Ice can be wrapped in it easily .

questions.explanation: It is a poor conductor of heat.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is true when milk changes into curd?

  1. Its state is changed from liquid to semi-solid
  2. It changes colour
  3. It changes taste
  4. The change cannot be reversed

options.option: Both Its state is changed from liquid to semi-solid and It changes colour
Its state is changed from liquid to semi-solid, It changes taste and The change cannot be reversed
only Its state is changed from liquid to semi-solid

questions.explanation: Its state is changed from liquid to semi-solid, It changes taste and The change cannot be reversed

questions.question: A way of transferring heat from hot body to cold body which do not require any material medium is:
questions.explanation: Radiation

questions.question:
Which one of the following is a chemical change?
Twinkling of stars, Cooking of vegetables, Cutting of fruits, Boiling of water
questions.explanation: Cooking of vegetables

questions.question: Molecules of matter on heating:
questions.explanation: Move faster

questions.question: Some utensils of stainless steel are provided with copper bottoms because:

options.option: Both Copper is easy to clean and It looks good.
Copper is a better conductor of heat than stainless steel.

questions.explanation: Copper is a better conductor of heat than stainless steel.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is a physical change?
 Rusting of iron, Combustion of magnesium ribbon, Crumpling a sheet, Mixing acid and base
questions.explanation: Crumpling a sheet

questions.question: Convert -150o Celsius to Kelvin.
Higher kinetic energy of the molecules of the body is observed when the body is at ________ .
questions.explanation: Higher temperature

questions.question: One litre of water at 25oC is mixed with one litre of water at 35oC. The temperature of the mixture will be:
questions.explanation: Between 25oC and 35oC

questions.question: Method of heat transfer by which heat is transferred from sun to earth is:
questions.explanation: Radiation

questions.question: When a ribbon of magnesium is burnt in the air, magnesium oxide is formed. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: Chemical change

questions.question: Original substances participating in a chemical reaction are ________ and the new substances formed are known as the ________ .
questions.explanation: Reactants, products

questions.question: Gas evolving when acetic acid gets reacted with baking soda.
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide

questions.question: Burning of candle is a:
questions.explanation: Both Physical Change and Chemical Change

questions.question:
An iron rod is heated till it becomes red-hot.

Is heating of the iron rod a chemical change?
Select the correct response with explanation.

options.option: Yes, because heat is absorbed by the iron rod.
Yes, because the iron rod turns red when heated.
No, because the rod does not change its state.
No, because no new substance is formed.

questions.explanation: No, because no new substance is formed.

questions.question: Which of these is a physical change?

options.option: A butterly larva changing into a cocoon

questions.explanation: A lizard changing its skin colour

questions.question: Which of these can never happen in a physical change?
questions.explanation: Change in the composition of matter

questions.question: Which among the following is not a physical change?
questions.explanation: Decay of matter

questions.question: An example of a chemical change is ________.
questions.explanation: dropping sodium into water

questions.question: Which among the following is not a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice

questions.question: Which of these will cause a chemical change to occur?
questions.explanation: Lighting of a gas stove

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical change ?
questions.explanation: Solubility in water

questions.question: The sign used to indicate a reversible reaction is ________.
questions.explanation: {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex}

questions.question: Conversion of {tex}\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}{/tex} into CaO as per following reaction is an example of CaCO3 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex}CaO + CO2
questions.explanation: decomposition reaction

questions.explanation: {tex}\mathrm{FeCl}_{3}+3 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{OH})_{3}+3 \mathrm{NaCl}{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following is a nature of physical changes?
questions.explanation: Reversible

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Fruit ripening

questions.question: Which of the following can change the arrangement of molecules in a substance?
questions.explanation: Heat

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an example of a physical change?
questions.explanation: Reaction of baking soda with vinegar

questions.question: How many elements in the periodic table are naturally occurring?
HNO3 is chemical formula of which acid?
questions.explanation: Nitric acid

questions.question: Hydrogen (H2) and oxygen (O2) react to form water (H2O), is which type of reaction?
questions.explanation: Combination reaction

questions.question: Which reaction is the opposite of synthesis reaction?
questions.explanation: Decomposition reaction

questions.explanation: HCl
