chapters.name: Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate (Deleted)

questions.question: Which one is not an adaptation of camel for the desert condition:

options.option: It perspires a lot to keep the body cool
Its feet are broad with paddy soles
It excretes very little urine and dry dung.

questions.explanation: Camel perspires very less due to the absence of sweat glands. It releases thick urine have broad soles to make their walk on sand easy without digging into it.

questions.question: The maximum and minimum temperature displayed daily in the weather bulletin refer to the

options.option: highest day temperature and highest of the month.
average highest temperature of day and average lowest temperature of night.
temperature recorded at 12 noon and at mid night (00.00 hrs).
highest dat temperature and lowest night temperature of the day.

questions.explanation: highest dat temperature and lowest night temperature of the day.

questions.question: Out of the given definitions, which is the most appropriate definition of climate?

options.option: Change in weather patter in a few years.
Change in weather conditions in a years.

questions.explanation: Average weather pattern of many years.

questions.question: A long winter sleep of animals is called ________.
questions.explanation: Some animals undergo long winter sleep to avoid cold conditions, called hibernation. Organisms survive without taking food and water. Some animals which go into hibernation are polar bears, seals, etc.

questions.question: Wet and dry bulb hygrometer is an instrument which measures:

options.option: The amount of humidity in the atmosphere
Rainfall in a given period of time.

questions.explanation: Wet and dry bulb hygrometer is an instrument that measures the amount of humidity in the atmosphere. Humidity refers to the amount of water vapour present in the air

questions.question: Weather-related to atmospheric condition is of how much duration?
questions.explanation: Weather-related to atmospheric condition is for 24 hours duration as weather changes in a short time interval.

questions.question: Which of the following organism is the slowest mover?
questions.explanation:
Sloth is the slowest mover out of the following organisms.
Sloths are arboreal animals( animals living on trees) found in the tropical rainforest of Central and South America. They are so slow to move that they go to the ground to urinate only once a week

questions.question:
Given below are some adaptive features of animals:

  1. Layer of fat under the skin
  2. Long, curved and sharp claws
  3. Slippery body
  4. Thick white fur

Which of them are the adaptive features of a polar bear?
questions.explanation: (i), (ii) and (iv) only

questions.question: A carnivore with stripe on its body moves very fast while catching its prey. It is likely to be found in:
questions.explanation: tropical rainforests

questions.question: In western Ghats of India, the Long tailed macaque is also known as
questions.explanation: Beard Ape

questions.question: Birds migrate to:
questions.explanation: Birds migrate from one place to another to avoid the extreme climatic condition of a particular place. Some migratory birds are Siberian Crane, Arctic Tern

questions.question: Tropical rainforest support a large number of plants and animals because of:
questions.explanation: The tropical rainforest supports a large number of plants and animals because of continuous warmth and rain throughout the year.

questions.question: Long Inactivity and metabolic depression of animals during winters is known as:
questions.explanation: hibernation

questions.question: Toucan has long and strong beaks to:
questions.explanation: Toucan birds have long and strong beaks to reach the fruits on weak branches.

questions.question: Migratory birds may be guided by:

options.option: Sun during day and star during the night

questions.explanation: Some migratory birds travel very long distances. These birds may be guided by Sun during day and star during night.

questions.question: Aquatic organisms have:

options.option: Oily body and scale on the body

questions.explanation: Aquatic organisms have a streamlined body and webbed feet to move swiftly in water. They respire by gills.

questions.question: The large ears of the elephant help them to:

options.option: Hear loud sound the remove the insects
Hear the soft sound and cool the body

questions.explanation: The large ears of the elephant help them to hear the soft sounds and cool the body by waving it continuously.

questions.question: Which is not a cold-blooded animal?
questions.explanation: Frog, fish, and Snakes can change their body temperature with environmental temperature so, cold-blooded but birds are warm-blooded.

questions.question: Climate is the::

options.option: Average rain pattern over a long period of time
The average weather pattern over a long period of time
The average weather pattern over a short period of time
The average temperature over a long period of time

questions.explanation: Climate is the average weather pattern over a long period of time that remains the same for many years.

questions.question: A place that receives very little rainfall and the temperature is high throughout the year, the climate of this place is:
questions.explanation: Hot and dry climate can be experienced in the desert as deserts receive minimum rainfall due to which very few plants grow there.

questions.question: Two regions of the earth with the extreme climatic condition are:
questions.explanation: Tropical regions and Polar Regions are extreme climatic conditions. Tropical regions having extremely hot and polar regions having the extreme cold conditions are opposite to each other.

questions.question: The type of climate in Kerala is:
questions.explanation: The climate of Kerala is hot and wet as rainfall occurs frequently throughout the year.

questions.question: The tropical rain forest has a large population due to:
questions.explanation: The tropical rainforest has a large population due to continuous warmth and heavy rain that support organisms for food.

questions.question: We find animals of particular type living in a particular habitat because:
questions.explanation:
The answer is they have adapted to that habitat
Animals of a particular type are found in a particular habitat as they have adaptive features to adjust to the particular type of environment. For example, polar bears have a thick layer of fat beneath their skin to keep them warm in polar regions, camels have a hump in their back to store water in the desert

questions.question: Why do coastal areas experience less contrast in weather condition?
questions.explanation: Coastal areas experience less contrast in weather condition due to moderating effect of sea as land and sea breeze.

questions.question: Polar bears are good swimmers they go on swimming to:

options.option: Remove the ice attached on the body
Keep the body cool in hotter condition and hunt down fishes

questions.explanation: Polar bears are so well insulated as they contain a thick layer of fat beneath their skin so they tend to overheat. So to keep them cool due to overheating and hunt down fishes they go on swimming.

questions.question: What is the role of the Meteorological Department of India?

options.option: To predict the nature of animals in region
To collect data on temperature, humidity, and rainfall

questions.explanation: The meteorological department of India collects data on temperature, humidity, and rainfall to predict weather conditions.

questions.question: Many tropical animals blend their skin colour with surroundings. This feature is called
questions.explanation: Camouflage is a way of hiding that allows an  animal to blend in with its environment to make them  go unnoticed by predators.

questions.question: Three animals living in the polar region includes:
questions.explanation: Polar bears, Arctic foxes, and penguins have the required adaptation like a thick layer of fat beneath their skin, thick fur, and white skin to camouflage in the snow to live in the polar region.

questions.question: Many tropical animals blend their skin colour with their surroundings. This feature is called ________.
questions.explanation:
The answer is camouflage
Some tropical region animals change their colour with the surrounding to avoid being noticed by the predators, one example of such camouflage is Chameleon

questions.question: Long trunk, large ears and modified tooth are adaptive features of
questions.explanation: Elephants have large ears, long trunk and modified tooth as adaptive feature to survive in tropical regions.

questions.question: Thick fat deposits below the skin is useful in polar region to
questions.explanation: The thick fat deposits below the skin are useful in polar region to prevent the heat loss from the body as fat acts as insulator.

questions.question: The seal is found in:
questions.explanation: The seal is found in the polar region that contains blubber beneath the skin to avoid the cold condition

questions.question: Amount of moisture in air is called ________.
questions.explanation: Humidity is the content of moisture in the air. The higher the amount of moisture content the more humid the climate will be and there will be difficulty in evaporation of sweat and drying of clothes.

questions.question: Daily weather reports carry information about:

options.option: About the temperature, humidity, and rainfall of the last 72 hours
About the temperature, humidity, and rainfall of the next 72 hours
About the temperature, humidity, and rainfall of the last 24 hours
About the temperature, humidity, and rainfall of the next 48 hours

questions.explanation: These reports are made by the Meteorological department which briefly describes the temperature, humidity (amount of water vapour) and rainfall of last 24 hours.

questions.question: ________ is basically a measuring cylinder with a funnel on the top to collect rainwater.
questions.explanation: Rain gauge is used to measure rainfall over a particular interval of time. It is basically a measuring cylinder with a funnel on the top to collect rainwater.

questions.question: An animal found in the desert.
questions.explanation: Camel is found in deserts because it can survive for many days without water and easily walk on sand.

questions.question: Which of the following is a complex phenomenon that can vary in a very short period of time?
questions.explanation: Weather is a very complex process phenomenon that can vary in a very short period of time. Weather changes frequently so it is measured over a period of 24 hours

questions.question: In India tropical rainforests are located in
questions.explanation: In India tropical rainforests are located in Western Ghats. It covers Kerala, Tamilnadu, Karnataka, Goa etc.

questions.question: Which of these provide camouflage to Polar bear?
questions.explanation: White fur on the body of polar bear camouflage with the snow of polar region.

questions.question: Why do we wear woollen clothes in winter?
questions.explanation: We wear woollen clothes in winter to ward off cold as air trapped in woollen clothes act as an insulator to prevent heat loss.

questions.question: Thick fat deposits below the skin are useful in polar region to:

options.option: Provide energy in the absence of food

questions.explanation: The thick fat deposits below the skin are useful in the polar region to prevent the heat loss from the body as fat acts as an insulator.

questions.question: If there is heavy rainfall on most of the days at a place, the climate of that place will be:
questions.explanation: If there is heavy rainfall on most of the days at a place, the climate of that place will be hot and wet as more the heat means more evaporation and hence heavy rainfall

questions.question: Penguins huddle together to:
questions.explanation: Penguins huddle together to keep themselves warm as heat lost by one penguin is used by others to heat up.

questions.question: The polar bear has a layer of fat under its skin to:
questions.explanation: The polar bear has layer of fat under its skin to insulate its body from cold condition as fat acts as an insulator to prevent heat loss from the body.

questions.question: Red-eyed frogs have developed sticky pad to:
questions.explanation: Red-eyed frogs have developed sticky pad to climb up a tree easily.

questions.question: Which of these is shown by humidity?
questions.explanation: The presence of moisture is shown by humidity. Higher the moisture content in air more will be humidity level.

questions.question: Which layer present on the earth's atmosphere protects us from harmful UV rays of Sun
questions.explanation:
The answer is Ozone
The ozone layer or ozone shield is a region of Earth's atmosphere that absorbs most of the Sun's ultraviolet (UV) radiation and protects us from the harmful UV rays which can cause skin cancer.

questions.question: Why do coastal areas experience less contrast in weather conditions?

options.option: Due to the moderating effect of sea

questions.explanation: Coastal areas experience less contrast in weather conditions due to the moderating effect of the sea as land and sea breeze.

questions.question: What is a mango shower?
questions.explanation: The pre-monsoon rainfall in Karnataka and Kerala is commonly known as mango shower. It is also called as April rain.

questions.question: Weather changes:
questions.explanation: The day-to-day condition of the atmosphere at a place with respect to the temperature, humidity, rainfall, wind-speed, etc. is called the weather at that place. The weather is generally not the same on any two days, two weeks, or even months. The maximum temperature of the day occurs generally in the afternoon while the minimum temperature occurs early in the morning.

questions.question: Rainfall is measured by:
questions.explanation: Rainfall is measured by an instrument called the rain gauge.It is basically a measuring cylinder with a funnel on top to collect rainwater.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an adaptive feature in polar bears?
questions.explanation: Animals in the polar region is adapted to the extremely cold climate by having some special characteristics such as a white fur, strong sense of smell, a layer of fat under the skin, wide and large paws swimming and walking, etc. The long tail is not an adaptive feature in the polar bears.

questions.question: The average weather pattern taken over a long time is called the climate of the place. Here long time means:
questions.explanation: The average weather pattern taken over a long time, say 25 years, called the climate of the place.

questions.question: Which of the following part of India has a hot and dry climate?
questions.explanation: In Rajasthan typical desert climate is found. Rajasthan's has a hot and dry climate.

questions.question: Which of the following is the climate found in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Kerala is very hot and wet. Kerala has a coastal state that has a hot and humid climate during April-May and pleasant, cold climate in December-January.

questions.question: Which of the following countries have tropical rainforests?

questions.question: Which part of the earth receives the maximum amount of solar energy?

questions.question: What is the name given to wind blowing from sea to land?
questions.explanation: The wind blows from the sea towards the land along the pressure gradient in an attempt to equalize pressure. This is known as a sea breeze.

questions.question: When cold water is poured over the hot tin can, it gets distorted due to:

options.option: The pressure inside the can is equal to the atmospheric pressure
High pressure outside and low pressure inside
Pressure outside the can is same as that inside
High pressure inside and low pressure out side

questions.explanation: When boiled water is filled in a tin can and its mouth is closed with the lid; followed by cold water being poured over it, the can gets distorted. This happens because the air inside the can gets condensed and creates low pressure. The pressure of the air inside can become lower than the air outside, the can’s shape will become distorted.

questions.question: It is difficult to ride a bicycle against the direction of the wind because:
questions.explanation: The pressure exerted by the air on all bodies at all times in all directions is called air pressure. When air moves at high speeds, it creates a low-pressure area. So,  It is difficult to ride a bicycle against the direction of the wind because air exerts pressure on it. Wind opposes the motion of the bicycle.

questions.question: Which of the following is not due to pressure change?
questions.explanation: Thunderstorm, cyclone, and lightning occur due to pressure change in two places but earthquake occurs due to movement in plates deep inside the earth crust.

questions.question: Wind speed of 170 Km is called ________.
questions.explanation: Wind blowing at a speed of 170 km/h is called a typhoon. It is very destructive in nature due to high kinetic energy.

questions.question: In a cyclone, wind speed is about:
questions.explanation: In cyclone, wind speed is about 200-250 Km/h having very high kinetic energy to brings huge destruction of life and property.

questions.question: If a storm is accompanied by lightning, we should not take shelter under an isolated high tree because.

options.option: It has more chance of falling lightning

questions.explanation: Thunder is the result of lighting. Lighting is a result of electrical charges. Lighting is attracted to the tallest tip of a conductor. Trees are often the tallest objects within an environment. Therefore, trees make great targets for lighting. However, trees do not contain as many ions as humans do. Thus, humans are better conductors than trees. If the tip of a tree is struck by lighting and a person is standing under the tree, then the current from the lighting may travel down the tree and jump towards the human conductor. Therefore, it is not wise to stand under a tree during a thunderstorm.

questions.question: Lightening is heard little earlier than thunder sound because:
questions.explanation: Lightening is heard little earlier than thunder sound because light travels much faster than sound. So, light takes less time to reach us than sound.

questions.question: Which effect is created by high-speed winds?
questions.explanation: High-speed wind creates an area of low air pressure: Thatched roof is blown off because of high-speed wind. This happens because high-speed wind creates an area of low pressure. Due to this, the thatched roof is sucked up and finally gets blown away. Thus, an increase in wind speed is associated with a decrease in air pressure.

questions.question: The main cause of wind movement is the:
questions.explanation: The main cause of wind movement is the uneven heating of earth to create a high and low-pressure area.

questions.question: Cyclone activities are monitored by using:
questions.explanation: Cyclone activities are monitored by using radar and satellite systems to observe the position, direction, and speed of the wind.

questions.question: A cyclone warning is issued:
questions.explanation: Cyclone occurs due to the formation of low-pressure area, it can be predicted about the position and timing of cyclone in 24 hours advance.

questions.question: A balloon when taken near candle  burst because:
questions.explanation: A candle's flame produces heat. When a balloon is taken near a candle, the heat causes the air molecules in the balloon to vibrate, or take more place. The air inside balloons expands. Hence the balloon's size increases. When the point till it can stretch exceeds, the balloon bursts.

questions.question: Which of the following place is unlikely to be affected by cyclone?
questions.explanation: The cyclone occurs near the coastal area. As Amritsar is far away from the coastal areas, it is unlikely to be affected by cyclones.

questions.question: In case of anticyclone in southern hemisphere wind spirals:
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere, while the pressure gradient force acts in the direction form high pressure to low pressure, the Coriolis force now causes the deflection to be directed to the left path of motion. Thus a cyclone in the Southern Hemisphere is characterized by a clockwise flow of air that spiral inward with rising air at the center. While an anticyclone exhibits a counterclockwise flow that spirals outward with descending air in the centre.

questions.question: ________ occurs at night near ocean.
questions.explanation: Sea breeze occurs at night near the ocean. At night wind blows from sea to land called sea breeze to maintain the temperature.

questions.question: Cyclone does not affect the:
questions.explanation: Cyclones can be highly destructive. The effects of cyclones include heavy rains and strong winds. Cyclones can destroy buildings, vehicles, bridges, agriculture fields, telecommunication systems, electricity supply, etc. People living near coastal areas get affected during cyclones. Cyclones do not affect the climate or weather.

questions.question: The eye of storm is a
questions.explanation: The eye of a hurricane is an area of mostly calm weather at the centre of the storm. It is roughly circular in shape and usually between 20 and 40 miles in diameter.

questions.question: We fill the air to bicycle tube to:
questions.explanation: We fill the air to bicycle tubes to keep it tight as air exerts pressure on the tube that is helpful in smooth riding. The air in the bicycle tube keeps it in shape. A bicycle tube overfilled with air may burst.

questions.question: The wind is generated due to:
questions.explanation: Wind currents occur due to uneven heating between the regions near the equator and the poles and the rotation of the earth.

questions.question: The eye of storm is a ________.
questions.explanation: The eye of a hurricane is an area of mostly calm weather at the centre of the storm. It is roughly circular in shape and usually between 20 and 40 miles in diameter.

questions.question: The monsoon comes to India from which direction?
questions.explanation: Monsoon winds are seasonal winds that blow from the Indian Ocean to the Indian subcontinent during summers. In summers, the land close to the equator becomes very hot as compared to the ocean. The air over the land gets heated and rises. The cooler wind from over the ocean blows towards the land. These are called monsoon winds. They are rain-bearing winds as they carry water. They are also known as summer monsoon winds. These winds are generated from the southwest direction in summers.

questions.question: What is the effect of greater pressure difference on wind speed?
questions.explanation: Wind speed is caused by air moving from high pressure to low pressure, usually due to changes in temperature.

questions.question: What makes the balloon inflated when placed in hot water?
questions.explanation: We know that air expands and gets lighter when heated. So when the air inside the bottle is heated, it expands and gets lighter, and tries to escape out of the bottle through the mouth of the bottle. But instead, the balloon attached to the bottle's mouth gets filled up with the escaping air, and so it gets inflated. As the air expands it exerts pressure on the walls of the balloon and causes inflation. So, Temperature also affects the atmospheric pressure as it causes the air to change its density. Higher the temperature, the higher the pressure.

questions.question: What is the speed of a violent tornado?
questions.explanation: Tornadoes are the very fast-moving dark cloud in the shape of a funnel. The speed of violent tornadoes may be up to 300km/h. Tornadoes are as destructive as a cyclone. Tornadoes may form within cyclones.

questions.question: Weathering is the:

options.option: No change in weather for longer time

questions.explanation: Weathering is the breaking down of rocks, soil, and minerals as well as wood and artificial materials through contact with the Earth’s atmosphere, biota, and waters.

questions.question: Change in seasons  is caused due to:
questions.explanation: During the year, the seasons change depending on the amount of sunlight reaching the Earth as it revolves around the Sun.

questions.question: There is a difference in the climate of northern and southern hemisphere due to:

options.option: Rotation of earth on its own axis
Revolution of earth on its own axis
Revolution of the earth around the sun

questions.explanation: The earth's axis of rotation is 23.4 degrees in relation to its orbital plane. The axis is fixed, constantly pointing in the same direction regardless of the movement of the planet. The tilt causes different amounts of sunlight exposure to the southern or northern hemisphere dependent on where the earth is in its orbit around the sun. The seasons experienced by the northern and southern hemisphere always differ by six months – when it is summer in the northern hemisphere, it is winter in the southern hemisphere, and so on.

questions.question: The sudden appearance of light in the sky is called ________.
questions.explanation: Lightning is a bright flash of electricity produced by a thunderstorm. All thunderstorms produce lightning and are very dangerous. If you hear the sound of thunder, then you are in danger from lightning. Lightning kills and injures people and properties.

questions.question: Lightning is heard little earlier than thunder because:
questions.explanation: The speed of sound in air is 344m/s. Whereas the speed of light is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108 therefore speed of light is greater than that of sound so light reaches faster than sound. So, Lightning is heard a little earlier than thunder.

questions.question: Which economic system is based upon the market forces of demand and supply?
questions.explanation: Capitalism is an economic system and an ideology based on private ownership of the means of production and their operation for profit. Characteristics central to capitalism include private property, capital accumulation, wage labor, voluntary exchange, a price system, and competitive markets.

questions.question: Air moves from:

options.option: The region of low pressure to high pressure
The region of high pressure to low pressure

questions.explanation: When air moves, it is called wind. Air moves from the region where the air pressure is high to the region where the pressure is low. The greater the difference in pressure, the faster the air moves.

questions.question: What is a cyclone?

options.option: Very low pressure system with very high speed winds revolving around it.
The dark funnel-shaped cloud that reaches from the sky to the ground.
Very High pressure system with very less speed winds revolving around it.

questions.explanation: Before cloud formation, water takes up heat from the atmosphere to change into vapour. When water vapour changes back to liquid form as raindrops, this heat is released to the atmosphere. The heat released to the atmosphere warms the air around. The air tends to rise and causes a drop in pressure. More air rushes to the centre of the storm. This cycle is repeated. The chain of events ends with the formation of a very low-pressure system with very high-speed winds revolving around it. It is this weather condition that we call a cyclone.

questions.question: Which one of the following place is unlikely to be affected by a cyclone?

questions.question: In which year was Orissa hit by a cyclone in the recent past?
questions.explanation: On 29th October 1999, the Super Cyclone, also known as Paradip Cyclone, hit Odisha with wind speeds of up to 250 kmph. It caused the deaths of about 10,000 people, and heavy to extreme damage in its path of destruction.

questions.question: Leaves of trees, banner or flags flutter when the wind is blowing. It is due to which property of air?

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the force of atmospheric pressure on a blimp or an inflated balloon used for advertisement?
questions.explanation: Presses on all sides

questions.question: The density of the atmosphere
questions.explanation: decreases with increasing altitude.

questions.question: Mercury is a convenient liquid to use in a barometer because
questions.explanation: it has a high density.

questions.question: Which location on Earth receives the most solar radiation in any given year?
questions.explanation: The tropics

questions.question: Uneven heating of Earth's surface directly leads to
questions.explanation: convection

questions.question: What type of breeze is it when air moves from the land behind you toward the ocean in front of you?
questions.explanation: Land breeze

questions.question: Which describes the deflection of wind currents due to Earth's rotation?
questions.explanation: Coriolis effect

questions.question: A thunderstorm usually does not develop under what conditions?
questions.explanation: Cold air

questions.question: Which would work against the development of a thunderstorm?
questions.explanation: Stable air

questions.question: How does a cyclone form?

options.option: Warm air rises over cool ocean water and becomes less dense. Surrounding air begins to spin anticlockwise direction.
Cool air rises over warm ocean water and becomes more dense. Surrounding air begins to spin in a clockwise direction.
Cool air rises over cool ocean water and becomes more dense. Surrounding air begins to spin in a clockwise direction.
Warm air rises over warm ocean water and becomes less dense. Surrounding air begins to spin in an anticlockwise direction.

questions.explanation: Warm air rises over warm ocean water and becomes less dense. Surrounding air begins to spin in an anticlockwise direction.

questions.question: What happens as air has weight?
questions.explanation: Air pushes objects.

questions.question: What results from differences in air pressure?
questions.explanation: Wind

questions.question: An anemometer is used to measure
questions.explanation: wind speed

questions.question: The uppermost fertile layer of earth crest is called ________.
questions.explanation: The upper fertile layer of earth crest is called soil. Soil is the crust part of the earth. It is mainly the mixture of rock particles and humus.

questions.question: Humus and the smallest particles of rock form the
questions.explanation: C-horizon

questions.question: In addition to the rock particles, the soil contains
questions.explanation: When dry soil is crushed in the hand, it can be seen that it is composed of all kinds of particles of different sizes. Most of these particles originate from the degradation of rocks; they are called mineral particles. Some originate from residues of plants or animals these are called organic particles. The soil particles seem to touch each other, but in reality have spaces in between. These spaces are called pores. The large soil pores are filled with both air and water.

questions.question: Which layer is formed of decayed organic matter?
questions.explanation: The decayed organic matter in the soil forms humus which is black in colour. Soil contains the rotting dead matter called humus. Fragrant, spongy, nutrient-rich material resulting from the decomposition of organic matter.

questions.question: Soil erosion is a process of:
questions.explanation: Removal of topsoil by weathering agents; such as wind, water, and ice is known as soil erosion. Soil erosion affects the fertility of the soil, as humus present in the soil is eroded along with the soil. It is important to prevent soil erosion.

questions.question: Which of these do not cause weathering?
questions.explanation: freezing of rainwater

questions.question: Breaking down of big pieces of rock into smaller pieces is called ________.
questions.explanation: The process of breaking down of rocks by the action of wind, water, and climate is called weathering. Soil is formed by weathering.

questions.question: Water percolating into the soil collects over.
questions.explanation: C-horizon

questions.question: Soil best suited for the cultivation of wheat, rice, and sugarcane is ________.
questions.explanation: Alluvial soil is found in the northern plains of India. Alluvial soil is rich in potash but poor in potassium and nitrogen. Nevertheless, it is a very fertile soil, which can support a wide variety of crops such as rice, wheat, cotton, jute, and sugarcane.

questions.question: Red soil In which horizon of soil weathering is taking place?
questions.explanation: The third layer is the C-horizon, which is made up of small lumps of rocks with cracks and crevices. Below this layer is the bedrock, which is hard and difficult to dig with a spade. water can be held in the tiny gaps. The weathering of soil takes place in the C-horizon of the soil profile.

questions.question: Which one is not a natural force that causes the weathering of rock?
questions.explanation: The weathering of rock takes place by the action of wind, water, and Glacier. Earthquake is not a natural force to cause weathering.

questions.question: Clayey particles are of a size:
questions.explanation: The finest particles of the soil are called clay are smaller than 0.02 mm. A clay layer won’t let pass water. Pure clay particles are smooth and stick to your fingers.

questions.question: Waterlogging can be expected in soil which is rich in
questions.explanation: humus

questions.question: Collective name of layers of soil is ________.
questions.explanation: The different layers of soil are together called soil profile. The term soil profile represents the vertical section of the earth crust, which is made up of a succession of horizontal layers, each of which varies in thickness, colour, texture, structure, consistency, porosity, acidity, and composition.

questions.question: The water holding capacity is the highest in
questions.explanation: Particles of sandy soil are larger in size. Because of being larger in size, sand particles cannot fit close together and hence there is enough space among them. These spaces are filled with air. Water drains quickly through sandy soil. So, sandy soil is called well aerated, light and dry.

questions.question: Availability of water and minerals in the soil for maximum absorption by roots is in the
questions.explanation: A-horizon

questions.question: Soil conservation measures are mainly aimed at protecting which of the following?
questions.explanation: Top soil

questions.question: What is the best soil texture in terms of water availability for plants?
questions.explanation: Loamy soil has the right water holding capacity and is well aerated. This is considered as the best soil for the growth of plants.

questions.question: Usually, fertile soil will have:
questions.explanation: Fertile soil will have a low colloid content as colloids increase the transparency of water. Colloid material is the agency through which credit may be effectively restored to the soil. This is possible because of the great holding power of the soil colloids for mineral constituents. When plant foods are added as fertilizers only art of water-soluble material added may be taken up immediately by the crop.

questions.question: Fungi and bacteria grow mostly in which layer of soil?
questions.explanation: Fungi and bacteria are decomposers. Decomposers are mostly found in top soil.

questions.question: The usual vertical sequence of horizons in soil from the surface downward are
questions.explanation: Layers of soil are referred to as horizons. Horizons of soil are categorized into four types: A-horizon, B-horizon, C-horizon, and Bedrock. A-horizon is the top, B-horizon is second, C- the horizon is third and D-horizon is the last one.

questions.question: Which kind of particle is present in the deepest layer in the soil profile?
questions.explanation: The bottom-most layer in the soil profile is called bedrock. This is far more solid in composition than the other layers and is very hard. It is difficult to dig up this layer even with a spade. Under the bedrock, it is found very hard material.

questions.question: The type of soil suitable for growing Masoor dal is ________.
questions.explanation: Loamy soil is considered the best for almost all types of crops. It is suitable for lentils and other pulses. So loamy soil is suitable for growing Masoor dal.

questions.question: Which of the following definitions describe the soil-forming process of leaching?

options.option: The transport of clay particles forms the B horizons to the E horizons.
Seeping of water dissolved soil materials and moving deeper soil
The addition of material to the soil body.
Surface erosion carries sediment away from the upper most layer of soil.

questions.explanation: Leaching, in geology, loss of soluble substances and colloids from the top layer of soil by percolating precipitation. The materials lost are carried downward and are generally redeposited in a lower layer. This transport results in a porous and open-top layer and a dense, compact lower layer. The rate of leaching increases with the amount of rainfall, high temperatures, and the removal of protective vegetation.

questions.question: The soil used for making matka’s and surahi’s is:
questions.explanation: Particles of loamy soil are smaller than sand and larger than clay. Loamy soil is a mixture of sandy soil, clayey soil, and silt. Silt is the deposit in river beds. So loamy soil is used for making matkas and surahis.

questions.question: A Which of the following worms live in the soil?
questions.explanation: Earthworms live in the soil. They are called tillers of the soil.

questions.question: Which of these soils are rich in nutrients and makes the soil fertile?
questions.explanation: Humus provides nutrients and makes the soil fertile.

questions.question: Percolation rate of water is highest in which of the following soils?
questions.explanation: Percolation rate of water is highest in sandy soil.

questions.question: ----------------- is the process in which soil is washed out by rain water.
questions.explanation: Soil erosion is partially caused by rain runoff washing away the soil. “Runoff” refers to the water that flows over soil’s surface. It occurs when the soil is saturated or unable to absorb more water.

questions.question: Distinctive horizontal layers that differ in physical composition, chemical composition, and organic content are called ________.
questions.explanation: The term soil profile represents the vertical section of the earth's crust, which is made up of a succession of horizontal layers, each of which varies in thickness, colour, texture, structure, consistency, porosity, acidity, and composition. The distinctive horizontal layers that differ in physical composition, chemical composition, and organic content are called a soil profile.

questions.question: Which soil is suitable for growing cereals like wheat and gram?
questions.explanation: Clayey and loamy soils are suitable for growing cereals.

questions.question: What processes slowly destroys irrigated soils in arid regions:
questions.explanation: Salinization has a direct effect on both plant growth and the structure of the soil. If the soil is saline a plant will have to expend energy bringing water into its cells because it is forced to work against osmotic potential. So Salinizationslowly destroys irrigated soils in arid regions.

questions.question: ________ are black in colour and have a high clay content in which deep vertical cracks develops during the dry season.
questions.explanation: Mollisols are the most productive soils of the earth. They are rich in humus content. They have a dark-colored surfaces. Mollisols are soft, even when dry, with granular pads, loosely arranged when dry. These humus-rich organic soils are high in basic cations and have high fertility.

questions.question: ________ are soils in which more than half of the parent mineral matter is volcanic ash and appear very dark in colour.
questions.explanation: Volcanic soils are soils in which more than half of the parent mineral matter is volcanic ash and appear very dark in colour. They are often very rich in nutrients and hold water well because of their volcanic ash content. These soils are called Andisols, and they are often very young, and acidic depending on which type of volcano they come from.

questions.question: The major factors that affect soil development are:
questions.explanation: Soil is formed by weathering. Weathering is the process of breaking down of rocks by the action of wind, water, and climate. The formation of even one inch of soil takes many years. So, the major factors that affect soil development are climate, vegetation, and time.

questions.question: Which of the following crops are grown in loamy soil?
questions.explanation: Cereals, pulses and vegetables are grown in loamy soils.

questions.question: Hygroscopic water is the:

options.option: Water which ponds up on the soil surface
Water held as a tight film around soil particles
Water which is available to drain through the due to gravity
Water which represents the majority available for plant uptake

questions.explanation: Hygroscopic water is the water forms very thin films around soil particles and is not available to the plant. The water is held so tightly by the soil that it can not be taken up by roots. not held in the pores, but on the particle surface.

questions.question: Soil acidity is measured by the concentration of which cations?
questions.explanation: Soil acidity is measured in pH units. Soil pH is a measure of the concentration of hydrogen ions in the soil solution. The lower the pH of the soil, the greater the acidity.

questions.question: Coffee cultivation is best suited in:
questions.explanation: Laterite Soil is found in areas of India with heavy rainfall, particularly near the coasts. It is an acidic soil and is rich in iron, which gives the soil a somewhat red appearance. It is used to grow more tropical crops such as cashew, rubber, coconut, tea, and coffee.

questions.question: Humus makes the soil:
questions.explanation: The decaying dead matters in the soil are called humus. Humus makes the soil fertile and provides nutrients to the plants.

questions.question: From which elements are clay elements are formed?
questions.explanation: The elements necessary for good soil are dissolved and carried to the oceans. The soil in such a climate is made up of a simple clay that contains hydrogen, aluminum, silicon, and oxygen. This soil is red because of iron oxide.

questions.question: How many essential elements are required for the normal growth of green plants?
questions.explanation: Sixteen plant food nutrients are essential for proper crop development. Each is equally important to the plant, yet each is required in vastly different amounts. This sixteen nutrients are carbon (C), nitrogen (N),calcium (Ca),boron (B),hydrogen (H), phosphorus (P), magnesium (Mg), chlorine (Cl), oxygen (O), potassium (K), sulfur (S), copper (Cu), iron (Fe), manganese (Mn), molybdenum (Mo) and zinc (Zn).

questions.question: Why are cations attracted by clay minerals?

options.option: Because no other minerals bound with cations
Because clay minerals are the largest particle

questions.explanation: The clay then carries a negative electrical charge per formula unit equal to that carried on one electron. Chagres on clay minerals is always balanced by ions on the crystal surface. The most common of these ions are aluminium, calcium, magnesium, potassium, and hydrogen and they are interchangeable, being held by electrostatic forces between the negative clay charge and positive ion charge and positive ion charge. Because of electrostatic attraction cations are attracted by clay minerals.

questions.question: Why is it important to apply nitrogen fertilisers at an appropriate time of the year?

options.option: Fertiliser require a threshold level of soil water
Fertiliser is more expensive at a particular time
During winter, nitrate more easily leached in soil.

questions.explanation: Nitrogen from urea can be lost to the atmosphere if fertilizer urea remains on the soil surface for extended periods of time during warm weather. Fertiliser application should be timed to achieve maximum plant uptake, thereby reducing losses of nutrients to the environment. So, it is important to apply nitrogen fertilisers at an appropriate time of the year.

questions.question: C-horizon of soil profile consists of:

options.option: Large lumps of rocks with cracks and crevices
Small lumps of rock with cracks and crevices

questions.explanation: The third layer of soil is known as C-horizon. C-horizon is made of small lumps of rocks with cracks and crevices.

questions.question: ------------- is hard and difficult to dig with a spade.
questions.explanation: The bottom-most layer in the soil profile is called bedrock.     This is far more solid in composition than the other layers and is very hard.  It is difficult to dig up this layer even with a spade. Under the bedrock it is found very hard material.

questions.question: In which of the following climates weathering takes most rapid?
questions.explanation: Weathering is the disintegration and decomposition of rock at or near the surface of the earth or simply the breaking of rocks. Weathering occurs fastest in hot, wet climates. It occurs very slowly in hot and dry climates. Without temperature changes, ice wedging cannot occur. In very cold, dry areas, there is little weathering.

questions.question: Humus: decomposition of organic matter:: soil :?
questions.explanation: Humus is formed by the decomposition of organic matter and soil is formed by weathering of rocks.

questions.question: Soil can be classified as:
questions.explanation: Size of particles of soil is very important for the properties of soil. So, soils are categorized mainly in three types based on the proportion of size of particles. These are Sandy Soil, Clayey Soil and Loamy Soil.

questions.question: Uppermost layer of soil profile is ________.
questions.explanation: Top layer of soil is called A-horizon. It is also called top-soil. A-horizon is generally dark in colour. It is rich in humus. A horizon is generally soft and porous. It retains more water.

questions.question: Percolation rate is the:

options.option: The volume of water absorbed by the soil
The volume of water passing through soil per unit time

questions.explanation: The amount of water drained through the water in unit time is known as the percolation rate of water in the soil. Percolation rate of water can be calculated using the formula given here. Percolation rate (mL/min) = {tex}Amount \ of \ water \ in \ mL \over Time \ taken \ to \ percolate{/tex}. The percolation rate of water in sandy soil is fastest and in clayey soil is slowest.

questions.question: Which soil is made from basaltic rock?
questions.explanation: Black soil is formed by the weathering of basaltic lava rocks. These are mostly found in volcanic regions. Basalt is a dark-colored, fine-grained, igneous rock composed mainly of plagioclase and pyroxene minerals.

questions.question: A different layer of soil having a characteristic feature is called:
questions.explanation: The term soil profile represents the vertical section of the earth crust, which is made up of a succession of horizontal layers, each of which varies in thickness, colour, texture, structure, consistency, porosity, acidity  and composition.

questions.question: The upper layer of soil is dark coloured due to presence of ________.
questions.explanation: The uppermost horizon is called topsoil or the A horizon. It is dark in colour due to the presence of humus and minerals. This layer is fertile because of the presence of humus.

questions.question: ________ is the process in which soil is washed out by rainwater.
questions.explanation: Soil erosion is partially caused by rain runoff washing away the soil. “Runoff” refers to the water that flows over the soil’s surface. It occurs when the soil is saturated or unable to absorb more water.

questions.question: Root of the plant are present in ________.
questions.explanation: The top layer of soil is called A-horizon. It is also called top-soil. A-horizon is generally dark in colour. It is rich in humus. A horizon is generally soft and porous. It retains more water. Roots of small plants are entirely embedded in topsoil.

questions.question: If the soil contains a greater proportion of big particles the soil is called ________.
questions.explanation: Particles of sandy soil are larger in size. Because of being larger in size, sand particles cannot fit close together, and hence there is enough space among them.

questions.question: The soil used for making matkas and surahs is:
questions.explanation: Particles of loamy soil are smaller than sand and larger than clay. Loamy soil is the mixture of sandy soil, clayey soil and silt. Silt is the deposit in river beds. So loamy soil is used for making matkas and surahis.

questions.question: Partially decomposed vegetative matter present in the soil is called ________.
questions.explanation: Soil contains the rotting dead matter called humus. Fragrant, spongy, nutrient-rich material resulting from the decomposition of organic matter.

questions.question: Best suited soil for lenticels and other pulses is:
questions.explanation: Loamy soil has the right water holding capacity and is well aerated. This is considered as the best soil for the growth of plants. So best suited soil for lenticels and other pulses is Loamy soil.

questions.question: The relative proportion of sand, silt, and clay in soil refers to its ________.
questions.explanation: Soil texture refers to the relative proportion of particles or it is the relative percentage by weight of the three soil separates viz., sand, silt, and clay or simply refers to the size of soil particles.

questions.question: Distinctive horizontal layers that differ in physical composition, chemical composition, organic content are called:
questions.explanation: The term soil profile represents the vertical section of the earth crust, which is made up of a succession of horizontal layers, each of which varies in thickness, colour, texture, structure, consistency, porosity, acidity, and composition. So Distinctive horizontal layers that differ in physical composition, chemical composition, organic content are called a soil profile.

questions.question: The display of horizons on a vertical cross-section through the soil is termed as:
questions.explanation: The term soil profile represents the vertical section of earth crust, which is made up of a succession of horizontal layers, each of which varies in thickness, colour, texture, structure, consistency, porosity, acidity  and composition.

questions.question: Which of the following is the mixture of decomposed organic matter and is usually dark in colour?
questions.explanation: Soil contains the rotting dead matter called humus. Fragrant, spongy, nutrient-rich material resulting from the decomposition of organic matter. Black soil gets its color from various salts or from humus.

questions.question: Which of the following soil type is loosely packed with large air spaces?
questions.explanation: Sandy soil

questions.question: Which type of soil is well aerated?
questions.explanation: Particles of sandy soil are larger in size. Because of being larger in size, sand particles cannot fit close together and hence there is enough space among them. These spaces are filled with air. Water drains quickly through sandy soil. So, sandy soil is called well aerated, light and dry.

questions.question: Removal and transport of soil by water and wind is called ________.
questions.explanation: Soil erosion is the displacement of the upper layer of soil, one form of soil degradation. A low level of erosion of soil is a naturally occurring process on all land. The agents of soil erosion are water and wind, each contributing a significant amount of soil loss each year.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a step in the formation of soil?

options.option: All are involved in the formation of soil

questions.explanation: Soil is formed by weathering. Weathering is the process of breaking down of rocks by the action of wind, water and climate. Addition of humus, Earthquakes or volcanic eruptions all are involved in the formation of soil.

questions.question: Minerals are found in which horizon of soil?
questions.explanation: The uppermost layer of soil is called topsoil or A-horizon. It is soft, porous, and holds more water than the other layers. It is rich in humus and minerals.

questions.question: ________ is hard and difficult to dig with a spade.
questions.explanation: The bottom-most layer in the soil profile is called bedrock. This is far more solid in composition than the other layers and is very hard. It is difficult to dig up this layer even with a spade. Under the bedrock, it is found very hard material.

questions.question: Which of the following type of soil will form a colloidal solution when suspended in water?
questions.explanation: Property of colloidal solutions are components that cannot be separated by ordinary filtration. And it is translucent. When Clay soil forms a colloidal solution when suspended in water then it can be separated by ordinary filtration and it is translucent.

questions.question: Which factor influences soil formation?
questions.explanation:
Some of the important factors responsible for soil formation in India are as follows:

  1. Parent Material
  2. Relief
  3. Climate
  4. Natural Vegetation

The parent material determines the colouration of the soil, its mineral composition, and texture.
The most important climatic factors affecting soil formation are the amount and seasonal distribution of temperature and rainfall.
The formation and development of soil is very much influenced by the growth of vegetation.

questions.question: Which of the following does not cause erosion of soil?
questions.explanation: Deforestation is one of the major factors responsible for Soil erosion. Removal of forest cover which functions as a binder of the top layer of the soil with increasing land demand has resulted in enhancing extent of soil erosion. Overgrazing is also responsible for soil erosion. Fragmentation of rocks does not cause erosion of soil.

questions.question: The soil in the ground has a particle size of more than 3 mm. What type of soil is it?
questions.explanation:
Clay - particles less than 0.002 mm in diameter.
Fine sand - particles between 0.2 mm and 0.02 mm in diameter.
Silt - particles between 0.02 mm and 0.002 mm in diameter.
Gravel - particles greater than 3 mm in diameter

questions.question: Which one of the following does not add nutrient to the soil?
questions.explanation: Ploughing is a method to loosen the soil before sowing seed. It brings the soil that is rich in nutrients to the top from layers below, but does not add any nutrients of the soil.

questions.question: Soil profile consists of mainly
questions.explanation: three layers

questions.question: Parent rock material is present in
questions.explanation: Bed rock

questions.question: Humus is present in
questions.explanation: A-horizon

questions.question: Soil fertility is enriched by
questions.explanation: certain plants like legumes

questions.question: Soil best suited for plant growth is
questions.explanation: loamy

questions.question: Plants prevent

options.option: the loosening of soil and they bind the soil with the help of their roots

questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Sometimes when we do heavy exercise, anaerobic respiration takes place in our muscle cells. What is produced during this process?
questions.explanation: Lactic acid only

questions.question: Breathing is a:
questions.explanation: Breathing is a physical process and respiration is a chemical process. Breathing is a process of taking oxygen into the lungs while respiration is taking the oxygen from the lungs into the bloodstream or to the cells.

questions.question: Yeast is used in wine and beer industries because it repries
questions.explanation: anaerobically producing alcohol.

questions.question: Inhalation is the:
questions.explanation: The process of taking in of air rich in oxygen into the body is called ‘inhalation’. During inhalation, ribs move up and outwards and the diaphragm moves down.

questions.question: The opening of the pharynx to the larynx is guarded by:
questions.explanation: The larynx is a tubular chamber supported by a cartilaginous framework. Opening of pharynx into larynx is guarded by a cartilaginous leaf-like flap called epiglottis which covers his opening during food swallowing.

questions.question: In plants, the exchange of gases takes place through:
questions.explanation: Leaves of plants have tiny pores called stomata, which is used for the exchange of gases. The oxygen, taken in through stomata is used by the cells in the leaves to break down glucose into carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: Fish breathe with the help of gills which are richly supplied with blood vessels. The gills help the fist to
questions.explanation: take in oxygen dissolved in water.

questions.question: Haemoglobin is present in:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin, contained in the red cells of the blood and constituting the main site of iron in the body, is present in all vertebrate species.

questions.question: Instant source of energy is ________.
questions.explanation: Glucose is a simple sugar. Carbohydrates, when consumed, get digested into glucose. As glucose can be easily absorbed in the blood, it provides instant energy to the body. Hence, when glucose is taken directly, it does not have to be digested and thus acts as an instant source of energy.

questions.question: In cockroaches, air enters the body through
questions.explanation: spiracles

questions.question: The amount of volume of air that can be inspired/expired normally is called:
questions.explanation: Tidal volume is the lung volume representing the normal volume of air displaced between normal inhalation and exhalation when extra effort is not applied. In a healthy, young human adult, tidal volume is approximately 500 mL per inspiration or 7 mL/kg of body mass.

questions.question: How many molecules of oxygen are carried by one molecule of haemoglobin?
questions.explanation: Each haemoglobin molecule can combine with four oxygen molecules, but with no more. The complete combination is called oxygen saturation.

questions.question: From nasal cavity, air reaches to lungs through:
questions.explanation: Air first enters your body through your nose or mouth, which wets and warms the air. (Cold, dry air can irritate your lungs.) The air then travels through your voice box and down your windpipe. The windpipe splits into two bronchial tubes that enter your lungs.

questions.question: During inhalation, ribs move up and diaphragm moves:
questions.explanation: The process of taking in of air rich in oxygen into the body is called ‘inhalation’. During inhalation, ribs move up and outwards and the diaphragm moves downward.

questions.question: Breathing involves:
questions.explanation: We take in air through our nostrils, when we inhale air, it passes through our nostrils into the nasal cavity and from there it reaches our lungs through the windpipe. Lungs are present in the chest cavity which is surrounded by ribs on the sides; diaphragm forms the floor of the chest cavity. Breathing involves the movement of the diaphragm and the rib cage.

questions.question: Smoking damage the lungs and may cause:
questions.explanation: Tobacco smoke is the number one risk factor for lung cancer - and every time it is inhaled, it deals a double blow to lung cells, creating conditions almost ideally suited to the formation of cancer.

questions.question: Insects have a network of air tube for exchange of gases called ________.
questions.explanation: Cockroach and other insects breathe through spiracles and tracheae. There is a network of hollow tubes running through the body of an insect. These hollow tubes are called trachea.

questions.question: Inspiration is the process of:
questions.explanation: Respiration is the term for the exchange of oxygen from the environment for carbon dioxide from the body's cells. The process of taking in air rich in oxygen is called inhalation or inspiration. Similarly, the process of giving out air rich in carbon dioxide is called exhalation or expiration.

questions.question: Respiratory organs of plants include:
questions.explanation: During respiration in plants, very little transport of gases takes place from one plant part to another. Therefore, each plant part takes care of its own energy needs, or in other words, its gas exchange need. Roots, stems and leaves of plants exchange gases for respiration separately. . Leaves of plants have tiny pores called stomata, which is used for the exchange of gases. If stems are woody gaseous exchange is carried out by lenticels. Root of plant respire by root hair.

questions.question: What is the percentage of oxygen in inhaled air?
questions.explanation: The air in our environment has 21% oxygen. It is the same, environmental air that goes into the lungs during inspiration.

questions.question: Oxidation of food inside the cell takes place in
questions.explanation: Respiration happens inside the cells. A cell organelle called mitochondria is the site of cellular respiration.

questions.question: The root of plants takes air from air space present between:
questions.explanation: The oxygen present in between the soil particles diffuse into the root hairs. From root hairs, oxygen is supplied to all the parts of roots for respiration. During respiration, oxygen is converted into carbon dioxide gas which is diffused out of the roots by the same root hairs.

questions.question: During exhalation, the ribs
questions.explanation:
During inhalation the lungs inflate. Therefore to creates space the ribs moves upward and outward whereas the diaphragm moves downwards.
During exhalation the lungs deflate. Therefore the ribs moves to their original position by moving downward and inward whereas the diaphragm moves upwards.

questions.question: Respiration is essential for the survival of the organism because it:

options.option: Increase carbon dioxide gas in the air

questions.explanation: Respiration is the biochemical process through which all the cells of living organisms get the energy to perform certain life processes that are essential for survival.

questions.question: During respiration, breakdown of food takes place:
questions.explanation: Cellular respiration takes place in the cells of all organisms. In the cell, the food (glucose) is broken down into carbon dioxide and water using oxygen. When the breakdown of glucose occurs with the use of oxygen it is called aerobic respiration. Food can also be broken down, without using oxygen.

questions.question: Human being: aerobic respiration:: Yeast :?
questions.explanation: In the cell, the food (glucose) is broken down into carbon dioxide and water using oxygen. When the breakdown of glucose occurs with the use of oxygen it is called aerobic respiration. Food can also be broken down, without using oxygen. This is called anaerobic respiration. Breakdown of food releases energy. Human respires aerobically. Yeast is a single-celled organism that respires anaerobically and during this process yield alcohol.

questions.question: Respiratory organs and the process of breathing is not the same as human beings in:
questions.explanation: Respiration in cockroach and other insects is not the same as the respiratory system in humans. Animals like birds, cows, elephants, frogs, lions, lizards, snakes, including humans, have lungs as their primary respiratory organ. In these animals, lungs perform the function of exchanging air with the aid of nostrils, nasal cavity, and the windpipe. However, insects and worms do not have lungs to perform their respiratory function.

questions.question: Which of the following organism breathe through the skin?
questions.explanation: Earthworm breathes through their skins, the skin of an earthworm feels moist and slimy on touching and gases can pass easily through them.

questions.question: Skeletal structure surrounding the chest cavity is called ________.
questions.explanation: Ribs are joined with the backbone to form a box. This box is called a rib cage. Heart and Lungs protected by the rib cage. So the Skeletal structure surrounding the chest cavity is called rib cage.

questions.question: Respiratory pigment in human being is called ________.
questions.explanation: The respiratory pigment in people is hemoglobin. Its principle capacity is to transport oxygen to different parts of the body. At the point when the blood goes through the lungs, hemoglobin takes up oxygen from the lungs and tie to it. One particle of hemoglobin ties to four atoms of oxygen. Oxygen-hemoglobin complex is then transported to different parts of the body and oxygen is discharged into the tissues.

questions.question: The mountaineers carry oxygen with them because :

options.option: The pressure of air is higher than that on the ground.
At an altitude of more than 5 km there is no air.
The temperature of air is higher than that on the ground.
The amount of air available to a person is less than that available on the ground.

questions.explanation: At sea level, the atmospheric pressure is greater than the pressure in the lungs, and oxygen molecules can diffuse more easily into the bloodstream. Air pressure decreases with increase in altitude. The amount of oxygen that is available or that can diffuse easily into the bloodstream decreases. So mountaineers carry oxygen with them.

questions.question: The two-layered membrane that encloses the lung is called ________.
questions.explanation: The lungs are surrounded by a thin double-layered membrane known as the Pleural membrane. The outer layer, called the Parietal Pleura, is the thoracic part of the same membrane that coats the inner wall of all body cavities. The inner part, called the Visceral Pleura, in particular to the lungs and is in contact with the lung surface.

questions.question: More energy is released in:
questions.explanation: In anaerobic respiration we use oxygen to break down the food. Due to the breaking down of food, we get energy stored in it. But due to the unavailability of oxygen in anaerobic respiration, breaking down of food does not take place and we get very less energy.

questions.question: The chemical used to test the carbon dioxide gas in exhaled air is ________.
questions.explanation: Limewater will turn cloudy milky when a certain amount of carbon dioxide is passed through it. It is a specific test for carbon dioxide.

questions.question: The minute pores present on the surface of leaves are called ________.
questions.explanation: Leaves of plants have tiny pores called stomata, which is used for the exchange of gases. The oxygen, taken in through stomata is used by the cells in the leaves to break down glucose into carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: During respiration glucose is ________.
questions.explanation: Respiration is an oxidation reaction. Aerobic respiration breaks down glucose. During aerobic respiration, oxygen gets reduced by donating an electron to hydrogen forming water. In this process glucose gets oxidised to produce carbon dioxide, water and energy.

questions.question: In anaerobic respiration which chemical substance is produced?
questions.explanation: Anaerobic respiration is a type of respiration that does not use oxygen. Alcoholic fermentation is used in the brewing industry for the production of various types of beers, whiskey, and other wines.

questions.question: In higher plants, gaseous exchange takes place through:
questions.explanation: In lower plants (and also protozoans) exchange of gases takes place through the general body surface as they are not highly modified or specialised. Also, the body surface allows the diffusion of gases. In higher plants, the exchange of gases takes place through stomata.

questions.question: Production of alcohol by yeast is called ________.
questions.explanation: Fermentation is a metabolic process that consumes the sugar in the absence of oxygen. The products are organic acids, gases, or alcohol. It occurs in yeast and bacteria, and also in oxygen-starved muscle cells, as in the case of lactic acid fermentation

questions.question: Lime water turns milky in presence of:
questions.explanation:
When CO2 is passed through lime water, it reacts with it and changes the colour of the lime water milky due to the formation of calcium carbonate.
Calcium hydroxide (lime water) + carbon dioxide {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} calcium carbonate + water

questions.question: Size of the thoracic cavity is changed due to action of:
questions.explanation: The thoracic diaphragm, or simply the diaphragm is a sheet of internal skeletal muscle that extends across the bottom of the thoracic cavity. The diaphragm separates the thoracic cavity, containing the heart and lungs, from the abdominal cavity and performs an important function in respiration: as the diaphragm contracts, the volume of the thoracic cavity increases and air is drawn into the lungs.

questions.question: The number of ATP molecules produced during aerobic respiration is ________.
questions.explanation: 38 ATP molecules can be made per oxidised glucose molecule during cellular respiration (2 from glycolysis, 2 from the Krebs cycle, and about 34 from the electron transport system).

questions.question: The product of Glycolysis is ________.
questions.explanation: Pyruvic acid is formed as an end product of glycolysis, a process that breaks down glucose (a six-carbon molecule) into two molecules of pyruvate (a three-carbon molecule) and simultaneously yields a small net gain of the universal energy storage molecule adenosine triphosphate (ATP), used to power cellular function.

questions.question: The process complementary to respiration is ________.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis and cellular respiration are complementary processes by which living things obtain needed substances. They both consume and create the same substances (water, glucose, oxygen, and carbon dioxide) but in different ways.

questions.question: The respiratory surface should be:

options.option: Thin, permeable and richly supplied with blood
Thin-walled, moist and devoid of blood vessels
Thick, impermeable and richly supplied with blood

questions.explanation: For effective respiration, the skin of animals is thin, moist, highly permeable, and rich of supplied with blood capillaries that are present immediately below the cuticle. The air sac walls are very thin so that gases can quickly diffuse through them. The air sacs are moist with mucus so that gases can dissolve before diffusing. The surface area for gases to diffuse through in the human lungs is roughly the same as a tennis court. The air sacs have a large capillary network so that large volumes of gases can be exchanged.

questions.question: Respiration in addition to producing energy also carries out the function of:

options.option: Producing the major part of body heat

questions.explanation: Respiration in addition to producing energy also carries out the function of producing the major part of body heat. Carbon dioxide is a waste product of respiration and is formed from carbon and oxygen which were originally part of the food molecules. Heat energy may be released from cells during respiration.

questions.question: The expired air differs from inspired air in:

options.option: Having more oxygen and less carbon dioxide
Having more carbon dioxide and less oxygen

questions.explanation: The air that we inspire is a mixture of gases. The most important of these are nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and water vapour. The air that we expire is not the same. In exhaled air, carbon dioxide percentage is ten times higher than atmospheric CO2 and oxygen is 16% instead of 21% which we find in the atmosphere.

questions.question: The metallic element present in haemoglobin is ________.
questions.explanation: Each heme group contains an iron atom that is able to bind to one oxygen (O2) molecule. Therefore, each hemoglobin protein can bind four oxygen molecules.

questions.question: Normal range of breathing rate per minute in an average adult person at rest is:
questions.explanation: On an average, an adult human being at rest  breathes in and out 15-18 times in a minutes. During heavy exercise, the breathing rate can increase upto 25 times per minute.

questions.question: What is the raw material for the cellular respiration?
questions.explanation: Glucose from the food that organisms consume gets broken down into simpler substances and energy is released. Since respiration takes place in the cells of organisms, it is called cellular respiration.

questions.question: What are the gases involved in breathing?
questions.explanation: Breathing or External Respiration involves the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide. Oxygen is taken from the environment and carbon dioxide and water vapour are given out.

questions.question: Name the organ of the body in which the blood is oxygenated.
questions.explanation: In the lungs, oxygen is put into the blood and carbon dioxide is taken out of the blood during the process of breathing. After the blood gets oxygen in the lungs, it is called oxygenated blood.

questions.question: Breathing rate in human beings in normal condition is:
questions.explanation: On an average, an adult human being at rest  breathes in and out 15-18 times in a minutes. During heavy exercise, the breathing rate can increase upto 25 times per minute.

questions.question: The mountaineers carry oxygen with them because:

options.option: At an altitude of more than 5 km there is no air.
The temperature of air is higher than that on the ground.
The amount of air available to a person is less than that available on the ground.

questions.explanation:
The mountaineers carry oxygen with them because:

  1. At an altitude of more than 5 km, there is no air.
  2. The amount of air available to a person is less than that available on the ground.
  3. The temperature of the air is higher than that on the ground.
  4. The pressure of air is higher than that on the ground.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms can do anaerobic respiration?
questions.explanation: Yeasts are single-celled organisms. They respire anaerobically and during this process yield alcohol. They are, therefore, used to make wine and beer.

questions.question: What are the end products of aerobic respiration?
questions.explanation: When the breakdown of glucose occurs with the use of oxygen, it is called aerobic respiration. During this process, the food molecules are combined with oxygen and get oxidised inside the cell. They are then broken down into carbon dioxide and water and energy are released.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, we get cramps in the legs due to the accumulation of
questions.explanation: After strenuous exercise muscles experience a lack of oxygen. In the absence or insufficiency of oxygen, they respire anaerobically thereby producing lactic acid. The accumulation of lactic acid causes cramps in muscles.

questions.question: Yeast is used in:
questions.explanation: Yeasts are single-celled organisms. They respire anaerobically and during this process yield alcohol. They are, therefore, used to make wine and beer.

questions.question: Earthworms and frogs breathe through their skin because of which the skin of both the organisms is
questions.explanation: The skin of the earthworm and the frog feels moist and slimy on touching. It is so because, in earthworms and frogs, the exchange of gases takes place by diffusion through the moist body surface.

questions.question: By which organ, the respiration in frog happens?
questions.explanation: The frog has three respiratory surfaces on its body that it uses to exchange gas with the surroundings: the skin, in the lungs and on the lining of the mouth. While completely submerged all of the frog’s respiration takes place through the skin.

questions.question: The apparatus for measuring the rate of breathing is-
questions.explanation: A respirometer is a device used to measure the rate of respiration of a living organism by measuring its rate of exchange of oxygen and/or carbon dioxide. They allow investigation into how factors such as age or chemicals affect the rate of respiration.

questions.question: In plants, air enters through ________.
questions.explanation: stomata

questions.question: Breathing rate per minute in human beings under normal conditions is ________.
questions.explanation: The normal respiration rate for an adult at rest is 12 to 20 breaths per minute.

questions.question: Frog (on land) breathes through the ________.
questions.explanation: lungs

questions.question: Glucose is oxidised to ethyl alcohol during the process of
questions.explanation: Anaerobic respiration

questions.question:
Match the column I with the column II and select the CORRECT option.

Column I Column II
a. Respiration i. Oxygen
b. Earthworm ii. Skin
c. Bronchioles iii. Alveoli
d. Roots iv. Epiblema

options.option: a - i, b - ii, c - iii, d - iv
a - iii, b - i, c - iv, d - ii
a - ii, b - iii, c - iv, d - i
a - iv, b - iii, c - ii, d - i

questions.explanation: a - i, b - ii, c - iii, d - iv

questions.question: Which one of the following respires only anaerobically?
questions.explanation: Yeast

questions.question: A person puts warm water in an aquarium, thinking that the fish would be more comfortable in it. However, the fish died. What can be the reason for this?

options.option: Fish can bear the extreme heat of the water.
The concentration of oxygen decreases in warm water.
The concentration of carbon dioxide increases in warm water.
Fish cannot bear the extreme heat of the water.

questions.explanation: The concentration of oxygen decreases in warm water.

questions.question: Blood is red coloured due to presence of ________.
questions.explanation: haemoglobin

questions.question: Breathing is a ________ process while respiration is a ________ process.
questions.explanation: physical, chemical

questions.question:
Find the odd one out.
Water, Fossil fuels, Plants, Animals
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels

questions.question: What is the function of the hair in the nose?

options.option: To prevent germs from entering the lungs

questions.explanation: The hair present in the nose trap the dust participles and prevent them from entering into the trachea.

questions.question: Gas P diffuses from a capillary into an alveolus in the lungs. When is gas P expelled out from the alveolus?
questions.explanation: During exhalation

questions.question: Which of the following diagram CORRECTLY illustrates the passage of oxygen in the respiratory system?
questions.explanation: Nose {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Wind pipe {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Lungs {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Blood

questions.question:
Which organism is described in the information given below?

  1. Respire anaerobically
  2. Used to make wine
  3. Reproduce by budding
questions.explanation: Yeast

questions.question:
