questions.question: The change in position with respect to the time and surroundings is called ________.
questions.explanation: The change in position of a body with respect to time and its surrounding is called its motion. Motion is the opposite of rest. It is the transfer or movement of an object from one place to another.

questions.question: S.I unit of speed is ________.
questions.explanation:
S.I unit of speed is meter per second (m/s) which is equal to the distance travelled in meters in one second(time).
Speed = distance / time ,S.I unit of distance is meter and time is second so the S.I unit of speed is meters per second

questions.question: One hour is equal to:
questions.explanation:
One hour = 60 minutes (or) 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 minnute but one minute = 60 seconds.
Therefore, one hour = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = 3600 seconds.

questions.question: Which is the most accurate watch?
questions.explanation: The atomic watch is the most accurate watch. It is used for extremely accurate timekeeping. They use the vibrations of atoms to keep time.

questions.question: S.I unit of time is ________.
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of time is second. Time is the gap between the two events. The other units being minute, hour, day, year, century and Millenium.

questions.question: Which of the following instrument measure time with the most accuracy?
questions.explanation: quartz clock

questions.question: The rate of change in position of a body is called ________.
questions.explanation:
The rate of change in the position of a body is called speed, which is the distance covered by a body in unit time.
Speed = {tex}distance \over time{/tex}
S.I unit of speed is meter per second.

questions.question: The small metallic ball in a simple pendulum is called ________.
questions.explanation: Most of the simple pendulum consists of a small metallic ball called a Bob hung on a string or a wire. The time period of the pendulum does not change with the change in size or weight of the Bob.

questions.question: The stopwatch is used to measure:
questions.explanation: The stopwatch is used to measure the exact time during an event like race. It is mostly used to measure the time between two events.

questions.question: The pendulum of a given length always takes:
questions.explanation: The pendulum of a given length always takes equal time to complete one oscillation. It is called a time period of the pendulum. The time period of a simple pendulum of a fixed length remains constant.

questions.question: Odometer is used to measure:
questions.explanation: Odometer is a device, which is used to measure total distance travelled by the vehicle from the initial position to the final position.

questions.question: The number of vibrations per second is called ________.
questions.explanation: The number of vibrations per second is called frequency. S.I. unit of frequency is Hertz (Hz).Frequency = {tex}1 \over time \ period{/tex}.

questions.question: Time taken to complete one vibration is called ________.
questions.explanation: Time taken to complete one vibration is called a time-period. The time period is the reciprocal of the frequency. It's S.I unit is 'second'.

questions.question: To and fro movement along an axis is called ________.
questions.explanation: One complete to and fro movement of a body along an axis is called oscillation.1 vibration = one complete to and fro motion

questions.question: A sundial built in the 18th century by Maharaja Jaisingh of Jaipur can be seen in which one of the following monuments.
questions.explanation: In the early 18th century, Maharaja Jaisingh of Jaipur constructed five Jantar Mantar in total, in New Delhi, Jaipur, Ujjain, Mathura, and Varanasi. The Jantar Mantar has an equinoctial sundial, consisting of a gigantic triangular gnomon with the hypotenuse parallel to the Earth's axis. The instrument is intended to measure the time of day, correct to half a second. Jantar Mahal in Jaipur is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Speed is a scalar quantity but velocity is a:
questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity but velocity is a vector quantity because velocity has both magnitude and direction, but speed has only magnitude.

questions.question: The slope of distance time-graph represent:
questions.explanation: The slope of a distance-time graph represents the speed of the body at a particular interval of time. Speed is the distance travelled in unit time.

questions.question: A bus travels 54 km in 90 minutes. The speed of the bus is
questions.explanation:
Speed = distance travelled in m / time in seconds. Here distance = 54km = 54 x 1000 =54000 m ; time = 90 minutes = 90 x 60 sec = 5400 sec.
Speed of bus = 54000 m / 5400 sec = 10 m/s

questions.question: The basic unit of speed is
questions.explanation: Speed is the distance travelled by a body in unit time . So speed = distance / time.The SI unit ot speed = m/s (SI units of distance is metre and time is second).

questions.question: The time standard in our country is maintained by which Laboratory.
questions.explanation: The time standard in our country is maintained by the National Physical Laboratory, New Delhi. It is done by an atomic clock in which there is a continuous digital display of time.

questions.question: Lightning of thunder is heard earlier than it sounds because:

options.option: Speed of light is less in air

questions.explanation: Lightning of thunder is heard earlier than it sound because light travels faster than sound as Speed of light = 3,00,000 km/h and speed of sound is only 340 m/s.

questions.question: Speed of the vehicle is not always uniform on the road because of:
questions.explanation: The speed of the vehicle is not always uniform on road because of traffic and red light at which vehicle stops and de-accelerate, in turn, the speed of the vehicle changes.

questions.question: A truck travels 540 km in 4.5 hrs. The speed of the truck is:
questions.explanation: Here, distance travelled = 540 km and time taken = 4.5 hrs. Therefore, Speed of the truck = {tex}distance \over time{/tex} = {tex}540 \over 4.5 {/tex} = 120 km/h.

questions.question: The distance-time graph of an object is a straight line perpendicular to the distance axis. What does this graph indicate about the motion of the object?

options.option: The object is moving in a traffic.

questions.explanation: In a distance-time graph ,we represent time in seconds on X-axis and distance in metres on Y-axis.If the graph is a straight line perpendicular to the distance ,then the object is not moving at different intervals of time without change in distance.

questions.question: Is Speed a Scalar or Vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity as it has the only magnitude as vector quantity has both magnitude and direction.

questions.question: Hand of an athlete at a race is in:
questions.explanation: Periodic motion is the motion repeated in equal intervals of time. The hand of an athlete at race moves back and forth at an equal interval time, so, it is a periodic motion.

questions.question: In 100 m race the time taken by the athletes is measured in:
questions.explanation: In a 100 m race, the time taken by the athletes is measured in seconds as the metre is the SI unit of distance so we use the SI unit of time too(seconds).

questions.question: Winding clocks and wristwatches are a refinement of:
questions.explanation: All clocks make use of periodic motion(motion repeated in equal intervals of time). The pendulum is the periodic motion used in clocks. Winding clocks and wristwatches are a refinement of the pendulum clock.

questions.question: Which speed is greater: 30m/s or 30km/h?

options.option: Both speeds (30 m/s and 30 km/h ) are equal.

questions.explanation: The basic unit of speed is m/s.1 m/s =18 / 5 km/h speed which clearly shows that 30 m/s speed will be a greater value than 30 km/h speed.

questions.question: It takes a simple pendulum 28 s to make 16 oscillations. Calculate its time period.
questions.explanation:
The time period is the time taken to complete one oscillation.
therefore,time period = {tex}time\ taken \over no.\ of\ oscillations{/tex}
= {tex}28  \over16{/tex} = 1.75 s

questions.question: What kind of motion a bullet shows when fired from a gun?
questions.explanation: A bullet fired from a gun shows linear motion as it goes straight in one direction(bullet moves forward ). Linear motion is motion in a straight line.

questions.question: Distance covered by a body can be calculated by:
questions.explanation: Distance is how much ground is covered by an object, regardless of its starting or ending position and it is a scalar quantity (magnitude). Distance covered by a body can be calculated by multiplying speed with time as we are aware that Speed = {tex}\frac {distance}{time}{/tex} so can only be then, Distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time.

questions.question: The distance-time graph is a type of:
questions.explanation: The distance-time graph is a type of Line graph. A line graph is a graphical display of information that changes continuously over time.It is an effective way to understand the motion of an object. The distance travelled is compared to the time taken by the object to travel that distance. This helps us to find out the speed of an object.

questions.question: A car is moving with 72 km/hrs. The speed of car in m/s is ________.
questions.explanation: Speed of car = 72km/h = 72 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}1000 \over 60{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = 20 m/s (1km = 1000m , 1h = 60 min = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 s= 3600 s , so 1km/h = {tex}1000 \over 3600{/tex} m/s)

questions.question: A motorcycle moving with 45 km/h will cover how much distance in 2 hours?
questions.explanation:
Speed = {tex}distance \over time{/tex}, so Distance = Speed {tex}\times{/tex} time.
Here, speed = 45 km/h, time taken = 2 hrs. 
Then distance covered by the motorcycle = 45 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 90 km.

questions.question: The change in position with respect to the surroundings is called ________.
questions.explanation: Motion is a change in the position of an object over time in relation to surroundings. Motion is described in terms of displacement, distance, velocity, acceleration, time, and speed. Motion is mainly of two types: uniform motion and non-uniform motion.

questions.question: Odometer reading at the start of the journey was 1250 km and after 2 hours its reading changes to 1400 km. The average speed of the vehicle for this journey is ________.
questions.explanation:
Distance travelled = 1400 km - 1250km = 150km, time taken = 2 hrs.
Average speed = {tex}distance\ travelled \over time\ taken{/tex}
= {tex}150 \ km \over 2 \ h{/tex} = 75 km/h.

questions.question: A body covers 50 m in the first 2 seconds, 50 m in the next 2 seconds, and 100 m in the next 4 seconds. The speed of the body is:
questions.explanation: A body covering the equal distance in equal interval of time is called uniform motion. Here, in each case distance travelled in one second is 25 m, as the body covers 50m in 2 sec, so, it is uniform motion.

questions.question: A bus covers a distance of 120 m in 1 minute. Its average speed in m/s is
questions.explanation:
Distance travelled = 120 m, time = 1 minute = 60 seconds.
Average speed = {tex}total \ distance \ travelled \over total\ time \ taken{/tex}
= {tex}120\ m\over 60\ s{/tex} = 2 m/s.

questions.question: The watch used to measure a short interval of time is
questions.explanation: The stopwatch is used to measure a short interval of time duration involving any event. A stopwatch is a handheld timepiece designed to measure the amount of time elapsed from a particular time when it is activated to the time when the piece is deactivated.

questions.question: The speed of light in air is about 300000 km/s. How much distance will it travel in 5 seconds?
questions.explanation:
Speed of light = 300000 km/s = 300000 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000 m/s.,Time = 5 seconds.
Distance travelled = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time
= 30, 00,00,000 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 1.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 109 m/s.

questions.question: Early time people used such type of clocks and this is used in Jantar Mantar.Name it.
questions.explanation: A sundial is a device that measures time by using a light spot or shadow cast by the position of the Sun on a reference scale. It consists of a flat plate (the dial) and a gnomon which casts a shadow onto the dial. The Jantar Mantar is a sundial, consisting of a gigantic triangular gnomon with the hypotenuse parallel to the Earth's axis.

questions.question: What happens to the period of a simple pendulum if the pendulum's length is doubled?
questions.explanation: Lengthening the string of a pendulum increases its period. The longer the length of string, the farther the pendulum falls; and therefore, the longer the period, or back and forth swing of the pendulum.

questions.question: How much time a car will take to cover 200 km at a speed of 40 km/h?
questions.explanation:
Distance covered = 200 km, speed of car = 40 km/h.
Time taken = {tex}speed \over distance\ travelled{/tex} (as speed = {tex}distance \ travelled \over time \ taken{/tex})
= {tex}200 \ km \over 40 \ km/h{/tex} = 5 hrs.

questions.question: A simple pendulum completes 150 oscillations in 30 s. The frequency of the pendulum is:
questions.explanation:
Frequency of pendulum is the number of oscillation per second. Number of oscillations = 150, time = 30 s ,so frequency = {tex}150 \over 30{/tex} = 5 Hz.
OR
Given that 150 oscillations are made by the pendulum for 30 s so, 5 oscillations are made by the pendulum for 1sec.
We know that the number of oscillations made per second (5 oscillations per second) is called its frequency
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Frequency = 5 Hz
Time period = {tex}1\over Frequency{/tex} = {tex}1 \over 5{/tex} = 0.2sec

questions.question: Speed is the ratio of:

options.option: Mass of the body to the time taken

questions.explanation: Speed is the ratio of the distance travelled to the time taken. The basic formula says Speed = {tex}Distance \over Time{/tex} . From this formula, it is evident that speed is directly proportional to distance and inversely proportional to time.

questions.question: A passenger is sitting in a moving train the person is:

options.option: Neither in state of rest nor in state of motion
In-state of motion as well as in a state of rest

questions.explanation: Rest and motion are relative terms. A body may be seen to be at rest with respect to one object but may appear to be in motion with respect to another object. So a passenger sitting in a moving train is in state of rest with respect to other passengers inside the train but in state of motion with respect to outside the train.

questions.question: The state of rest or motion is always compared with:

options.option: Position of the body in the vehicle

questions.explanation: The state of rest or motion is always compared with other bodies in the surrounding. Rest refers to an object being stationary relative to a particular object. When the position of a body with respect to its surroundings does not change with time it is said to be "at rest". According to the theory of relativity, it is said that an object is "at rest relative to" another. For example, a train deaccelerates approaching a station and eventually comes to rest alongside the platform. The train can be said to be "at rest with respect to the station",

questions.question: A car travelled at a speed of 50 km/h for 30 minutes and then at a speed of 80 km/h for 45 minutes. The total distance covered by car is equal to:
questions.explanation:
In first case, speed = 50 km/h, time = 30 minutes = 0.5 hrs. Distance = speed x time = 50 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5= 25 km. 
In second case, speed = 80 km/h, time = 45 minute = {tex}45\over 60{/tex} = {tex}3 \over 4{/tex} hrs. Distance = speed  {tex}\times{/tex} time = 80 {tex}\times{/tex}  {tex}3 \over 4{/tex}h = 60km.
Total distance = 25 + 60 = 85 km.

questions.question: The speed of the vehicle is recorded by
questions.explanation: A speedometer or a speed meter is a gauge that measures and displays the instantaneous speed of a vehicle.

questions.question: S.I unit of speed is
questions.explanation: Speed and velocity are both measured using the same units. The SI unit of distance and displacement is the meter. The SI unit of time is the second. The SI unit of speed and velocity is the ratio of two — the meter per second.

questions.question: Odometer is used to measure
questions.explanation: An odometer or odograph is an instrument for measuring the distance travelled by a vehicle, such as a bicycle or car. The device may be electronic, mechanical, or a combination of the two.

questions.question: Number of vibration per second is called
questions.explanation: Frequency is the number of waves that pass a fixed point in unit time; also, the number of cycles or vibrations undergone during one unit of time by a body in periodic motion. The number of vibration per second is called as frequency. It is measured in Hertz.

questions.question: When a body covers equal distance in equal interval of time. The speed of the body is called as
questions.explanation: If an object covers equal distance in equal intervals of time, we can say that the object is moving with a uniform speed.

questions.question: Total distance covered by a body in unit interval of time in particular direction is called
questions.explanation:
Velocity of a body is the distance traveled by it per unit time in a given direction. 
Velocity = {tex}\frac{\text{distance traveled in a given direction}}{\text{time taken}}{/tex}
V = {tex}\frac{s}{t}{/tex}
Where v = velocity
S = distance traveled in a given direction
T = time taken (for displacement)
The S.I. unit of velocity is m/s. it is vector quantity.

questions.question: Speedometer fitted on dashboard of car measures
questions.explanation: A speedometer or a speed meter is a gauge that measures and displays the instantaneous speed of a vehicle. Speedometer fitted on dashboard of a car measures speed of a car at particular moment.

questions.question: Tip of a second clock moves in
questions.explanation: Circular motion is a movement of an object along the circumference of a circle or rotation along a circular path. So, Tip of a second clock moves is in circular motion.

questions.question: Average speed is equal to
questions.explanation:
Average speed can be viewed as the rate of change in distance with respect to time.
So, Average Speed = {tex}\frac{\text{Total distance traveled}}{\text{total time taken}}{/tex}

questions.question: The speed of a car traveled in particular interval of time is
questions.explanation: Average speed can be viewed as the rate of change in distance with respect to time.  A car traveling at an average speed of 25 miles per hour covers an average distance of 25 miles every hour.

questions.question: When a body is said to be in uniform speed, it covers equal distance in
questions.explanation: If an object covers equal distance in equal intervals of time, we can say that the object is moving with a uniform speed.

questions.question: Lightening of thunder is heard earlier than it sound because

options.option: Speed of light is less in air

questions.explanation: Light travels at about 300 million metres per second, so you effectively see the flash instantly. Sound travels at more like 340 metres per second, so it can take many seconds for the sound to reach you. So light travels faster than sound.

questions.question: Speed of vehicle is not always uniform on road because of
questions.explanation: We have to apply brakes many time due to which velocity decreases therefore velocity do not remain uniform so the speed of a vehicle not always uniform. Speed of vehicle is not always uniform on road because of traffic and red light.

questions.question: In 100 m race the time taken by the athletes is measured in
questions.explanation: Speed is distance traveled per unit of time. The SI units for speed are m/s (meters per second). So, in 100 m race the time taken by the athletes is measured in second.

questions.question: Winding clocks and wristwatches are refinement of
questions.explanation: Galileo found that a pendulum of a given length takes always the same time to complete one oscillation. This observation led the development of pendulum clocks. Winding clocks and wristwatches were refinements of the pendulum clocks.

questions.question: Distance covered by a body can be calculated by
questions.explanation:
Speed is distance traveled per unit of time.
Speed = Distance {tex}\div{/tex} time
So, Distance travelled = Speed {tex}\times{/tex} time.

questions.question: A car covers 90 km distance in 2 hours. The average speed of the car is
questions.explanation:
Distance travelled = 90 km, time taken = 2 hours.
Average speed = total distance {tex}\div{/tex} total time taken = 90 km {tex}\div{/tex} 2 hr = 45 km/h.

questions.question: Speed of a car is 80 km/h. How much distance will it cover in 3 hours?
questions.explanation:
Speed = 80 km/h, Time = 3 hrs.
Speed = Distance {tex}\div{/tex} time
So. Distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time = 80 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 240 km.

questions.question: To and fro movement of the body is termed as:
questions.explanation: When a body moves to and fro about its mean position is called vibratory motion. Vibratory motion can be described as any object moving/swinging back and forth, moving up and down, pulsating, spinning, or vibrating. swinging; pendulums, swings, metronomes are the example of vibratory motion.

questions.question: A car covers 20 km in the first hour and cover 30 km in the last 4 hours. Find its average speed.
questions.explanation:
Given, Total distance covered = 20 km + 30 km = 50 km

Total time taken = 1 h + 4 h = 5 h

We know that, Average speed = Total distance travelled / total time taken

= 50 km / 5h

= 10 km/h

questions.question: A coin and a feather are dropped from same height, which one will reach first on the ground?
questions.explanation: When a coin and a feather are dropped from same height, the coin will reach first on the ground. Due to more friction on feather as it is lighter and has more surface area as compare to coin.

questions.question: The basic unit of speed is:
questions.explanation:
m / s

All the distances are traveled in metres and time is calculated in seconds.

So, we say speed of a body has units metres per second.

questions.question: A car moves with a speed of 40 km/h for 15 minutes and then with a speed of 60 km/h for the next 15 minutes. The total distance covered by the car is
questions.explanation:
S1=40km/hr ; t1=15mins=1/4hr

D1= s1 x t1 =10km

S2=60km/hr;t2=15mins =1/4hr

D2=S2 x t2 =15km

Total distance covered = D1 + D2 =25km

questions.question: Nearly all the clocks make use of:
questions.explanation: All the hands of a clock repeat themselves in a regular interval of time so the motion by a clock is called periodic motion.

questions.question: A simple pendulum takes 42 s to complete 20 oscillations. What is its time period?
questions.explanation:
The time period is defined as the total time taken to complete one oscillation.
Total time = 42 seconds
Number of oscillations =20
1 oscillation = {tex}42\over 20{/tex}
Time period = 2.1 seconds

questions.question: One-millionth of a second is called:
questions.explanation: 1 million has 6 zeros in it. Therefore, 1 millionth of a second) = 10-6 seconds = micro-seconds.

questions.question: What part of a second is nano second?
questions.explanation: A nanosecond is 10-9 seconds and is equal to one-billionth of a second.

questions.question: How will you convert the speed given in km/h to m/s?
questions.explanation:
1km =1000m
And 1hr = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60seconds
1km/hr = {tex}\frac{1000 \mathrm{~m}}{3600 \mathrm{sec}}{/tex}
1km/hr = {tex}\frac{5}{18}{/tex} ms-1

questions.explanation: The variable whose quantity depends upon the change in the value of another quantity is called as dependent variable and is plotted along the y-axis.

questions.question: What is the relation between displacement and speed?

questions.question: Boojho walks to his school which is at a distance of 3 km from his home in 30 minutes. On reaching he finds that the school is closed and comes back by bicycle with his friend and reaches home in 20 minutes. His average speed in km/h is
questions.explanation:
Average speed {tex}=\frac{\text { Total distance }}{\text { Time taken }}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(3+3) \mathrm{km}}{\left(\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}\right) \mathrm{h}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{6}{5 / 6}=\frac{36}{5}{/tex} = 7.2 km/h.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is a conductor?
questions.explanation: That substance that passes electricity is called the conductor. Silver is a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Magnetic effect of electric current was observed by:
questions.explanation: Hans Christian Øersted was a Danish physicist and chemist who discovered that electric currents create magnetic fields, which was the first connection found between electricity and magnetism.

questions.question: A device which prevents or allows the current to flow through it:
questions.explanation:
An electric switch is a device for making and breaking the connection in an electric circuit. 
If a switch is open, no current flows through that path (ie it is off). If the switch is closed, the current is allowed to flow (the path is on).

questions.question: Electromagnet loses its magnetic property when:
questions.explanation: Electromagnet shows magnetic properties only as long as the electric current flows through its coil.

questions.question: An electromagnet is based on:
questions.explanation: An electromagnet is a magnet that works as magnet in the presence of current only. It is based on the magnetic effect of electric current.

questions.question: In an electric circuit, components are represented by using:
questions.explanation: In an electric circuit, various electric components are represented by using the symbol of components. It is much easier to draw a circuit diagram using symbols. A pictorial circuit diagram uses simple images of the components while the schematic diagram shows the components and interconnections of the circuit using standardized symbolic representations.

questions.question: In the symbol of an electric cell, the longer line represents:
questions.explanation: In a symbol of the electric cell, longer lines represent positive terminal and smaller lines represent the negative terminal. questions.question: Electricity will not flow through the circuit if:

options.option: There is a key in the circuit

questions.explanation: Electric current will not flow through a circuit if the circuit is incomplete. It is to be noted that the resistance of an air gap is infinity. If the circuit is not complete then it is called an open circuit. A closed-circuit means a complete electrical connection around which current flows or circulates.

questions.question: In a battery, the cells are connected:
questions.explanation: In a battery, the cells are connected positive to negative terminals to complete the circuit. The cells are connected in series with the opposite terminals together.An electric battery is a device consisting of one or more electrochemical cells.When a battery is supplying electric power, its positive terminal is the cathode and a negative terminal is the anode.

questions.question: In an electric circuit bulb glows only when the switch is in:
questions.explanation: In an electric circuit, bulb glows only when the switch is in the 'ON' position and the electric circuit is closed. A closed-circuit is the one through which current flows and the path a path is provided for the flow. A circuit is usually closed by plugging in a switch.

questions.question: When a bulb gets fused, its:
questions.explanation: When a bulb gets fused, its filament is broken that breaks the circuit to prevent the flow of current. Filaments are made up of wire with a high melting point like tungsten. The filament is part of the light bulb that produces light.

questions.question: In an electric circuit, a key or switch can be placed:
questions.explanation: In an electric circuit, key or switch can be connected anywhere in the circuit. It is called a closed circuit.

questions.question: Electricity is produced in power stations by:
questions.explanation: In power stations, electricity is produced by using a huge generator or dynamo to convert mechanical energy to electrical energy. The generator is a device that converts motive power into electrical power.

questions.question: An electric cell produces electricity from the:
questions.explanation: An electric cell produces electricity from the chemical stored in it by converting chemical energy to electrical energy. Special chemical reactions that occur inside the electrical cell results in oxidation and reduction inside the cell. This produces electrical energy. Normal batteries work like this.

questions.question: Inside the torch two or more cells are placed with:
questions.explanation: In the torch, two or more cells are placed with Positive – negative- positive –negative terminals in series. The positive terminal of one cell and the negative terminal of other cells are free to draw current from this combination of two cells.

questions.question: A is used in electric clocks.
questions.explanation: A dry cell is used in electric clocks and other devices that are mobile. A dry cell is a cell in which electrolyte is present in the form of a paste so that it does not spill and hence can be portable and easy to use. It is used in toys, calculators and many more.

questions.question: Which of the following lightening device uses comparatively less energy?
questions.explanation: A compact fluorescent lamp uses comparatively less energy than tube light, bulb, and filament rod. The fluorescent lamp does not have filaments so, they do not waste electricity by producing heat and they are very power efficient.CFL also called as compact fluorescent light, energy saving light is a fluorescent lamp used in place of the bulb, tube light as its energy-efficient and one can get less electricity bill.

questions.question: ISI mark is issued by:
questions.explanation: ISI mark is issued by Bureau of Indian standards (BIS) that certifies that instrument is safe and wastage of energy is minimum (energy efficient).The abbrevation of ISI is Indian standard institute, former name of Bureau of Indian Standards.The ISI mark is mandatory for certain products to be sold in India.

questions.question: ISI marks to ensure that the appliance is safe and wastage of energy is ________.
questions.explanation: ISI marks on appliances ensure that the appliance is safe and the wastage of energy is minimum.ISI mark is the certification mark for industrial products in India given by the Bureau of Indian Standards(BIS).

questions.question: An electrical fuse is made of material:

options.option: That breaks when a high current flows

questions.explanation: An electric fuse is made of a material having a low melting point that breaks the circuit when a high current flows. An electrical fuse is a safety device that operates to provide overcurrent protection of an electrical circuit.

questions.question: The maximum limit of current that can flow through a circuit is called:
questions.explanation: The maximum limit of current that can flow through a circuit is called its current rating. It mostly depends on the insulation temperature and electrical resistance of the conductor.

questions.question: An electric circuit, fire may occur due to:
questions.explanation: An electric circuit, fire may occur due to overloading and short-circuit. An extremely large current can flow in the electric circuits under these two circumstances. When the current in a circuit takes an unintended path we call it a short circuit. Overloading is a condition when high current more than expected flow through the circuit. In both conditions, the circuit may burn to lead to the damage of appliances.

questions.question: MCB is used in the circuit because:

options.option: Both turn ON and turn OFF automatically
Neither turn ON and turn OFF automatically

questions.explanation: MCB is a kind of switch that automatically turn OFF when the current in a circuit exceeds the safe limit.MCB is known as Miniature Circuit Breaker. It is connected with wires and is used as a safety purpose.MCB prevents the short circuit by discontinuing current flow or switch trips and the circuit is broken. Unlike a fuse, MCB need not be replaced.

questions.question: When a magnetic compass is placed near a current-carrying conductor. Compass:
questions.explanation: When a magnetic compass is placed near a current-carrying conductor, compass deflects due to the magnetic effect of electricity. This clearly indicates that the compass needle interacts with the magnetic field of the material.

questions.question: Electromagnet looses its magnetic property when
questions.explanation: The magnetism of an electromagnet remains as long as the current is flowing in its coil. An electromagnet is a type of magnet in which magnetic field is produced by the flow of electric current.An electric current flowing in a wire creates a magnetic field around the wire.

questions.question: In electric bell, the bell rings when:
questions.explanation: In an electric bell, the bell rings when the hammer at the end of the strip strikes the gong of the bell to produce sound due to the magnetic effect.

questions.question: Which one is a bad conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Salty water, tap water, and drinking water contain a small number of salts in it to conduct electric current but distilled water is a bad conductor. Distilled water does not contain any dissolved ions which can conduct electricity by acting as a carrier of electric charge and hence they do not conduct electricity.

questions.question: When a fuse rated 8 A, it means:

options.option: It will melt or break if the current exceeds 8 A
It will works if the current exceeds 8 M

questions.explanation: When a fuse rate 8A, it means that it will melt or break if current in the circuit exceeds 8A.The current rating of a fuse is the amount of current that a fuse can allow to pass through it.If the current exceeds the current rating of the fuse then it will melt or break the circuit.

questions.question: Fuse wire is made up of alloys of:
questions.explanation: Fuse wire is made up of alloys of the low melting point that melts if higher current flows through the circuit. Generally, fuse wires are made of alloys because when too much of current is passed it melts preventing any further damage.

questions.question: When there is an electric current passing through a wire, the particle moving is:
questions.explanation: When there is an electric current passing through a wire, then an electric current is actually a flow of charged particles called 'electrons' in a conductor.

questions.question: MCB in electric circuit stands for
questions.explanation: MCB is electric circuit stands for miniature circuit breaker used to break circuit in case of excess of current.

questions.question: A battery is used to:
questions.explanation: A battery in a circuit is used to maintain the potential differences in the circuit to allow the flow of current.

questions.question: Earth wire is connected to those electrical appliances that have:
questions.explanation: Earth wire is a safety device that protects us from electric shocks. It is used in appliances having a metallic body.

questions.question: Why gases such as argon or Nitrogen are filled inside an electric bulb?
questions.explanation: In an electric bulb, argon or nitrogen gas is filled because they are chemically unreactive and prevent oxidation of filament. Bulbs can't be filled with air as the presence of oxygen will cause the filament to burn and if the bulb is made vacuum then the filament will evaporate due to very high temperature making the bulb unusable.

questions.question: In domestic circuit, appliances are generally connected in:
questions.explanation: In a house, there are many electrical appliances that have to run independently of each other. If one appliance is turned on or off it should not affect the other appliances. This is not possible if all the appliances were connected in a series arrangement as there would be one switch that either switch all of them on or off. When appliances are connected in a parallel arrangement, each of them can be put on and off independently. This is a feature that is essential in domestic wiring.

questions.question: Metals are good conductors of electricity because it has:
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductor of electricity because it has free electrons that conduct current. When electricity is passed through metals these free electrons move in a particular direction to conduct the flow of charges which hel in the conduction of electricity.

questions.question: The amount of work done to move a unit charge from one point to the other point is called ________.
questions.explanation: The amount of work done to move a unit charge from one point to the other point is called the potential difference. It is also described as the potential energy difference per unit charge or voltage. The unit of potential difference is the Volt (V).

questions.question: Which of the following group contains the only conductor?
questions.explanation: Copper, graphite, and silver are good conductors of electricity. While, woollen cloth, rubber, and distilled water are bad conductors of electricity.

questions.question: Silicon and Germanium are elements having:
questions.explanation: Silicon and Germanium are elements having a resistivity between conductor and insulator. Such type of elements is called as semi-conductors.

questions.question: In an electric bulb, argon or nitrogen gas is filled because they are:
questions.explanation: we cannot fill a bulb with air as hot tungsten wire will combust in the presence of oxygen. So we have to fill it with nonreactive gas like nitrogen or argon.

questions.question: Chemical effect of electric current is used in
questions.explanation: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reactions. Electroplating is one of its most common application. Electroplating is the process of depositing a layer of any desired metal on another material by means of electricity.

questions.question: Ohm is the SI unit of
questions.explanation: Ohm is the SI unit of resistance. Resistance opposes the flow of current.

questions.question: Electromagnet is a
questions.explanation: Electromagnet is a temporary magnet that works till current flow through it. In electromagnet magnetic field is produced by an electric current. The magnetic field disappears when the current is turned off.

questions.question: Short circuit is caused due to

options.option: Direct contact of live wire and earth wire
Direct contact of live wire and hot wire
Direct contact of neutral wire and earth wire
Direct contact of live and neutral wire

questions.explanation: Short circuit is caused due to direct contact of live and neutral wire without insulation. The two wires may touch each other due to wearing off the insulation or due to some faulty connection. This causes overheating of the wire or a fire.

questions.question: Connecting too many appliances with single socket may lead to
questions.explanation: Connecting too many appliances with single socket may lead to overloading. Never overload an extension lead by plugging in appliances that together will exceed the maximum current rating stated for the extension lead. This could cause the plug in the wall socket to overheat and possibly cause a fire.

questions.question: Which two wires passes through the electric meter?
questions.explanation: Live wire and neutral wire passes through the electric meter to measure the amount of energy consumed.

questions.question: Fuse is a safety device which is made up of
questions.explanation: Fuse is a safety device which is made up of alloy of low melting point to break the circuit in case of excess current. It prevents damages to electrical circuits and possible fires.

questions.question: In the symbol of electric cell, the longer line represents
questions.explanation: In the symbol of electric cell, the longer line represents positive terminal and smaller line represents negative terminal.

questions.question: The wire used to connect the various components of circuit is represented by a
questions.explanation: The wire used to connect the various components of the circuit is represented by a straight line. A straight line is the symbolic representation of a wire.

questions.question: The SI unit of current is
questions.explanation: The SI unit of current is ampere. Ampere is represented by the symbol A.

questions.question: The magnet produced by using electric current is called
questions.explanation: The magnet produced by using electric current is called electromagnet. Electromagnet is a temporary magnet which looses its magnetic field when current is switched off.

questions.question: A safety device that protects electrical appliances from short circuiting and overloading is called
questions.explanation: A safety device that protects electrical appliances from short circuit and overloading is called a fuse. Fuse is made up of an alloy of low melting point.

questions.question: In an electric bell, bell rings when
questions.explanation: In an electric bell, bell rings when hammer hits the gong to produce a sound.

questions.question: The electric meter measure the current used in
questions.explanation: One kilo-watt hour is a measure of electrical energy equivalent to a power consumption of one thousand watts for one hour.

questions.question: Earth wire is connected to those electrical appliances that have
questions.explanation: Earth wire is connected to those electrical appliances that have metallic body. It prevents us from getting electric shock.

questions.question: In current electricity, the electric charges are in
questions.explanation: In current electricity, the electric charges are in motion. An electric current is the flow of electrons.

questions.question: The commercial unit of electrical energy consumed is
questions.explanation: The commercial unit of electrical energy is kilowatt-hour (kWh). One kilowatt-hour is unit of energy equals to one kilowatt of power expended for one hour.

questions.question: Miniature circuit breaker (MCB) is used to
questions.explanation: Miniature circuit breaker (MCB) is used to save the electric circuit from overloading and short circuit.

questions.question: Earth wire is used to
questions.explanation: Earth wire is used to protect us from electric shock. Earth wire is conncted from exposed metallic appliance to the earth.

questions.question: In bulbs, electric energy is converted into:
questions.explanation: In the light bulb, the flow of charge through the filament heats it up and causes it to glow. In this way, the light bulb converts electrical energy to heat energy and light energy.

questions.question: Where can the key or switch be placed in a circuit?

options.option: Can be placed anywhere in the circuit
Near the positive terminal of the bulb

questions.explanation: The switch is a device used to close or open an electric circuit. Switch in a circuit can be placed anywhere.

questions.question: Which mark is necessary for electric appliances?
questions.explanation: ISI mark is a certification mark for industrial products in India. The mark certifies that a product conforms to the Indian Standard. The name ISI is an abbreviation of Indian Standards Institute, the former name of the Bureau of Indian Standards. The ISI mark is mandatory for certain products to be sold in India, like many of the electrical appliances like switches, electric motors, wiring cables, heaters, kitchen appliances etc.

questions.question: What is the full form of MCBs?
questions.explanation: The full form of MCB is Miniature Circuit Breakers. A circuit breaker is an automatically operated electrical switch designed to protect an electrical circuit from damage caused by overload or short circuit.

questions.question: Who discovered the magnetic effect of current?
questions.explanation: The magnetic effect of electric current was discovered by H.C. Oersted in 1820. He observed that the flow of electric current through a conductor produces a magnetic field around it. The magnetic effect of current can be demonstrated by doing the following experiment.

questions.question: Which device is used to prevent damages to electrical circuits and possible fires?
questions.explanation:
MCBs is automatically operated electrical switch designed to protect an electrical circuit from damage caused by overload or short circuit.
An electric fuse is a safety device to prevent damage to an electrical circuit when excessive current flows through it.

questions.question: In an electric bell, which effect of current is used?
questions.explanation: When an electric current flows through a wire, it produces a magnetic effect around it. A current-carrying coil of insulated wire wrapped around a piece of iron is called an electromagnet. Magnetic Effect of electric current has many applications like Electric Bell, Motor, Fan, etc.

questions.question: The amount of heat produced in a wire depends on:
questions.explanation:
The amount of heat produced in a wire depends on

  1. Its material– better conductors produce less heat; e.g.   Elements of electric appliances are made of nichrome whereas connecting wires are made up of copper which is a better conductor so they do not get heated up as the element.
  2. its length –as length increases heat produced increases;e.g. elements are coiled in heaters to increase length.
  3. Thickness – as thickness increases heat produced decreases;e.g. lead wires of iron, heater etc. are thicker than normal connecting wire so that they don’t get heated up easily

questions.question: In making a battery:

options.option: The terminal of one cell is not connected to the next cell.
The positive terminal of one cell is connected to the negative terminal of the next cell
The positive terminal of one cell is connected to the positive terminal of the next cell
The negative terminal of one cell is connected to the negative terminal of the next cell

questions.explanation: A combination of two or more cells connected together is called a battery. It is formed by connecting the positive terminal of one cell to the negative terminal of another.

questions.question: One of these is not a property of the magnet

options.option: A magnetic compass is used by sailors to know the direction
Like poles attract and unlike poles repels
Like poles repels and unlike poles attract
Alloy like alnico is used in making temporary magnet.

questions.explanation: Like poles attract and unlike poles repels is not a property of the magnet. Unlike poles attract each other and like poles repel each other.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a magnetic substance?
questions.explanation: Materials that are attracted by a magnet are called magnetic materials. Examples of magnetic materials include iron, cobalt, nickel, steel, etc. Materials that are not attracted by magnets are called non-magnetic materials. Examples of non-magnetic materials include rubber, plastic, stainless steel, feather, paper, mica, gold, silver, leather, etc.

questions.question: Electromagnet is not used in
questions.explanation: Electromagnets are used in a number of electrical devices, such as electric bell, telephone, telegraph, radio, transistor, television, loud speaker. Electromagnet is not used in electric press.

questions.question: Which one of the following is based on the heating effect of current?
questions.explanation: When an electric current flows through a wire, the wire gets heated. It is the heating effect of current or when current flows through a metal wire it gets heated up, this is called heating effect of current. E.g. a glowing electric bulb becomes warm. This effect has many applications like Electric Heater, Light Bulb,Geyser etc.

questions.question: In dry cell carbon rod functions as
questions.explanation: A dry cell has the electrolyte immobilized as a paste, with only enough moisture in it to allow current to flow. The cell is made up of an outer zinc container, which acts as the anode. The cathode is a central carbon rod, surrounded by a mixture of carbon and manganese dioxide (MnO2).

questions.question: The fuse wire is made of-
questions.explanation: A fuse wire is made of tin alloy because it has low melting point, so that it may melt easily, whereas a copper wire cannot be used as a fuse wire because it has a high melting point due to which it will not melt easily when a short circuit takes place.

questions.question: Rectifier is used in-

options.option: To convert thermal energy into electric energy
To convert light energy into electric energy

questions.explanation: Using a rectifier in the power supply helps in converting AC to DC power supply. Bridge rectifiers are widely used for large appliances, where they are capable of converting high AC voltage to low DC voltage.

questions.question: When the glass rod is rubbed with the piece of silk, then according to known tradition:

options.option: Glass rod got positively charged and silk piece got negatively charged
Glass rod got negatively charged and silk piece got positively charged

questions.explanation: When the glass rod is rubbed with silk clothes, glass quickly loses electrons, and silk takes electrons out of the glass rod. So after rubbing, the glass rod becomes positively charged and the silk gets negative charge. So glass acquires a greater order than silk as electron losses.

questions.question: An electric current can produce
questions.explanation: An electric current can produce heating, chemical and magnetic effects. When a current is flowing in conductor, then magnetic field produces around the conductor. When a current flows through a conductor, heat energy generates in the conducts that is known as heating effect of electric current. When an electric current flows through a conducting solution chemical reaction take place in solution. This is called the chemical effect of electric current.

questions.question: Gases are conductor of electricity
questions.explanation: Gases can conduct electricity, but they are considered as insulators for the most part. Gas on its own cannot conduct electricity but it can be made to conduct electricity by subjecting it under low pressure and high voltage.

questions.question: Dynamo is a machine, the work of which is-

options.option: To convert low voltage into high voltage
To convert mechanical energy into electrical energy
To convert high voltage into low voltage
To convert electric energy into mechanical energy

questions.explanation: Dynamos and Generators convert mechanical rotation into electric power. Dynamo - a device that makes direct current electric power using electromagnetism. It is also known as a generator; however the term generator normally refers to an “alternator” which creates alternating current power.

questions.question: What is the apparatus used in measuring the electric current?
questions.explanation: The electric current in one part of a circuit is measured with an ammeter, which gives a reading in ampere. To take the measurement, a gap is made in the circuit and the ammeter is connected into that gap, so that the charged particles moving around the circuit must pass through the meter.

questions.question: Electric current is the flow of ________.
questions.explanation: electrons

questions.question: If electric wires and appliances are overheated due to flow of high electric current they can ________.
questions.explanation: both catch fire and burn

questions.question: Miniature circuit breaker is a small ________.
questions.explanation: electromagnetic switch

questions.question: In a circuit diagram having a cell, a switch and a bulb, the sequence of their arrangement will be
questions.explanation: in any sequence

questions.question: Electric bell is based on

options.option: Both heating effect of current and chemical effect of current

questions.explanation: magnetic effect of current

questions.question: The appliance that does not use electromagnet is
questions.explanation: geyser

questions.question: The heat energy produced by a glowing bulb can be minimised by using ________ in place of ordinary electric bulbs.
questions.explanation: CFL bulbs

questions.question: Who discovered that when a compass needle is kept near to an electric circuit in which current is flowing, it deflects?
questions.explanation: Michael Faraday

questions.question: Electric bell works on which of the following principles?

options.option: Electrical energy is converted into sound energy.
Mechanical energy is converted into electric energy.
Mechanical energy is converted into sound energy.
Electrical energy is converted into mechanical energy.

questions.explanation: Electrical energy is converted into sound energy.

questions.question: An instrument that automatically stops the current in a circuit if the current in it exceeds the specified maximum limit.
questions.explanation: MCB

questions.question: ________ piece is attracted by an electromagnet.
questions.explanation: Iron

questions.question: Do not use appliances if ________.

options.option: both it is damaged and wires are exposed

questions.explanation: both it is damaged and wires are exposed

questions.question: Which of the following statements will be true for the given circuit, when the switch will be on?

options.option: Lamp 1 and 2 will glow together and lamp 3 will glow later.

questions.explanation: Lamp 3 will glow first.

questions.question: An electric heater is used by us to convert electrical energy to ________.
questions.explanation: heat energy

questions.question: Short wire has ________ whereas long wire has ________ resistance.
questions.explanation: Less, more

questions.question: A circuit diagram which includes a battery of 3 cells, a bulb and an open switch is:
A fuse wire has
questions.explanation: low melting point

questions.question: Nichrome is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: The electric bell makes use of
questions.explanation: U - shaped electromagnet

questions.question: Element of electric iron become red-hot on passing current because:
questions.explanation: It has very high resistance.

questions.question: A wire carrying an electric current possesses a magnetic field around it was concluded by:
questions.explanation: H. C. Oersted

questions.question: Electric fuse is used to protect household electric appliances from accidental:
questions.explanation: Large flow of current

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is INCORRECT for the given circuit?

options.option: The given circuit is incorrect because the circuit is connected from negative to negative terminal which is not possible.
When compass is kept near the circuit, it will not show any deflection.
Due to battery in this circuit, electric wire behaves as a magnet.

questions.explanation: The given circuit is incorrect because the circuit is connected from negative to negative terminal which is not possible.

questions.question:
When a switch is in ON position,

  1. Circuit is open
  2. Circuit is closed
  3. Current is flowing in the circuit
  4. No Current is flowing through it

Choose the correct combination of answer for the above information.
questions.explanation: (ii) and (iii)

questions.question: More heat is produced in an electric circuit when:

options.option: Both Magnitude of current is increased and Wire with greater resistance is used

questions.explanation: Both Magnitude of current is increased and Wire with greater resistance is used

questions.question: Thickness of wire used in working circuit M is more than the thickness of wire used in circuit N. Which of the following options is correct?

options.option: More heat will be produced in circuit N than circuit M.
More heat will be produced in circuit M than circuit N.
Equal amount of heat will be produced in both the circuits.

questions.explanation: Equal amount of heat will be produced in both the circuits.

questions.question: A solenoid acts as a ________ when electric current passes through it.
questions.explanation: Bar magnet

questions.question: A circuit in which current is flowing, consists of a steel spoon coiled with copper wire, when some allpins are brought near this spoon, the allpins get attracted due to:
questions.explanation: Magnetic effect of current

questions.question: ________ is a magnet produced by electric current.
questions.explanation: Electromagnet

questions.question: Current means:
questions.explanation: Flow of electrons in the circuit.

questions.question: ________ is measured by voltmeter.
questions.explanation: Potential difference

questions.question: Which is the commercial unit of electrical energy?
questions.explanation: Kilowatt-hour

questions.question: Which one of the following does not work on the heating effect of current?
Electric fuse works on ________ effect of electric current.
questions.explanation: Heating

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs is incorrect?
questions.explanation: Long wire - Low resistance

questions.question: The amount of current that flows depends on the amount of ________ applied by the battery.
questions.explanation: Voltage

questions.question: Which of these is the correct arrangement of electric cells in a battery?

questions.explanation: Electric kettle

questions.question: Which of these labels on an electric device confirms standard quality?
Which of these consumes the least electricity for producing the same amount of light?
questions.explanation: LED

questions.question: When two bodies are rubbed against each other

options.option: one body acquires a charge and other remains uncharged.

questions.explanation: they acquire equal and opposite charges.

questions.explanation: Conductor

questions.question: Which of the following is a conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Tungsten

questions.question: A circuit diagram is

options.option: a drawing of circuit with picture for the different electrical components.
Both a picture of a circuit and a drawing of circuit with picture for the different electrical components
a drawing of an electrical circuit with standard symbol for the different electrical components.

questions.explanation: a drawing of an electrical circuit with standard symbol for the different electrical components.

questions.question: The metallic cap provided at the one end of a dry cell is
questions.explanation: Positive terminal

questions.question: A fuse is placed in an electrical circuit to protect it from
questions.explanation: Short circuit

questions.question: MCBs are connected to the
questions.explanation: Live wire

questions.question: Soft iron is used in making ________.
questions.explanation: Electromagnets

questions.question: An electric bell has
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: When we bring a magnetic compass needle near a current-carrying wire

options.option: It makes the magnetic needle point north-south.
It makes the magnetic needle point east-west.
It deflects the magnetic needle of the compass in any direction.
It has no effect on the magnetic needle.

questions.explanation: It deflects the magnetic needle of the compass in any direction.

questions.question: A safety device that protects the circuit is known as?
questions.explanation: Fuse

questions.question: A device used to break or complete the circuit is called a
questions.explanation: switch

questions.question: Tungsten metal has
