questions.explanation: Electricity

questions.question: In which of the following type of circuit does the electricity flow?
questions.explanation: Closed

questions.question: How are cells connected in a circuit when more electric power is required to operate a device?
questions.explanation: In series

questions.question: What is the term used for the path of electricity?
questions.explanation: Circuit

questions.question: What allows the electric current top pass through it?
questions.explanation: Conductor

questions.question: Which element is used to make the filament of the bulb?
questions.explanation: Tungsten

questions.question: Temporary magnet created by passing electricity through coil of wires is known as?
questions.explanation: Electromagnet

questions.question: Which gas is filled inside the bulb?
questions.explanation: Argon

questions.question: The coil of wire in all the appliances is termed as?
questions.explanation: Element

questions.question: In which of the following are the electromagnets used?
questions.explanation: Doorbell

questions.question: Convex lens focus a real, point sized image at focus, the object is placed
questions.explanation: At infinity

questions.question: Bouncing back of light in same medium is called ________.
questions.explanation: An image can be seen in the mirror because the light reflected from an object falls on the mirror and it is reflected. So, light incident on any smooth shiny surface like a mirror bounces back into the same medium. This bouncing of light by any smooth surface is called reflection of light.

questions.question: The radius of curvature of a mirror is 20cm the focal length is
The visually impaired can also read with fingers using a special alphabet called:
questions.explanation: Braille

questions.question: Concave mirror is used in head light of vehicle as:

options.option: It forms the strong beam of light
It reflects the parallel beam of light

questions.explanation: Concave mirrors are commonly used in not only vehicle headlights but also in torches and search-lights to get powerful parallel beams of light to focus the light at the longer distances by placing the source of light at the focus of the Concave mirrors.

questions.question: The amount of light that is reflected depends on:

options.option: the material of the surface and the nature of the surface

questions.explanation: the material of the surface and the nature of the surface

questions.question: Form of energy that gives us the sensation of sight is:
questions.explanation: light

questions.question: What will be the angle of incidence if a ray of light is reflected back at an angle of 30:
The virtual image is always:
questions.explanation: An image, such as that seen in a mirror, in which the rays of light appear to be emanating from some object that isn’t there is called a virtual image. Virtual images are always erect. They, also, maybe either larger or smaller than the object.

questions.question: Which colour of light scattered at least?
questions.explanation: When light from the sun passes through the atmosphere it interacts with particles in the air causing it to bend and scatter. Red light scatters the least. Blue and violet light which bend the most are also scattered the most.

questions.question: Which type of image can be obtained on screen?
questions.explanation: A real image can be obtained on the screen. The real image is always inverted.

questions.question: Objects are visible when reflected light:
questions.explanation: We are able to see an object only when light from an object enters our eyes. The light entering our eyes may have been reflected by the object or may have been emitted by the object.

questions.question: When you stand in front of a small mirror. The image formed will appear to be of:
questions.explanation: When you stand in front of a small mirror. The image is of the same size as the object and the image is erect. Since the image cannot be obtained on screen, it is virtual.

questions.question: When we raise our left hand in front of the plane mirror it will appear to be right hand due to the phenomenon of:
questions.explanation: If you stand in front of a plane mirror and raise your right hand, you mirror image shows your left hand. This left-right reversal of an object and its image is termed as lateral inversion of light. The size of the image is equal to the size of the object.

questions.question: A burning candle is placed at a distance of 25 cm in front of a large plane mirror. The image of the candle will be:

options.option: 10 cm behind the mirror and virtual
50 cm behind the mirror and real

questions.explanation: A plane mirror forms a virtual image. The in-plane mirror image is formed at the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front of the mirror. So, A burning candle is placed at a distance of 25 cm in front of a large plane mirror. The image of the candle will be 25 cm behind the mirror and virtual.

questions.question: The image formed by a concave mirror may be:
questions.explanation: When the concave mirror is placed very close to the object, a magnified and virtual image is obtained. But if we increase the distance between the object and the mirror then the size of the image reduces and a real image is formed. So the image formed by a concave mirror can be small or large and it can also be real or virtual.

questions.question: The magnifying lens consists of:

options.option: A convex lens of short focal length
A convex lens of long focal length
A concave lens of short focal length

questions.explanation: A shorter focal length gives better magnification. A magnifying glass is a convex lens that is used to produce a magnified image of an object. So, the Magnifying lens is a convex lens of small focal length.

questions.question: The prism splits the sunlight into:
questions.explanation: When a white light beam is passed through a prism, a band of seven colors is formed is known as a spectrum of white light. The seven colors in the spectrum are red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet respectively.

questions.question: The mirror which always forms virtual, erect and the diminished image is:
questions.explanation: A convex mirror is also known as a diverging mirror as this mirror diverges light when they strike on its reflecting surface. A convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image, irrespective of the distance of the object from the mirror.

questions.question: The real image in the lens is always formed to:
questions.explanation: A real image is an image which is located in the plane of convergence for the light rays that originate from a given object. The real image is inverted. A real image is always on the opposite side of a converging lens from the object (when the converging lens creates a real image).

questions.question: Concave lens diverges the rays of light away from each other so it is called as:
questions.explanation: A concave lens bends the light rays outward or diverges the light, so it is called a diverging lens. Things look smaller when viewed through a concave lens.

questions.question: Which of the following instrument do not use lens?
questions.explanation: The lens is used in a microscope, telescope, and spectacles but only a mirror is used in periscope. Periscope is based on the principle of successive reflections from two plane mirrors.

questions.question: Which type of mirror will you use to form a virtual, erect, and magnified image of an object?
questions.explanation: A concave mirror is used to form a virtual, erect, and magnified image of an object. The nature of the image formed in a concave mirror depends on the portion of the object in front of the mirror. If the object is very close to the mirror the image is virtual, erect & magnified. As the distance increases image becomes real, inverted & its size keeps changing.

questions.question: The virtual image formed by a concave lens is always:

options.option: The erect and opposite side of the object
Erect and same side of the object

questions.explanation: Images formed by a concave lens are always virtual, erect, and smaller in size than the object. The position of the virtual image is always the other side of the object.

questions.question: In which of the following medium light travels fastest?
questions.explanation: The speed of light depends upon the medium through which it travels. The higher the index of refraction is, the slower the speed of light is. The indexes of refraction for a diamond, air, water, and glass are, respectively, 2.42, 1.00, 1.33, and approximately 1.50, depending upon the composition of the glass. Light travels slowest in diamond and fastest in air.

questions.question: The bright spot formed on the surface if a convex lens is a hold in sun is the:
questions.explanation: The light from the sun constitutes parallel rays of light. These parallel rays of light are converged by the lens at the sharp bright spot formed on the paper. The bright spot is the fact the real image of the sun.

questions.question: The image of the object placed at infinity is always formed at:
questions.explanation:
In a concave mirror when an object is placed at infinity, a real image is formed at the principal focus. The size of the image is much smaller as compared to that of the object.
In convex mirror when an object is placed at infinity, a virtual image is formed at the principal focus. The size of the image is much smaller as compared to that of the object.

questions.question: The image formed by a plane mirror can be obtained on screen because it is:
questions.explanation: A virtual image is that image which is formed when the light rays coming from an object do not actually meet, but appear to meet when produced backwards. These images cannot be obtained on the screen. The virtual image is always erect.

questions.question: Which of the following object can acts and both converging and diverging mirrors?
questions.explanation: The inward surface of the steel spoon acts as a concave mirror, while its outer surface acts as a convex mirror. A concave mirror is called a converging mirror and Convex mirrors are called diverging mirrors. So, steel spoon can act and both converging and diverging mirrors.

questions.question: An object of length 5 cm is placed at a distance of 20 cm in front of a plane mirror. The size and position will be:

options.option: 10 cm high and 10 cm behind the mirror
5 cm high and 20 cm behind the mirror
3 cm high and 40 cm behind the mirror
10 cm high and 20 cm in front of mirror

questions.explanation: The in-plane mirror image is of the same size as the object. And the distance between the object and mirror is equal to the distance between the mirror and the image formed behind the mirror. So, An object of length 5 cm is placed at a distance of 20 cm in front of a plane mirror. The size and position will be 5 cm high and 20 cm behind the mirror.

questions.question: When a Newton disc is rotated at good speed, the colours of the disc would blend and gives
questions.explanation: white colour

questions.question: When an object is placed in front of the mirror, the left part appears to be right side the mirror is likely to be:
questions.explanation: Plane mirror or convex mirror shows the phenomenon of lateral inversion to appear left part to the right side. So, When an object is placed in front of the mirror, the left part appears to be right side the mirror is likely to be a plane mirror or convex mirror.

questions.question: The splitting of white light into seven colours is called ________.
questions.explanation: Dispersion is the splitting up of white light into seven colors on passing through a transparent medium like a glass prism. When a white light beam is passed through a prism, a band of seven colors is formed is known as a spectrum of white light. The seven colors in the spectrum are red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet respectively.

questions.question: Which one is a transparent object?
questions.explanation: An object through which light can pass completely is called transparent object. We can clearly see through these objects. They can also be called see-through objects. So reading glass is a transparent object.

questions.question: ________ is a non-luminous body that reflects the light of Sun.
questions.explanation: Non-Luminous Objects are objects that reflect light from luminous bodies. Moon does not produce light of its own. Instead, they reflect the light from the sun falling on them. Hence the moon is a non-luminous body.

questions.question: The elementary particles that define light is called ________.
questions.explanation: A photon is an elementary particle. It is a quantum of light. It is the basic unit of all light.

questions.question: The reflection of light in a plane mirror or any other mirror takes place at:
questions.explanation: Silver metal is one of the best reflectors of light. A polished block of silver metal reflects almost all the light falling on it and does not transmit or absorb any of the light rays. But the smooth and polished surface of the silver metal can get easily scratched and may turn rough. In order to avoid this, ordinary mirrors are made by coating a thin layer of silver metal or mercury on one side of a plane glass sheet. The silver/mercury layer is protected by applying a coat of red paint over it. We use plane mirrors to see what we look like and adore ourselves. We need to study a set of laws to understand the reflection of light.

questions.question: Which of the following will not form a circular shadow?
questions.explanation: A shoebox is a rectangular structure that will never form a circular shadow. A circular disk, ring. the ball can give a circular shadow when light is incident from its top or bottom. A shoebox is a rectangular structure that will never form a circular shadow.

questions.question: An real image is formed when light coming from it meet after passing through a mirror or lens:

options.option: Moves away from the source of light
Meets the same side of the lens

questions.explanation: The incident ray, the reflected ray and the normal to the interface of two transparent media at a point incident, all lie in the same plane. A real image is formed when light coming from it meet after passing through a mirror or lens meets at a point.

questions.question: In refraction, bending of light rays takes place at the:
questions.explanation: The refraction occurs at the boundary of the path of a light wave as it passes from one material into another material crossing the boundary. The tendency of a ray of light to bend one direction or another is dependent upon whether the light wave speeds up or slows down upon crossing the boundary.

questions.question: Rainbow formation takes place due to:
questions.explanation: The rainbow is an arch of seven colors visible in the sky which is produced by the dispersion of the sun’s light by rainbow drops in the atmosphere. Dispersion of light occurs when white light is separated into its different constituent colors because of refraction and Snell's law. The formation of a rainbow is based on the process of dispersion of light and refraction.

questions.question: In spectrum, the rays of light that bends least appear:
questions.explanation: When a white light beam is passed through a prism, a band of seven colors is formed is known as a spectrum of white light. The seven colors in the spectrum are red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet respectively. Out of seven colours, the red colour deviates the least, and hence the red colour is present at the top of the spectrum.

questions.question: Danger signals are red coloured because:

options.option: It can be seen by colour blinds

questions.explanation: The primary reason why the color red is used for danger signals is that red light is scattered the least by air molecules. The effect of scattering is inversely related to the fourth power of the wavelength of a color. Therefore blue which has the least wavelength of all the visible radiations is scattered the most and red which has the highest wavelength of all the colors we can see is scattered the least. So red light is able to travel the longest distance through fog, rain, and the like.

questions.question: Bodies which give out their own light is called:
questions.explanation: Objects that give out or emit light of their own are known as luminous objects. For example candles, sun, etc. Objects that cannot give out or emit light of their own are known as non- luminous objects. Such as chair, book, etc.

questions.question: The blue colour of the sky during the day is due to:
questions.explanation: The blue colour of the sky is due to the scattering of light by small particles of the atmosphere (air molecules) when the light is incident on particles whose size is smaller than the wavelength of light, it is scattered. The scattered blue light reaching the eye gives the appearance of a blue sky.

questions.question: White light consists of:
questions.explanation: The cause of dispersion of light is that white light consists of seven different colours, and each colour has a different angle of deviation. Therefore, on passing through the prism different colours deviate through different angles. Hence the seven colours of white light separate and form a spectrum. Out of seven colours, the red colour deviates the least, and hence the red colour is present at the top of the spectrum. On the other hand, the violet colour deviates most that is why violet colour is present at the lower end of the spectrum.

questions.question: The image of a tree situated at a far distance is obtained using a concave lens. The image will be formed at:
questions.explanation: The rays of light coming from a distant object e.g. sun or a distant building can be considered to be parallel to each other. When these parallel rays of light fall on a concave mirror along its axis, reflect and meet at a point in front of the mirror, which is called as Principal focus of the mirror.

questions.question: We can see an object when it:

options.option: Refract or absorb the rays of light

questions.explanation: Luminous objects emit light. We see them by the light they emit. Non-luminous objects do not emit light. We see them by the light they reflect. So, We can see an object when it Reflects or emits rays of light.

questions.question: If you stand before a plane mirror, your left hand appears right. This phenomenon is:
questions.explanation: If you stand in front of a plane mirror and raise your right hand, your mirror image shows your left hand. This left-right reversal of an object and its image is termed as a lateral inversion of light. The size of the image is equal to the size of the object.

questions.question: Light rays enter the room during the day due to:
questions.explanation: Diffuse reflection originates from a combination of internal scattering of light, i.e. the light is absorbed and then re-emitted, and external scattering from the rough surface of the object. So, Light rays enter the room during the day due to diffuse reflection.

questions.question: When light bounces off a surface, the surface is ________.
questions.explanation: When rays of light bounce from a surface, the surface is called a reflector. Smooth, light colored, or shiny surfaces like glass, water, and metal are excellent reflectors.

questions.question: The word ambulance is written as below on patient carrying vehicle because Rear view mirror of ahead vehicle
questions.explanation: The word AMBULANCE written laterally inverted on the front of the ambulance cause if you are driving ahead of ambulance vehicle, then you can see ambulance written laterally to normal way in your side mirror and give the way to this emergency vehicle.

questions.question: In convex mirror, the reflection of light takes place at
questions.explanation: In convex mirror, the reflection of light takes place at bulging out the surface as convex mirror, has a reflective surface that curves outward. Convex mirror or diverging mirror is a curved mirror in which the reflective surface bulges toward the light source. Convex mirrors reflect light outwards, therefore they are not used to focus light. Such mirrors always form a virtual image. The image is smaller than the object but gets larger as the object approaches the mirror.

questions.question: Concave mirror is used in head light of vehicle as

options.option: It reflect the parallel beam of light
It forms the strong beam of light

questions.explanation: Concave mirror is used in headlight of vehicle as it reflect the parallel beam of light. Concave mirrors are commonly used in not only vehicles headlights but also in torches and search-lights to get powerful parallel beams of light to focus the light at the longer distances by placing the source of light at the focus of the concave mirrors.

questions.question: The mirror used as rear view mirror in vehicle is
questions.explanation: The mirror used as rear view mirror in vehicle is convex mirror as convex mirror forms virtual, erect and diminished image of object that increase view field.

questions.question: In which type of mirror image is always laterally inverted?
questions.explanation:
In plane mirror, the image is always laterally inverted in which the right side appears left-sided. A plane mirror is a mirror with a flat (planar) reflective surface. The image formed:-

  •  is virtual
  •  is erect and the same size as the object
  • The distance of the object from the plane mirror is same as the distance of the image from the plane mirror.

questions.question: Dentist use concave mirror because as it forms
questions.explanation: Dentist use a concave mirror because it forms magnified image of the teeth. Concave mirrors are used by dentists because at a short-range (object distances less than the focal length) they produce magnified, upright images. It is useful to have a magnified image of a tooth when you're looking for or repairing cavities, cracks, or other abnormalities.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used as concentrator in solar furnace?
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors are used in solar furnaces because concave mirrors are the only type of mirror that reflects light towards a single focal point. A flat mirror does not have a focal point. It does regular reflection off all of its points. A convex mirror does have a focal point, but it is an imaginary point that exists behind the mirrored surface.

questions.question: In plane mirror, image formed is
questions.explanation: In plane mirror, image is formed at same distance from the mirror as the distance of object from the mirror. The image distance always equals the object distance. The size of the image is the same as the object.

questions.question: Which of the following object can acts as both converging and diverging mirror?
questions.explanation: Steel spoon can acts as both converging and diverging mirror. The curved shining surface of a spoon acts as a mirror. The inward surface of the steel bowl or a spoon acts as a concave mirror, while its outer surface acts as a convex mirror.

questions.question: What is the name of phenomenon in which light change its direction after hitting the surface?
questions.explanation: During reflection of light, the light changes its direction after hitting the surface of the reflecting surface. Reflection is the abrupt change in the direction of propagation of a wave that strikes the boundary between two different media. At least some part of the incoming wave remains in the same medium.

questions.question: Light is a form of
questions.explanation: Light is a form of energy that enable us to see the different things present around us. When it strikes the earth's surface it converts to heat which is in the form of photons. These helps in transforming to electromagnetic energy

questions.question: Which of the following travels faster?
questions.explanation: Speed of light in air is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108m/s which is much faster than speed of sound (nearly 340m/s), jet airplane, and supersonic planes (765m/h).

questions.question: Which of the following object can acts as both converging and diverging mirror?
questions.explanation: Steel spoon can acts as both converging and diverging mirror. Its inner scoop side behaves as a concave mirror and outer bulged side as a convex mirror

questions.question: Which of the following physical quantity do not require medium for propagation?
questions.explanation: Light is the physical quantity that do not require medium for propagation. Sound require medium for propagation. Light can travels in vacuum also.

questions.question: Burning candle cannot be seen through a bended tube because
questions.explanation: Light travels in straight line. So, burning candle cannot be seen through a bended tube. This property of light is called rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: Mirror is a reflecting surface but glass is a
questions.explanation: Mirror is a reflecting surface but glass is a transparent object. Transparent object do not form image as reflection of light do not take place.

questions.question: Plane mirror always forms image
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms image behind the mirror as image is virtual, erect and of same size.

questions.question: Which of the following capital letter look the same in a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: In plane mirror lateral inversion takes place in which left side appear right and right side appear left. So, letter M appear same in plane mirror.

questions.question: From a source light travels as rays which are
questions.explanation: From a source light travels as rays which are parallel beam of light. Light always travel in straight line.

questions.question: On a sunny day, we are not able to see the shadow of Aeroplane flying in the sky because

options.option: Very large and faint image is formed

questions.explanation: On a sunny day, we are not able to see the shadow of an aeroplane flying in the sky because very large and fait image is formed on the ground.

questions.question: Light always travels in straight line. This property is called
questions.explanation: Light always travel in  a straight line. This property of light is called a rectilinear propagation of light. In a homogeneous transparent medium light travels in a straight line called rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: A toy is placed at 10 cm in front of a plane mirror. What is the distance of image from the mirror?
questions.explanation: The distance of image from plane mirror is 10 cm because the image formed by plane mirror has same distance, colour, size

questions.question: Dentist use concave mirror because it forms
questions.explanation: Dentist use concave mirror because concave mirror forms erect and magnified image of the object placed between pole and focus.

questions.question: Real image is always
questions.explanation: Real image is always inverted and can be obtained on screen. Real image is formed by actual meeting of rays.

questions.question: A person want to obtain virtual image of same size, he should use a
questions.explanation: Virtual image of same size is formed by plane mirror only. A person want to obtain virtual image of same size should use plan mirror.

questions.question: Diverging mirror is used as a rare view mirror in vehicle as it
questions.explanation: Convex mirror is called as diverging mirror which is used as rearview mirror in vehicle because it always form erect and diminished image and also cover wider area. As parallel beam of light diverges after reflection from a convex mirror and appears to meet at a point behind the mirror forming virtual, erect, and diminished image.

questions.question: Which type of mirror always form image behind the mirror?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror and convex mirror always form image behind the mirror which is virtual and erect. The image formed by plane mirror is same in size while that of convex mirror is smaller sized.

questions.question: In a plane mirror image formed is
questions.explanation: The image formed by a plane mirror is always virtual in nature. It is erect. It is of the same size as the object. It is formed at the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front of the mirror.

questions.question: A virtual image larger than the object can be produced by a
questions.explanation: A concave mirror forms virtual, erect and magnified image of the object provided that the object is between focus and pole of the mirror

questions.question: The path of the light is:
questions.explanation: Light travels in a straight line in a uniform transparent medium or in a vacuum. This mode of propagation of light is called the rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: David is observing his image in a plane mirror. The distance between the mirror and his image is 4 m. If he moves 1 m towards the mirror, then the distance between David and his image will be
questions.explanation:
In the case of a plane mirror, the distance between the object and the mirror (d1) is the same as the distance between the image and the mirror (d2)

Given, Distance between the mirror and David’s image, d2 = 4 m
Therefore, d1 = d2 = 4 m
If David moves 1 m towards the mirror, then d1 = 4 - 1 = 3m
Again, d1 = d2 = 3 m
Therefore, the distance between David and his image is d1 + d2 = 3 + 3 = 6 m

questions.question: Boojho and Paheli were given one mirror each by their teacher. Boojho found his image to be erect and of the same size whereas Paheli found her image erect and smaller in size. This means that the mirrors of Boojho and Paheli are, respectively
questions.explanation: Plane mirror forms erect and same size image while convex mirror forms erect and smaller size image.

questions.question: If an object is placed at a distance of 0.5 m in front of a plane mirror, the distance between the object and the image formed by the mirror will be
questions.explanation: Distance between object and mirror + distance between mirror and image = (0.5 + 0.5) m = 1 m

questions.question: Which of the following can be used to form a real image?
questions.explanation: The concave mirror can form a real image. Convex mirrors always form a virtual image.

questions.question: You are provided with a concave mirror, a convex mirror, a concave lens, and a convex lens. To obtain an enlarged image of an object you can use either
questions.explanation: When an object is kept between the focus and the pole of either a concave mirror or convex lens, the image formed is enlarge, virtual and erect.

questions.question: A rainbow can be seen in the sky

options.option: when the sun is in front of you.
only at the time of sun rise.

questions.explanation: Rainbow appears usually after the rain when the sun is low in the sky and you can see a rainbow only when your back is towards the sun.

questions.question: An erect and enlarged image can be formed by
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors produce real and virtual, erect and inverted, diminished, same-sized, and magnified images depending upon the position of the object on the principal axis. But the concave mirror forms a virtual, erect, and enlarged image only when the object is placed between the focus and the pole of the mirror.

questions.question: You are provided with a convex mirror, a concave mirror, a convex lens, and a concave lens. You can get an inverted image from
questions.explanation: both concave mirror and convex lens.

questions.question: An image formed by a lens is erect. Such an image could be formed by a

options.option: convex lens provided the image is smaller than an object.
concave lens provided the image is larger than object.
concave lens provided the image is of the same size.
concave lens provided the image is smaller than object.

questions.explanation: Image formed by a concave lens is always virtual, erect and smaller in size than the object.

questions.question: When on object moves towards a convex lens, the size of the image
questions.explanation: first decreases than increases

questions.question: The focal length of convex lens used as a magnifying glass is
questions.explanation: less

questions.question: Which one of the following materials cannot be used to make a lens?
questions.explanation: Clay

questions.question: In which of the following conditions the ray will not be deviated on passing through the lens?

options.option: The ray passes parallel to principal axis

questions.explanation: The ray passes through optical centre

questions.question: Which lens is used to rectify myopia?
questions.explanation: Shortsightedness is corrected using a concave (curved inwards) lens which is placed in front of a myopic eye, moving the image back to the retina and making it clearer.

questions.question: The speed of light in vacuum is-
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 300,000 kilometers (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108) per second in a vacuum, which has a refractive index of 1.0, but it slows down to 225,000 kilometers per second in water.

questions.question: From the given optical devices, choose that pair which can make real & magnified image with imaginary and magnified image of any object:
questions.explanation: That pair which can make real and magnified image with imaginary and magnified image of any object are concave mirror and convex lens.

questions.question: From the following which can convert an incident light from a point source into parallel beam?
questions.explanation: When light rays from a point source is incident on concave mirror and convex lens, emergent beams are parallel and the image is formed at infinity. Convex mirro * and concave lens on the other hand always form a virtual image. Emergent beams from these are not parallel but diverging when the object is kept at focus.

questions.question: Which mirror is used as a back mirror in motor vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in motor vehicles because they form virtual, erect and diminished images irrespective of distances of the object. Convex mirror helps the driver to view large areas of the traffic behind him and he can easily detect the vehicle coming or running behind him.

questions.question: If the object is 5 cm away from the plane mirror, the image formed by it and the object will be ________ cm apart.
questions.explanation:
Distance of object to plane mirror = 5 cm
Then, the distance of image to plane mirror = 5 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The distance of object to image = distance of object to plane mirror + distance of image to plane mirror
= (5 + 5) cm = 10 cm
So distance of object to image = 10 cm

questions.question: ________ is used in car headlights to reflect the light of the bulb.
questions.explanation: Concave mirror

questions.question: If an object is placed between focus and pole of the concave mirror then image formed will be:
questions.explanation: Magnified and erect

questions.question: A dentist uses ________ to see a magnified image of a teeth.
questions.explanation: Concave mirror

questions.question: A virtual, erect and magnified image is obtained by a concave mirror when object is placed:

options.option: At a distance between focal length and twice the focal length.
At a distance greater than twice the focal length.
At a distance less than its focal length.

questions.explanation: At a distance less than its focal length.

questions.question: Distance from focus to the centre of lens is:
questions.explanation: Focal length

questions.question: The given figure is showing:
questions.explanation: Converging of light rays.

questions.question: From which one of the following you will always get a virtual, erect and of the same size as object image?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror

questions.question: Images that can be obtained on screen are:
questions.explanation: Real images

questions.question: Which one of the following is the S.I. unit for potential difference?
questions.explanation: Volt

questions.question: A person is standing 2.5m from a plane mirror. How far is the image from the person?
A ray of light is incident on a plane mirror and angle of incidence is 80o. Calculate the angle between incident ray and the reflected ray.
questions.explanation: 160o

questions.question: Radius of curvature of a concave mirror is 8 cm. What is the focal length of the concave mirror?
questions.explanation: 4 cm

questions.question: The distance between focus and centre of a curved mirror is known as:
questions.explanation: Focal length

questions.question: A magnifying lens is a ________ lens.
questions.explanation: Converging

questions.question: Power of a lens is ________.
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac {1}{F}{/tex}

questions.question: Phenomenon of advance sunrise and delayed sunset is caused by:
questions.explanation: Refraction of light

questions.question: Look at the following figure and find the position of the image
questions.explanation: Between focus and centre of curvature

questions.question: Scientist who showed white light is made up of seven colours was:
questions.explanation: Isaac Newton

questions.question: When white light is passed into a prism it spilts into
questions.explanation: three colours

questions.question: Lens in a human eye is ________.
questions.explanation: biconvex

questions.question: Concave lens is also known as ________.
questions.explanation: diverging lens

questions.question: If angle of incidence of ray of light on a plane mirror is 30° then angle of reflection is ________.
questions.explanation: 45o

questions.question: Bulging outward surface of a spoon is:
questions.explanation: Convex

questions.question: Primary colours (as per modern colorimetry) consists of

questions.question: What is the least distance of distinct vision for a healthy man?
questions.explanation: For a normal person, the least distance of distinct vision, that is, the minimum distance for viewing an object comfortably and clearly is 25 cm.

questions.question: Man X is standing in front of a plane mirror while man Y is running towards him from behind. If the man Y is running at a speed of 2 ms-1, how many metres nearer does the man Y seem to be away from man X after 5 seconds?
questions.explanation: The image of running man in the mirror will appear to move at the same rate i.e. at 2 ms-1. So in 5 seconds, the image will be nearer by distance of 10 m.

questions.question: If a ray of light falls on a plane surface perpendicular to it, what will be the angle of reflection?
questions.explanation: If a ray of light falls on a plane surface in perpendicular direction, angle of incidence will be zero. So angle of reflection will be zero.

questions.question: An image can be formed by
questions.explanation: In lens, image is formed by refraction and in mirror, image is formed by reflection.

questions.question:
Statement I: Laws of reflection is obeyed in regular reflection.
Statement II: Laws of reflection is obeyed in irregular reflection.
Which of the following alternatives about the statements above is true?
questions.explanation: In both types of reflections, either regular or irregular, laws of reflection are obeyed.

questions.question: In periscope, we use mirrors inclined at
questions.explanation: In periscope, two reflecting plane mirrors both of which are inclined at 45° with the vertical are used. Each of the mirrors, produces a deviation of 90° in the direction of ray making net deviation equal to zero. The incident ray and emergent ray become parallel to each other.

questions.question: Power of accommodation of an eye is the

options.option: Neither Power to contract and dilate pupil nor Power to change the focal length of eye lens
Power to change the focal length of eye lens

questions.explanation: Power of accommodation is the ability of eye to change the focal length of eye lens, so that both near or far off objects can be seen comfortably.

questions.question: Vishan stands at A just on the side of a plane mirror and the objects P, Q and R are placed before the mirror as shown in figure. He can see the image of object
questions.explanation: We can see the images of objects situated at P and Q only because incident ray, reflected ray and the normal lie in the same plane. Incident ray from R will not reflected to reach point A, hence it will not be seen by Vishan.

questions.question: How does a lens work?

questions.question: What is persistence of vision?

options.option: Effect of vision remaining in action in brain for a short time
Both Viewing continuously something and Effect of vision remaining in action in brain for a short time.

questions.explanation: When we view an object, the impression of viewing lasts for some time in our brain. During that period we cannot view another object even if another object comes before our eyes. This is called persistence of vision.

  2. An incident ray that passes through the focal point of a converging lens will travel in parallel to the principal axis after emerging from converging lens.

The far point of a person having normal eye is
questions.explanation: Far point is the maximum distant point at which an object can be placed for clear viewing. It is at infinity for normal person.

Here, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 120o
So, number of image formed = {tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{\theta^{\circ}}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{120^{\circ}}{/tex} = 3

with the decrease in angle between two mirrors, the number of images formed increases.

questions.question: A light source O is placed in front of a plane mirror as shown in the figure. O' is a point on the path of reflected ray. What is the distance of the point on the mirror from where the light gets reflected (measured from point A)?

Let the point of reflection on the mirror be at a distance x from A.

{tex}\Delta{/tex}OAC and {tex}\Delta{/tex}O'BC are similar triangle so. {tex}\frac{O A}{A C}=\frac{O^{\prime} B}{B C} ; \frac{8}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{(10-x)}{/tex}
2x = 80 - 8x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 80 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 8 cm

For a plane mirror the distance between the image and mirror is same as between the object and mirror.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} For a distance of 5 m between Mahima and her image, she should move 2.5 m towards the mirror.

Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.question: Groundwater between two layers of rocks is called ________.
questions.explanation: The groundwater between two layers of rocks is called an aquifer. It bears permeable rocks. It is an underground layer of water-bearing permeable rock, rock fractures from which underground water can be extracted using a water well. The study of water flow in aquifers is called hydrogeology.

questions.question: World water day is celebrated on:
questions.explanation: World water day is celebrated on 22 March to enumerate the importance of drinking water. The day focuses attention on the importance of universal access to clean water, sanitation, and hygiene facilities in developing countries.

questions.question: There is an acute water shortage due to:
questions.explanation: There is an acute shortage of water throughout the world due to the overuse and misuse of water. As water is the necessity for everyone on earth and our earth constitutes near about 70% of water but that is not fit for drinking. Out of which drinking water is available in the form of glaciers, lakes, rivers. Some of the water is being contaminated with pollutants due to agriculture, industries, domestic waste etc.

questions.question: What percent of your body weight is water?
questions.explanation: About 70% of our body consists of water. It forms the main constituent of cell, tissue, and blood. The percentage of body water contained in various fluid compartments add up to total body water (TBW). This water makes up significant fraction of a human body both by weight and by a volume.

questions.question: Rainwater harvesting is a method of:

options.option: Collecting rainwater and directing into the ground

questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting is a method of collecting rainwater and directing it into the ground to maintain the groundwater level. It is a technique used for collecting, storing, and using rainwater for landscape irrigation and other uses. It is collected from various surfaces such as rooftops or man-made aboveground hard surfaces.

questions.question: Tubewells and Springs are examples of
questions.explanation: underground water

questions.question: There is an acute shortage of water due to:
questions.explanation: There is an acute shortage of drinking water in the world due to the growing population and overuse in other activities. Out of 70% hardly 3% of water is available for drinking and for other household activities and this too is available in the form of glaciers, lakes, rivers, etc. But maximum water is polluted due to the presence of unwanted polluted substances from agriculture, industries, domestic use making it unsafe for drinking.

questions.question: In irrigation, which technique reduces the water loss?
questions.explanation: Drip irrigation is a form of irrigation that saves water and fertilizer by allowing water to drip slowly to the roots of many different plants, either into the soil surface or directly into the root zone, through a network of valves, pipes, tubing, and emitters.

questions.question: Total water is maintained by:
questions.explanation: Total water is maintained by the water cycle during which evaporation and condensation of water take place. The water cycle is the process by which water circulates between the earth's oceans, atmosphere, and land involving precipitation as rain and snow, drainage in streams and rivers, and return to the atmosphere by evaporation and transpiration.

questions.question: In gaseous form, water is present in the atmosphere as:
questions.explanation: In the atmosphere, water is present in the form of water vapour along with other gases of the atmosphere. It is one of the states of water within the hydrosphere. It can be produced from the evaporation or boiling of liquid water is lighter than air and triggers convection currents that can lead to clouds.

questions.question: Earth appears blue from the sky due to presence of:
questions.explanation: Earth appears blue from the sky due to the presence of a large surface covered with water. About {tex}2\over 3{/tex} of earth surface is covered with water. Blue light is scattered in all the directions by the tiny molecules of air and water molecules present in the ocean due to its smaller wavelength  as water molecules absorb red light so when it is observed from the sky or space it appears blue.

questions.question: Amount of water recommended by the United Nation per person per day is:
questions.explanation: 40 litres of water is recommended by the United Nation per person per day to all day to day activities. As our body is made up of 70% of water content which is required by cells, tissues, and blood, etc. Water is also required for the removal of toxins from the body in the form of urine and perspiration.

questions.question: The process of changing water vapour into water is called ________.
questions.explanation: Condensation is the process by which water vapor in the air is changed into liquid water

questions.question: Which sector is measure user of water in India?
questions.explanation: In India, the agricultural sector is measure users of water as plants require water at a regular interval of time. Agriculture is the backbone of India and farmers use water for irrigation from the nearby canals, rivers, underground water from wells etc. As plants require water at regular interval so a major part of the water is consumed

questions.question: Water in converted into water vapour by the process of:
questions.explanation: Water is converted into water vapour by the process of transpiration and evaporation. Water evaporates as water vapor into the air in gaseous form. Ice and snow can sublimate directly into water vapour. Evapo transpiration is water transpired from plants and evaporated from the soil. The lowered temperature causes water vapour to condense into a tiny liquid water droplet which is heavier than the air and falls down again in liquid form.

questions.question: Bawris was the:
questions.explanation: Bawris was the Traditional way of collecting water. Bawris is one of the old methods of rainwater management. These are deep wells dug in the ground which have steps to reach the water stored. This bawris help in the storage of water for a longer time as the amount of evaporation of water from the deep well is very less.Bawris are very popular in Rajasthan

questions.question: Water-wise habits include:

options.option: Wash the flour one time in a day

questions.explanation: Water-wise habits include turning off taps while brushing. water is a renewable resource but drinking water is very less so we have to use it wisely. While brushing we must turn off the taps so that we can conserve it.

questions.question: Most liquids contracts on freezing but water
questions.explanation: Most liquids contracts on freezing but water expands on freezing and start floating on water surface. Water is one of the few exceptions to this behavior. When liquid water is cooled, it contracts like one would expect until a temperature of approximately 4 degrees . It is due to structure of water molecules

questions.question: Which if the following does not show water shortage?

options.option: Long ques protest for the demand for water
Marches and protest for the demand for water
A family gets three buckets of water per day

questions.explanation: A family gets three buckets of water per day. It is the right quantity of water required by a family. One should wisely use the water.

questions.question: In cities, water supply is through:
questions.explanation: In cities, water supply is through networks of pipes from water work to houses. A major part of the water supply network in cities is consists of interconnected pipes. This network creates a special class of problems in hydraulic design, with methods typically referred to as pipe network analysis. The aim is to determine the flow rate and pressure drops in the individual sections of the network.

questions.question: The water found below the water table is called ________.
questions.explanation: The water found below the water table is called groundwater. Groundwater is the water present beneath the earth's surface in soil pore spaces and in the fractures of rock formations. A unit of rock or an unconsolidated deposit is called an aquifer when it can yield a usable quantity of water. The depth at which soil pore spaces or fractures and voids in rock become completely saturated with water is called the water table

questions.question: Water in the aquifers can be usually pumped out with the help of:
questions.explanation: Water in aquifers can be usually pumped out with the help of tube wells and hand pumps. Aquifer follows a downward grade from its recharge zone, groundwater can become pressurized as it flows. This can create pressure in wells that flow freely and rise to a higher elevation which can be easily pumped out with the help of pumps

questions.question: Earth appears blue because:

options.option: One-half of the earth is covered by water
Two-third of the earth is covered by land
One-half of earth is covered by land
Two-thirds of the earth is covered by water

questions.explanation: Earth appears blue because {tex}2\over 3{/tex}rd of the earth is covered by water. That includes mainly the ocean and sea. The water bodies (ocean, lakes, river) appear blue because of the way sunlight is selectively scattered as it goes through our atmosphere. Taking Raman effect into account, Water absorbs more of the red light in sunlight. In this way, water also enhances the scattering of blue light in the surroundings.

questions.question: seas and oceans are full of water on earth .However, avery small percentage of water present on the earth is available for us. This percentage is roughly
questions.explanation: 0.006% of water is fit for drinking which is present at the poles in the form of glaciers and ice bergs whereas rest of water is in oceans and seas which contains dissolved metallic salts. And these salts are harmful for humans.

questions.question: Which two processes return the water to the atmosphere for the water cycle?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the loss of water in form of water vapour from leaves of the plants and evaporation is the changing of water into water vapour due to heating on its surface. Water evaporates as water vapor into the air. Ice and snow can sublimate directly into water vapour. Evapotranspiration is water transpired from plants and evaporated from the soil. The water cycle involves the exchange of energy, which leads to temperature changes. For instance, when water evaporates, it takes up energy from its surroundings and cools the environment

questions.question: We should not use ponds water for drinking without treating it because ponds water may be:
questions.explanation: We should not use ponds water for drinking without treating it because ponds water may contain pathogens or other harmful substances which can be the cause of various water-borne diseases. as ponds get water from the aquifers which are present in soil and rocks so can cause disease if not treated properly.

questions.question: which one of the following is not responsible for water shortage?
questions.explanation: heavy rainfall is the only factor which provides water to water bodies and only.  source which is responsible for the maintenance of water table on earth. And increase in  water table supplies the water for drinking as well as for other activities . So rainfall provides supply of water to it not decrease it. Whereas increase  in population and growth in industries are the factors for the consumption

questions.question: Water is called “Universal Solvent” because:

options.option: It dissolves all substance required by a living organism
It is present in cells of living organisms
It is used to dissolve sugar and salt

questions.explanation: Water is called a universal solvent because it dissolves all substances required by living organisms. Water is capable of dissolving a variety of different substances, which is why it is such a good solvent. And, water is called the "universal solvent" because it dissolves more substances than any other liquid.

questions.question: Which metabolic process of human beings also contributes to water cycling?
questions.explanation: Respiration is the metabolic process of human beings that also contributes in water cycling as water is released during respiration. there are many different ways that water and carbon dioxide are recycled by the water cycle. Our breath being absorbed by the atmosphere and dumped back down in the form of rain or snow. It allows organisms to gather oxygen from the air in exchange for carbon dioxide and water vapour

questions.question: Which of the following inhibits the seepage of rainwater into the ground?
questions.explanation: A pukka floor inhibits the seepage of rainwater into the ground. When floors don't have seepage tendency like soil have water from the rain flows into drains which cause wastage of it so it inhibits the seepage process.

questions.question: The purest form of water is ________.
questions.explanation: Rainwater is the purest form of water as it is free from all kinds of salts and other impurities. Most rainwater is safe to drink. Actually, rainwater is the water supply for much of the world's population. The levels of pollution, pollen, mold, and other contaminants are low - possibly lower than your public drinking water supply

questions.question: Excessive rain : flood :: less or no rain :?
questions.explanation: Excessive rain in an area may lead to floods while less rain or no rain may cause drought in an area. A drought is a period of below-average precipitation or rain in a given region, resulting in prolonged shortages in the water supply, whether atmospheric, surface water, or groundwater.

questions.question: The three forms of water present in-universe are:
questions.explanation: Water is present in all three forms in-universe which can be interchangeable by changing heat and pressure. Water strictly refers to the liquid state of that substance, that prevails at standard temperature and pressure; but it often refers also to its solid-state (ice) or its gaseous state (steam or water vapor). It also occurs in nature as snow, ice, fog, water vapours, clouds, aquifers which are interchangeable.

questions.question: River water is unfit for drinking because it has:
questions.explanation: River water is unfit for drinking because it has suspended impurities and pollutants that may cause disease. Rainwater can carry bacteria, parasites, viruses, and chemicals that could make us sick, and it has been linked to disease outbreaks. Dust, smoke, and soot from the air can be dissolved in rainwater before it lands on your roof. Roofing materials, gutters, piping, and storage materials can introduce harmful chemicals like asbestos, lead, and copper to the water, though building standards minimize some of this. Dirt and germs can be washed into collected rainwater

questions.question: What is the upper boundary of the zone of saturation called?
questions.explanation: The upper boundary of zone of saturation of earth crest is called a water table. The water table is the upper surface of the zone of saturation. The zone of saturation is where the pores and fractures of the ground are saturated with water. The water table is the surface where the water pressure head is equal to the atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about groundwater?

options.option: It is an important part of the water cycle
It is the main source of irrigation

questions.explanation: Groundwater is trapped inside the rocks that are not a part of the water cycle and no evaporation takes place from this water. Groundwater (or groundwater) is the water present beneath Earth's surface in soil pore spaces and in the fractures of rock formations. A unit of rock or an unconsolidated deposit is called an aquifer when it can yield a usable quantity of water

questions.question: Which one of the following is not responsible for the shortage of water?
questions.explanation: Increasing the population, increasing industries, and mismanagement of water leads to a shortage of water. Rainfall is the only source of water that can increase the amount of groundwater and water table. It helps in the storage of water in the water table due to seepage through the soil.

questions.question: Every Drop Counts is a slogan related to:

options.option: Counting of a drop of any liquid

questions.explanation: This slogan represents the importance of water. Water is a renewable resource but a very little quantity of water is fit for drinking. So to conserve water from pollution and from being get exhausted every one has to contribute to saving it.

questions.question: In which step of water cycle heat loss does not take place?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is a type of vaporization of a liquid that occurs from the surface of a liquid into a gaseous phase that is not saturated with the evaporating substance. The water cycle involves the exchange of energy, which leads to temperature changes. For instance, when water evaporates, it takes up energy from its surroundings and cools the environment

questions.question: Ultimate source of water is ________.
questions.explanation: The ultimate source of water is rain as the water cycle revolves around the rain pattern. Rainwater is a valuable natural resource that has been used to increase the water table. As rainwater percolates in the soil and increase the water table.

questions.question: Water shortage is a problem faced by
questions.explanation: Water shortage is a global problem faced by people living in all localities but it is more acute in urban areas than in rural areas. Obtaining sufficient drinking water with acceptable quality under circumstances of lack, such as droughts, is a challenge in drought-prone areas of India, suitability of water for drinking purposes. In addition to severe drinking water scarcity during drought, the quality of the drinking water was found to be a major problem

questions.question: The total water:

options.option: Of ponds and rivers of the world remain constant

questions.explanation: The total water of the world remains constant but it may be converted from one state to another state during water cycle. water is "in storage" for long periods of time than is actually moving through the water cycle. Water changes its form from liquid to gaseous, and then back to liquid or solid in the form of glaciers, ice,

questions.question: Water molecules consist of:

options.option: Two atoms of nitrogen and one atom of hydrogen
Two atoms of hydrogen and one atom of oxygen
Two atoms of oxygen and one atom of nitrogen
Two atoms of oxygen and one atom of hydrogen

questions.explanation: Water molecules consist of two atoms of hydrogen and one atom of oxygen. The chemical formula of water is H2O. Water is a tiny bent molecule with the molecular formula H2O, consisting of two light hydrogen atoms attached to each 16-fold heavier oxygen atom. Each molecule is electrically neutral but polar, with the center of positive and negative charges located in different places. Each hydrogen atom has a nucleus consisting of a single positively-charged proton surrounded by a 'cloud' of a single negatively-charged electron and the oxygen atom has a nucleus consisting of eight positively-charged protons and eight uncharged neutrons surrounded by a 'cloud' of eight negatively-charged electrons.

questions.question: Which type of floor does not allow water seepage into soil easily?
questions.explanation: Pukka floor does not allow water seepage into soil easily as it is hard and impermeable. Pukka floor reduces the water seepage as it drains the water into sewage drains as it doesn't have seepage capacity.

subtopics.name: Groundwater As An Important Source Of Water

questions.question: Ice floats on water surface because:
questions.explanation: Ice floats on the water surface because ice has less density than water. A substance floats if it is less dense, or has less mass per unit volume, than other components in a mixture. Ice is less dense than water because of its intermolecular forces.  Water contains hydrogen bonds between the H (hydrogen) of one atom and the O (oxygen) of another atom.  As the water gets colder and the kinetic energy of the molecules decreases, the hydrogen bonds keep the water molecules apart, in between creating hollow space, thus less density and so it floats

questions.question: Most liquids contracts on freezing but water:
questions.explanation: Most liquids contract on freezing but water expand on freezing and start floating on the water surface.  When liquid water is cooled, it contracts until a temperature of approximately 4 degrees Celsius is reached. After that, it expands slightly until it reaches the freezing point, and then when it freezes it expands by approximately 9%.

questions.question: Global warming causes:

options.option: Melting of ice on poles and mountain caps

questions.explanation: Global warming causes the melting of ice on poles and mountain caps that lead to flood in low land areas. Global warming referred to as climate changing. When temperatures rise and ice melts, more water flows to the seas from glaciers and ice caps, and ocean water warms and expands in volume. This combination of effects has played a major role in raising the average global sea level.

questions.question: Which two metal ions make the water hard?
questions.explanation: Calcium and magnesium ions present in water make water hard that prevent foam formation. Hardness is the amount of calcium and magnesium dissolved in the water. Hard water has no adverse health effects and may even taste better than soft waters. But hard water is less desirable because it requires more soap for effective cleansing, forms scum and curd, causes yellowing of fabrics, toughens vegetables during cooking, and forms scales in boilers, hot water heaters, pipes, and on cooking utensils.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for water shortage?
questions.explanation: The rapid growth of industries, increasing population, and mismanagement of water resources all are responsible for water shortage. Heavy rainfall is a natural resource of water so it’s not responsible for water shortage.

questions.question: Choose the correct option. The total water:

options.option: in the lakes and rivers of the world remains constant
in the seas and the oceans of the world remains constant

questions.explanation: The total water of the world remains constant because due to water cycle the total water in this planet will remain constant even though the quantity different water forms(sea, rivers...) may or may not be constant.

questions.question: On which day the world water day is celebrated?
questions.explanation: World Water Day is an annual event celebrated on 22nd March. The day focuses attention on the importance of universal access to clean water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) facilities in developing countries. The day also focuses on advocating for the sustainable management of freshwater resources.

questions.question: Which year was observed as International Year of Fresh Water?
questions.explanation: 2003 is the International Year of Freshwater. In recognition of the central importance of water resources to the planet's future, the United Nations General Assembly proclaimed the year 2003 as the International Year of Freshwater.

questions.question: How much surface of the earth is covered with water?
questions.explanation: Earth is known as the "Blue Planet" because 70 percent of the Earth's surface is covered with water.

questions.question: What portion of our body is water?
questions.explanation: The percentage of water in the human body is 75%. Water is very important for the proper functioning of the human body. Water helps in regulating the body temperature.

questions.question: Water is found in:
questions.explanation:
Water exists in three forms: Solid, Liquid, and Gas.
Solid - Snow, icebergs, glaciers, and hail are solid forms of water.
Liquid - Dew, rain, and water droplets are liquid forms of water.
Gaseous - Water vapour, steam, clouds, and fog are gaseous forms of water.

questions.question: A man digging the ground near a water body found that the soil was moist. As he kept digging deeper and deeper he reached a level where all the spaces between particles of soil and gaps between rocks were filled with water. The upper limit of this layer is called
questions.explanation: If we dig a deep hole in the ground, the soil below is moist. This shows the presence of water under the ground. If we go on digging deeper and deeper we will reach the level where all the spaces between the soil particles and the gaps between rocks are filled with water. The upper limit of this layer is known as the water table. This water table varies from place to place. At some places, it may be less than a metre deep while at other places it may be as deep as 100 metres or more.

questions.question: Which of the following is a way to use water economically?
questions.explanation: In drip irrigation, water is allowed to fall drop by drop from a pipe, just near the roots of plants. So, this minimises wastage of water.

questions.question: The amount of water recommended by the United Nations for drinking, washing, cooking and maintaining proper hygiene per person per day is a minimum of
questions.explanation: The United Nations has recommended that a minimum of 50 litres of water should be available to each of us per person per day for drinking, washing, cooking, and cleaning. This amount is about two and a half buckets of water per person per day. Therefore, a family getting three buckets of water per person per day does not show water shortage.

questions.question: Water cycle does not involve which of the following?
questions.explanation: Water constantly moves from the earth to the atmosphere and comes back to the earth again. This constant circulation of water in nature is known as the water cycle. The water cycle does not involve rainwater harvesting. In the water cycle water in seas, rivers, lakes, ponds, or streams evaporates and water vapor rises up (evaporation). The air higher up in the atmosphere is cooler. This cools the water vapor and it condenses to form tiny drops of water (condensation). These drops of water unite together and form clouds (cloud formation). As the clouds get cooled further, more water drops unite together and become bigger. When they become too heavy. They fall on the earth as rain (precipitation).

questions.explanation: About 71%

can never be replaced once they get used up

options.option: Cutting vegetation so that less water is lost by transpiration.

questions.explanation: Cutting vegetation so that less water is lost by transpiration.

questions.explanation: all of these

we use groundwater slowly than it is replenished
we plant too many trees causing loss of water by transpiration

questions.explanation: water table
